description: Riverview Farmers Market is a seasonal Sunday market in the Heights neighborhood of Jersey City. We are a thriving, community-supported, producer-first farmers market, offering a wide range of market vendors and goods which serves all community members, and which especially supports small, local family farmers. We are also a gathering place where the diverse population of Jersey City Heights can get nourishment for their minds, as well as their bodies, through art, music and kids programming. Our market runs every Sunday, May through November, 10 AM to 2PM. We are located in Riverview Fisk Park.

description: Kenosha Public Market is a year-round Saturday market located in the heart of the Downtown district. Our vendors present a variety of products including local produce, artisan pastries and confections, locally sourced meats, poultry and eggs, fine cheeses, prepared foods & handmade gifts. Order from our online store, KPM2GO, to ensure you get the products you want before they sell out. Place orders during the week and pick up at our Saturday market! Our location is in the center of this pedestrian-friendly neighborhood with convenient parking options, world-class museums, an authentic streetcar system, two lighthouses, unique dining, and plenty of shopping options. Kenosha Public Market is proud to be one of the many shopping destinations Downtown.

description: Chapel Hill Farmers’ Market was started in April 2008 by Farmers of Orange, a state non-profit organization created for local farmers by local farmers. All of our farmers and artisans live within 60 miles of Chapel Hill, NC and bring fresh quality produce, value-added farm products, and crafts to market year-round. The market is vendor run and operated by our market manager. Every dollar spent goes directly to our farmers and vendors. All items are grown, fished, or created by our farmers and artisans.

description: The GSFM is unique collaboration between local organizations with a shared goal of creating conditions under which people can be healthy. It is one of few markets in downtown Louisville and is dedicated to increasing availability of fresh foods to an underserved area. The market offers customers a great variety of local vendors and products. We are open the first Thursday of each month from 10 a.m. to 2 p.m. from June through October. HISTORY: In 2009, the University of Louisville School of Public Health and Information Sciences (SPHIS) led efforts to establish the Gray Street Farmers Market (GSFM) in partnership with the Louisville Metro Dept. of Public Health and Wellness (LMPHW). The GSFM operated weekly (rain or shine) from mid-May through the end of October for 11 seasons. We did not open in 2019 or 2020 due to COVID-19 pandemic and resource limitations .

description: With a focus on Michigan grown and Michigan made foods, the Battle Creek Farmers Market is a great place to find fresh and flavor packed local foods from Michigan businesses. Non-food businesses are also welcome. The Market is held in Festival Market Square in downtown Battle Creek on Wednesday, 9-1, beginning in May and ending in October. Customers will find a family friendly market with a good variety of variety of vendors. Vendors will find a well managed and vendor friendly market. An open air marketplace under a pavilion offers picnic tables, restrooms onsite and free 1-hour parking for customers in the Hamblin Avenue ramp. Live music and other activities make summer fun at the farmers market. BCFM participates in Bridge Card (SNAP), Double Up Food Bucks, Senior Market FRESH, WIC Project FRESH, NHBP Elders Nutrition Program. Payment with cash, Credit and Debit cards as well a market issued scrip accepted. Contact us for more information. Visit us on Facebook and Instagram, too!

description: Brookside Farmers’ Market is an sustainable, regenerative, pesticide-free local, producer-only market. Each Saturday you will find just-picked, farm-fresh vegetables and herbs, bright flowers, delicious meats, eggs from free-range chickens, freshly prepared breads and baked goods including many vegan and gluten-free items, fresh juices, and a place to sit and take in the scene. The market has brought farm fresh produce to the Brookside neighborhood and beyond since 2003. Come shop with us July through October, Tuesdays 2-5.30, and Saturdays 9-Noon.

name: The Renville County Market in Bird Island
description: The Renville County Market in Bird Island is a weekly community event on the Cultural Centre's green space, combining a farmers' market with food vendors, live music, craftspeople, and art, plus free children's art classes.

name: The Renville County Markent in Bird Island - Winter
Walnut Grove Farmers' Market
products: Fresh fruits; Fresh vegetables; Baked goods; Canned or preserved fruits/vegetables; Cut flowers; Eggs; Fresh/dried herbs; Honey; Maple products
description: Locally grown produce from A to Zucchini, along with standard and specialty jams, jellies, and marmalade's; all kinds of pickled goodies, an array of salsa's from mild to hot; baked goods, cut flowers, and so much more! Come shop with us June through October, Fridays, 3-6pm.

name: Worthington Farmers' Market
season: Tuesdays 3 PM to 6 PM and Saturdays 7 AM to noon, May through October
description: The Worthington Farmers Market runs Tuesdays and Saturdays in Worthington with vendors offering fresh produce, baked goods, and locally made items.

name: Albert Lea Farmers' Market
season: Wednesdays 3:30 PM to 5:30 PM and Saturdays 9 AM to 12 PM, May through October
description: The Albert Lea Farmers Market sets up at the North Broadway Parking Lot and offers produce, baked goods, canned items, local meat, flowers, handmade crafts, coffee, and maple syrup alongside live entertainment.

name: Cedar River Farmers' Market - Winter
description: Cedar River Farmers Market is a community-based marketplace in Austin, Minnesota, established in 2021, that prioritizes sustainable agriculture and supporting local businesses, operating Friday evenings rain or shine.

name: Chatfield Growers Market
description: The Chatfield Growers Market offers produce, baked goods, canned goods and handmade items, held on Fourth Street between the Chatfield Center for the Arts and the city park.

name: Farmers' Market Place LLC (Austin)
season: Mondays and Thursdays, 4 PM to 6 PM, mid-May through October
description: Farmers' Market Place LLC is held at 3rd Avenue and Main Street NE in Austin, offering locally sourced fresh produce, baked goods, meats, and artisanal items from area vendors.

name: La Crescent Farmers' Market
description: The La Crescent Farmers Market is held Tuesday evenings in the parking lot adjacent to Veteran's Park and the La Crescent Event Center, operating under a 'Buy Local. Buy Fresh.' focus on locally grown and produced goods.

name: La Crescent Farmers' Market - Winter
season: November-April, one day per month (winter market)
description: The La Crescent Farmers Market runs a winter season in addition to its summer market, operating one day per month from November through April for locally grown and produced goods.

name: Aicho’s Indigenous Food and Art Market
description: AICHO's Indigenous Food and Art Market showcases local Indigenous, Black, and POC artists and food producers, with garden-grown produce, frozen meats, smoked whitefish, wild rice, fermented foods, Indigenous teas, maple syrup, jams, honey, plus prints, apparel, beadwork, and jewelry. It is held in the One Roof parking lot at 12 E. 4th St. in Duluth and accepts SNAP/EBT.

name: Annandale Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, 8 AM to 12 PM, Memorial Day weekend through mid-September
description: The Annandale Farmers Market is held at City Hall Square in the Heart of the Lakes region, offering fresh fruits and vegetables, bakery items, grass-fed beef, chicken, farm eggs, maple syrup, honey, and flower bouquets.

name: Burnsville Farmers' Market - Mother Mary Church
Carver Farmers' Market
season: Tuesdays, June through September, 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM
description: The Carver Farmers' Market (Carver Community Market) gathers local growers and makers at 1607 Hartwell Drive in Carver, offering fresh produce, meats, eggs, cheeses, artisan breads, honey, maple syrup, plants, and crafts.

name: Centerville Main St Market
description: The Centerville Main Street Market is a community market held on select summer Thursday evenings on North Centerville Road near the Centerville Elementary School parking lot, featuring local artisans, fresh produce from neighborhood farmers, live music, and food vendors, with free parking on site.

name: Cgucc Farmers' Market
description: The CGUCC Farmers' Market is hosted by Cottage Grove United Church of Christ and features fresh produce and fruit from local vegetable farmers, baked goods, plants and flowers, artisan products such as honey, maple syrup, chocolate and soaps, plus food trucks and handcrafted items.

name: Champlin Farmers' Market
season: Tuesdays, June through September, 2:00 PM - 6:00 PM
description: The Champlin Farmers Market is held at Andrews Park (7200 117th Ave. N.) in Champlin and is organized by the City of Champlin as a weekly summer-season community market.

name: Chanhassen Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, 9 AM to 1 PM, June through late September
description: The Chanhassen Farmers' Market brings fresh, healthy local produce and handmade items to City Center Park, featuring more than 50 vendors offering homemade products and produce throughout the season.

name: Chaska Downtown Farmers' Market / SouthWest Metro Chamber of Commerce
Cimarron Market Stand (Cimarron Farm)
description: Cimarron Community Farm in Lake Elmo hosts a free, onsite farmers market open to Cimarron Park residents and the surrounding community, where visitors can pick out fresh organic vegetables at no cost (donations welcome but not required).

name: City of Oakdale Farmers' Market - Winter
City of White Bear Lake Farmers' Market
season: Mid-June to end of July, Thursdays 6:00-9:00pm (Marketfest)
description: The White Bear Lake Farmers' Market operates downtown as part of the city's weekly Marketfest, a free Thursday-evening event featuring a farmers' market alongside more than 200 vendors offering arts, crafts, food, children's activities, pony rides, and a classic car show.

name: Crystal Farmers' Market
description: The Crystal Farmers' Market is held at the Crystal Community Center, offering fresh produce, baked goods, handmade crafts, and prepared foods from local farms, bakeries, food trucks, and artisans, with live music and themed market days throughout the season.

name: Eagan Market Fest
season: Summer: June 3-Aug 26, Wednesdays 4-8pm; Fall: Sept 2-Oct 7, Wednesdays 4-7pm
description: Eagan Market Fest is the City of Eagan's weekly market at the Eagan Festival Grounds and Goat Hill Park, offering fresh local produce, artisan goods, and food trucks alongside a weekly summer concert series with local performers.

name: Eden Prairie Indoor Farmers' Market - Eden Prairie
Eden Prairie Indoor Farmers' Market - Plymoth
Farmington Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, May through November, 9:00 AM - 2:00 PM
description: The Farmington Farmers Market is an award-winning, community-focused market and a trusted source of healthy, local, and sustainable produce, featuring artisan vendors and live music in downtown Farmington.

name: Forest Lake Farmer's Market
description: The Forest Lake Farmers' Market is a Saturday-morning market run by the City of Forest Lake Parks Department and held at Forest Lake City Hall (95 East Broadway Avenue). Vendors offer fresh local produce and goods to the community. The 2026 season runs Saturday mornings from June 21 through late October, weather pending.

name: Fridley Farmers' Market Association
description: The Fridley Farmers' Market Association runs a community market at 6085 7th St NE in Fridley, where local vendors sell produce, raw honey, jams, and baked goods.

name: Fulton Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, 8:30 AM to 1 PM, mid-May through October
description: Operated by the nonprofit Neighborhood Roots in southwest Minneapolis, this Saturday market features diverse vendors offering fresh, local agricultural products and artisan goods.

name: Fulton Farmers' Market - Winter
season: Saturdays, November through March, 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM
description: The Neighborhood Roots Winter Market is an indoor farmers market held at Bachman's on Lyndale Avenue in Minneapolis, featuring vendors from the Kingfield, Fulton, and Nokomis farmers markets on select Saturdays through the cold months.

name: Hmong Village Farmers' Market
season: Monday through Saturday 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sunday 9:00 AM - 1:00 PM (year-round)
description: Hmong Village is a one-of-a-kind indoor market on the east side of St. Paul with more than 250 vendors, including a farmers market section, where visitors experience the foods, cultures, goods, and services of the Hmong and other Southeast Asian communities.

name: Hopkins Farmers' Market - Winter
description: The Hopkins Winter Farmers Market is the cold-season edition of the Hopkins Farmers Market, held indoors at the Hopkins Activity Center, 33 14th Avenue North, Hopkins.

name: House of Hope Farmers' Market
season: Fridays, May through October, 1:15 PM - 5:00 PM
description: The House of Hope Farmers' Market is a neighborhood location of the St. Paul Farmers' Market at the House of Hope Presbyterian Church (797 Summit Ave.), offering locally grown vegetables, fruits, cut flowers, plants, and artisan foods.

name: Howard Lake Area Farmers' Market - Winter
Inver Grove Heights Farmers' Market
description: The Inver Grove Heights Farmers' Market is a neighborhood market operated by the St. Paul Farmers' Market, held in the Veterans Memorial Community Center parking lot at 8055 Barbara Ave. Vendors sell locally grown vegetables, fruits, cut flowers, plants, and artisan food goods, with EBT, cash, card, and market bucks accepted. The market runs Sunday mornings during the growing season, June through October.

name: Kingfield Farmers' Market
season: Sundays, 8:30 AM to 1 PM, mid-May through late October
description: What began as a pickup truck full of vegetables parked behind Anodyne Coffeehouse has grown into the bustling Kingfield Farmers Market, with around 40 vendors every week offering locally sourced produce, prepared foods, handmade crafts, and artisan goods.

name: Lake Elmo Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, June through August, 9:00 AM - 1:00 PM
description: The Lake Elmo Farmers Market is held at Lake Elmo Elementary (11030 Stillwater Blvd N) and features local vendors offering produce, meat and eggs, flowers, artisan breads and pastries, body care, mushrooms, maple syrup, and honey, with a youth vendor spotlight.

name: Lakeville Area Arts Center Farmers' Market
Lexington Fire & Rescue Auxiliary Farmers' Market
Linden Hills Farmers' Market
description: The Linden Hills Farmers' Market is a community market at Settergrens of Linden Hills (2813 W 43rd St) in southwest Minneapolis, featuring 45+ local growers, makers, and musicians. Shoppers find fresh produce, artisanal food and crafts, and hot food stalls, with a vendor lineup that changes weekly. It runs every Sunday from May through October and was voted a Star Tribune readers' best Minnesota farmers market (Gold 2023, Bronze 2024).

name: Mahtomedi Area Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, June through September, 8:00 AM - 12:00 PM
description: The Mahtomedi Area Farmers' Market is held at Veterans Memorial Park (Triangle Park) at the intersection of Mahtomedi Ave. and Stillwater Rd., featuring farm-grown vegetables, fresh cut flowers, baked goods, eggs, berries, plants, meat, and hand-crafted items.

name: Minneapolis Farmers' Market - Lyndale Market
season: Wednesday through Friday 7 AM to 1 PM and Saturday through Sunday 6 AM to 1 PM, May through October
description: The Minneapolis Farmers Market's flagship Lyndale Market, marked by three signature red sheds, is the largest farmer/producer-managed market in the state, connecting local farmers and gardeners with Twin Cities residents over a shared love of food.

name: Minnetonka Farmers' Market
description: The Minnetonka Farmers' Market is a weekly market hosted by the City of Minnetonka, held Tuesday afternoons at The Marsh parking lot (15000 Minnetonka Blvd). More than 30 rotating local vendors offer fresh produce, eggs, meats, cheeses, baked goods, honey, maple syrup, fresh-cut flowers, and handcrafted goods. The 2026 season runs from early June through late September.

name: Monticello Farmers' Market
season: Summer season (recurring market dates listed June–July)
description: The Monticello Farmers' Market is a community market associated with the Monticello Community Center, where shoppers can stock up on fresh, local food and other products while supporting small farmers and businesses. It is listed as a recurring summer event on the community center's calendar.

name: New Brighton Farmers' Market
season: Wednesdays, June through September, 3:00 PM - 7:00 PM
description: The New Brighton Farmers Market is held at the New Brighton Community Center (400 10th Street NW), featuring local vendors selling produce, flowers, eggs, bread, honey, and crafts, along with food trucks and live music most weeks.

name: New Life Ramsey Farmers' Market
description: The New Life Ramsey Farmers Market is an outdoor market held at the corner of 167th Avenue and Highway 47 (St. Francis Blvd NW) in front of Fairway Liquors, across from Rum River Hills Golf Club in Ramsey.

name: Newport Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, March through December, 9:00 AM - 1:00 PM
description: The Newport Farmers Market, located at the corner of Angle and Highway 101, hosts over 60 vendors offering seasonal produce, art, fresh flowers, nursery plants, hot food, pastries, honey, salsas, coffee, crafts, and live music.

name: Nokomis Makers Market
season: Thursdays, June through September, 4:30 PM - 7:30 PM
description: The Nokomis Makers Market is a vibrant hub for craft makers, food stands, and arts organizations held at the Shoreview Triangle in south Minneapolis near Lake Nokomis (5342 25th Ave. S.).

name: Nokomis Makers Market - Winter
description: The Nokomis Makers Market hosts special indoor winter markets in November and December, extending its lineup of craft makers, food stands, and arts organizations into the holiday season in south Minneapolis.

name: Nowthen Farmers' Market
description: The Nowthen Farmers' Market is held in downtown Nowthen at the Bills Superette lot, on the corner of Viking Blvd and Nowthen Blvd. Vendors offer locally grown vegetables and fruit, honey, beef, canned goods, jams and jellies, artisan breads and baked goods, soaps, jewelry, textiles, and natural dog treats. The market runs Thursday afternoons from June through September.

name: Osseo Farmers' Market
description: The Osseo Farmers' Market is held on the north side of Boerboom Veterans Park (416 Central Ave), along 5th St NE between Central Avenue and 1st Ave NE. The City of Osseo market operates Tuesday afternoons during the summer season, welcoming local vendors.

name: Otsego Farmers' Market
description: The Otsego Farmers' Market is a seasonal market in the City of Otsego offering fresh produce from local vendors, sponsored by Coborn's. It includes a Senior and Kids Power of Produce (POP) program providing qualifying children and seniors with complimentary tokens to spend on fresh produce. The market runs Tuesday afternoons through late August.

name: OWC MN Tennant Union Farmers' Market
Plant Based Pop-Up Market
description: The Plant Based Pop-Up Market, launched in 2020 to increase access to nourishing food in underserved communities, brings together some of the Twin Cities' best plant-based makers and bakers at New Rules North (2015 Lowry Ave. North) in Minneapolis.

name: Red Wing Area Farmers' Market Winter
season: Winter: online order drive-thru/delivery (pickup at LaGrange Parking Ramp 10am–11am); summer market May–October
description: The Red Wing Area Farmers' Market is a producer market selling local produce, with programs including Veggie Rx, Power of Produce, and SNAP-EBT. The main season runs May through October on Levee Road in front of the train depot; during the winter months the market offers an online order drive-thru and home delivery, with pickup at the LaGrange Parking Ramp. The 2026 winter market schedule was listed as TBD.

name: Rushford Peterson Valley Farmers' Market
description: The Rushford Peterson Valley Farmers' Market is a seasonal market held at St. Joseph's Church (103 N. Mill St.) in Rushford, featuring local vendors selling produce, products of the farm, and craft items. It runs Tuesday afternoons from May through October, with the 2026 season scheduled to open May 19.

name: Simple Living Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, April through mid-October, 8:30 AM - 11:30 AM
description: The Simple Living Farmers' Market sits a quarter mile west of Mabel on Highway 44 and offers locally grown (much chemical-free) produce, fresh eggs, baked goods, homemade candy, crafts, and vegetable and bedding plants, weather permitting.

name: Spring Grove Farmers' Market
season: Third Saturday of the month, May through October, 9:00 AM - 1:00 PM
description: The Spring Grove Farmers Market is held in Main Street Park (8013 Blivin Street) in Spring Grove on the third Saturday of each month through the growing season.

name: St. Peter Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, June through October, 8:30 AM - 12:00 PM
description: The St. Peter Farmers Market, at the corner of Hwy 169 and Mulberry Street, offers produce, crafts, canned goods, and baked treats from vendors based within 40 miles of St. Peter.

name: Wells Area Farmers' Market
description: The Wells Area Farmers' Market is a community market in Wells, Minnesota offering fresh, locally grown produce and artisanal goods from local vendors. It operates twice weekly during the summer and fall season, on Wednesday afternoons and Saturday mornings.

name: Winona Farmers' Market
description: The Winona Farmers' Market is a local producer market at Levee Park where growers sell homegrown fruits, vegetables, herbs, eggs, honey, syrups, dairy, baked goods, meats, and flowers and plants. It accepts SNAP/EBT with Minnesota Market Bucks matching and runs a Power of Produce Club for children and seniors. The summer market runs Saturday mornings from May 4 through October 31.

name: Winona Farmers' Market - Winter
description: The Winona Farmers' Market winter market is held at the Morrison Annex Event Center, offering local produce, eggs, honey, baked goods, and other producer goods through the colder months. It runs on alternating Saturdays from November through April, with SNAP/EBT and Minnesota Market Bucks accepted.

name: New Hope Community Farmers Market
description: The New Hope Community Farmers Market is a City of New Hope market held in the New Hope Church parking lot (4225 Gettysburg Ave N), featuring vendor-grown and vendor-produced goods, food trucks, and entertainment, with the mission of providing fresh and healthy foods while supporting local farmers. Vendors may vary weekly and the market operates rain or shine. It runs Thursday afternoons from June through late September.

name: Rosemount Farmers' Market
season: Mid-June through late September, Tuesdays 1–5pm and Saturdays
description: The Rosemount Farmers' Market is a satellite location of the Saint Paul Growers' Association, set up in the Rosemount Community Center parking lot at 13885 S Robert Trail. Vendors offer locally grown vegetables, fruits, cut flowers, plants, and artisan food goods. EBT, cash, card, and market bucks/coins are accepted.

name: Savage Farmers' Market
description: The Savage Farmers' Market is an open-air market on 123rd Street near the Train Depot in Savage, offering 100% locally grown vegetables, fruits, cut flowers, plants, and artisan food goods.

name: Securian Building Farmers' Market
description: The St. Paul Farmers' Market at Securian Financial offers locally grown vegetables, fruits, cut flowers, plants, and artisan food goods just outside the Securian building at 400 Robert St N in downtown St. Paul. It is part of the Saint Paul Farmers' Market, providing community and local food since 1854, and accepts EBT, cash, card, and market bucks.

name: Signal Hill Farmers' Market
description: The Signal Hill Farmers' Market is held at Signal Hills Mall (1225 South Robert Street) in West St. Paul and is operated by the Saint Paul Farmers' Market, offering locally grown vegetables, fruits, cut flowers, plants, and artisan food goods.

name: St. Luke's Farmers' Market
description: St. Luke's Farmers' Market is a neighborhood market hosted at St. Luke Lutheran Church, 1807 Field Ave in St. Paul's Highland Park area. Now in its second decade, the market includes local vendors and a bread table, and accepts card payments via a point-of-sale device.

name: St. Paul Farmers' Market
description: The Saint Paul Farmers' Market is the flagship downtown market of the Saint Paul Growers' Association, operating in Lowertown at 290 5th St E since 1854. Vendors sell locally grown and made dairy, produce, meats, cheeses, breads, baked goods, coffee, flowers, mushrooms, honey, sauces, chocolate, and hot food. The association runs over 20 markets across the Twin Cities metro.

name: City of Elk River Farmers' Market
description: The City of Elk River Farmers Market is a weekly downtown market normally held in the King Avenue parking lot. Vendors sell hand-crafted items, homemade and baked goods, farm-fresh produce, meats, cheeses, eggs, honey, and maple syrup. Cash, credit (via tokens), and EBT with a matching program are accepted, and the market runs kids' Power of Produce and senior token programs.

name: Onamia Area Farmers' Market
description: The Onamia Area Farmers Market supports the health of the local region with fresh, sustainably grown products including locally grown wild rice, maple syrup, honey, and a variety of seasonal vegetables and fruits. It also offers home-baked breads, pastries, and preserves plus hand-crafted items, held at the Trailside Center next to the Soo Line Trail and Farm Market Cafe.

name: Princeton - Zimmerman Farmers' Market - Winter
season: November–April, second Saturday of the month 9am–noon
description: The Princeton-Zimmerman Farmers' Market Association runs an indoor winter market inside the Princeton Mall at 101 S Rum River Dr. As with its summer market, every product sold by members must be grown or produced on the member's own farm.

name: Lake City Market by the Lake
Le Sueur Family Night at the Market
description: Le Sueur Family Night at the Market is a weekly evening market at Legion Park (155 S Park Ln) featuring homemade baked goods, handmade crafts, honey, maple syrup, jams, soaps, and direct-sales goods, with vendor offerings varying weekly. Food vendors are on hand so attendees don't have to cook, making it a family-friendly community evening out.

name: Madison Lake Farmers' Market
description: The Madison Lake Farmers Market is a community market on Main Street in Madison Lake, launched in 2015 with grant funding. It emphasizes local agriculture and small-farm commerce, connecting residents with nearby food producers, and welcomes vendors on a flexible per-weekend basis.

name: Mankato Farmers' Market
season: May–October at 1895 Adams St (Tue & Thu 3–5:30pm, Sat 8am–noon); winter Nov–March 1st & 3rd Saturdays 9am–noon
description: The Mankato Farmers' Market gathers local growers and producers at 1895 Adams Street, selling seasonal produce, handmade items, and other goods from area producers. A separate winter market runs indoors nearby at 1940 Adams Street.

name: Mantorville Minnesota Farmers' Market
description: The Mantorville Farmers & Artisans Market is a seasonal market held at Riverside Park in Mantorville, bringing together local growers and artisans selling fresh produce and handmade goods. It is listed in the Minnesota Grown Directory.

name: North Mankato Farmers' Market
description: The North Mankato Farmers Market is held next to Caswell Park at 1825 W. Howard Drive, behind Fire Station #2. Now in its tenth year, vendors sell fresh locally grown fruits and vegetables, baked and canned goods, and handmade products. SNAP and Senior/Farmers' Market Nutrition Program benefits are accepted; dogs are not permitted.

name: Pine Island Farmers' Market
description: The Pine Island Farmers Market and Crafts is a weekly seasonal market held in the grass lot behind Island Market, across from the Cheese Factory building. Vendors offer fresh produce, canned goods, and artisan crafts. The market runs rain or shine, with cancellations only for severe weather.

name: Red Wing Area Farmers' Market
season: Late May through October, Saturdays 8am–1pm; weekday market Sunday–Friday 8am until sold out
description: The Red Wing Area Farmers Market sets up along the levee in front of the train depot in downtown Red Wing. It offers a managed Saturday market plus an unmanaged weekday market where vendors set up as product is available. SNAP-EBT and a Veggie Rx program are accepted, and online pre-ordering with drive-thru pickup is available.

name: Scituate Rotary Farmers Market
products: Fresh fruits; Fresh vegetables; Baked goods; Bedding plants; Canned or preserved fruits/vegetables; Coffee/tea; Crafts/woodworking items; Cut flowers; Eggs; Fish/seafood; Fresh/dried herbs; Honey; Pet food; Poultry/fowl meat and products; Prepared foods; Red/other non-poultry meat and products; Soap/body care products
description: The Scituate Rotary Farmers Market is run by a volunteer committee and 100 percent of set up fees go to charity. Our farmers are local, and most farms are located only a few miles from the market. The market has special events each week. We have 25-30 vendors, live music, special events, food trucks, we feature a different local author each week, and the best good vibes around. We are located in a beautiful setting near the Scituate Reservoir.

name: Hope Street Farmers Market
products: Fresh fruits; Fresh vegetables; Baked goods; Bedding plants; Canned or preserved fruits/vegetables; Coffee/tea; Cut flowers; Dairy products.; Eggs; Fish/seafood; Fresh/dried herbs; Honey; Juices/non-alcoholic ciders; Maple products; Mushrooms; Pet food; Poultry/fowl meat and products; Prepared foods; Red/other non-poultry meat and products; Wild harvested forest products

name: Ojai Certified Farmers' Market
description: The Ojai Certified Farmers' Market has operated for over 18 years behind the Arcade at 300 E Matilija St in downtown Ojai. Vendors sell locally grown fruits and vegetables harvested daily, along with jams, honey, breads, seafood, natural meats, eggs, nuts, dried fruit, prepared food, cut flowers, plants, and handmade soap. Ojai's famous Pixie tangerines appear in spring.

name: Old Monterey Farmers' Market
street: Alvarado St. at Franklin St., between Del Monte and Pearl

name: Old Town Calabasas Farmers' Market
description: The Old Town Calabasas Farmers Market is a certified year-round market on Calabasas Road in Old Town Calabasas. Vendors offer locally sourced produce including fruit, flowers, honey, nuts, eggs, cheese, and wine. The market runs rain or shine.

name: Old Town Elk Grove Farmers' Market
Old Town Temecula Farmers' Market
description: A weekly farmers' market in the heart of Old Town Temecula at Sixth & Front Street, drawing local farmers and vendors to the historic downtown district.

name: Otay Ranch Farmers' Market
description: The Otay Ranch Farmers Market is held weekly at the Otay Ranch Town Center, an open-air shopping center at 2015 Birch Rd in Chula Vista. Local sellers and farms offer fresh produce, prepared foods, and pantry staples.

name: Pacific Beach Tuesday Market
description: The Pacific Beach Tuesday Farmers' Market has operated since 2011 at 901 Hornblend Street in San Diego, offering fresh produce, meats, cheeses, flowers, prepared foods, and handcrafted artisan goods from local vendors.

name: Pacific Grove Farmers' Market
season: Mondays 3pm–7pm (3pm–6pm in winter), year-round except two weeks around the December holidays
description: Run by Everyone's Harvest at the corner of Central and Grand Avenues in Pacific Grove, this year-round certified market operates every Monday afternoon and runs through holiday weekends.

name: Paramount Farmers' Market
description: A certified farmers' market in Paramount, California, serving as a weekly community marketplace for locally grown produce and agricultural products.

name: Park West Place Stockton Farmers' Market
Pasadena Farmers' Market at Pasadena Corporate Center
People's Produce Farmers Market
season: Wednesday mornings (Mt. Hope Community Garden); multiple weekly neighborhood stops
description: The People's Produce Farmers Market is run by Project New Village as part of its mobile farmers market program serving the Good Food District of Southeastern San Diego, National City, Barrio Logan, and Lemon Grove. A refrigerated solar-powered truck brings locally sourced fresh produce to neighborhood sites across greater Southeastern San Diego.

name: Pescadero Grown! Farmers' Markets
season: Thursdays 3–7 pm, June through early November (seasonal)
description: Pescadero Grown! is a seasonal community farmers' market in Pescadero run by the nonprofit Puente, featuring local produce, grass-fed beef and pork, herbs, and jams alongside children's activities.

name: Placentia Certified Farmers' Market
description: A certified farmers' market with roughly 30 vendors offering fresh produce and homemade goods at the Placentia Town Center, at the corner of Yorba Linda Boulevard and Kraemer Boulevard.

name: Portola Valley Farmers' Market
description: A certified farmers' market operated by San Mateo County's Agriculture and Weights & Measures Department at 765 Portola Road, offering locally produced goods.

name: Quincy Farmers' Market
season: June through September, second Wednesday of the month 5–7:30pm (backyard of Rich Bar Taproom, 60 Main St)
description: The Quincy Farmers Market is a revived community market in downtown Quincy, in the Lost Sierra of Plumas County. Vendors sell locally sourced produce from regional farms and handcrafted goods from area artisans.

name: Rancho Santa Fe Certified Farmers' Market
Redlands Saturday Morning Certified Farmers' Market
Redondo Beach Farmers' Market
season: Year-round, Thursdays 8am-1pm (closed Thanksgiving and Christmas Day)
description: The Redondo Beach Certified Farmers' Market is a city-run market held outdoors at Veterans Park along the Esplanade, overlooking the ocean (it bills itself as "The Market with a View"). It offers certified fresh produce and other farm goods, and provides one hour of free validated parking in the Pier Parking Structure.

name: Redwood City Kiwanis Certified Farmers' Market
Redwood Empire Farmers' Markets
description: A farmers' market at the Santa Rosa Veteran's Building, 1351 Maple Ave, with 50+ vendors on Saturdays offering fresh fruits, vegetables, meats, cheeses, baked goods, and handcrafted items such as jewelry, pottery and soaps.

name: Redwood Valley Farmers Market
description: Part of the Mendocino County Farmers Market Association, this certified market is held at Lion's Club Park, 8920 East Road in Redwood Valley, offering locally sourced produce, meats, cheese, and prepared foods.

name: Richmond Certified Farmers' Market
season: Fridays 7:00am-1:00pm (Civic Center, 325 Civic Center Plaza); Sundays 10:00am-2:00pm (Marina Bay)
description: A certified farmers' market connecting farmers and consumers directly, featuring produce from growers representing various global regions, with markets at the Richmond Civic Center and Marina Bay.

name: Riverbank Certified Farmers' Market
description: The Riverbank Certified Farmers' Market is a seasonal certified market held in downtown Riverbank at 3600 Santa Fe St. It offers fresh produce and local goods and accepts both cash and card payments.

name: Roosevelt Park Farmers' Market
description: The Roosevelt Park Certified Farmers' Market is a seasonal certified market held at Roosevelt Park (9th and P Streets) in downtown Sacramento. It primarily offers farm-fresh seasonal produce alongside locally made goods.

name: Anchorage Farmers Market
description: The Anchorage Farmers Market is the only non-profit, farmer-directed market in Anchorage, dedicated to enhancing the viability of producing and marketing fresh Alaska Grown fruits, vegetables, herbs, flowers, plants, and animal products. Held in the Central Lutheran Church parking lot at 15th Avenue and Cordova Street, it features 100% Alaska Grown products and accepts WIC and Senior Farmers Market Coupons and QUEST.

name: Anchorage Market & Festival
season: Mid-May through mid-September, Saturdays 10am-6pm and Sundays 10am-5pm
description: The Anchorage Market & Festival brings together over 300 vendors across seven acres of downtown Anchorage at 3rd Avenue and E Street. Focused on Alaska-made artisan goods and prepared foods, it offers everything from reindeer sausage and salmon quesadillas to international cuisine, with free admission.

name: Central Kenai Peninsula Farmers' Market
street: E. Corral Ave. and Kenai Spur Hwy.
description: Established in 1995 as the Central Kenai Peninsula Farmers Market, this Soldotna market is a venue for peninsula growers to sell locally grown produce, herbs, plants, eggs, and honey, and has expanded to include cottage foods and local artists and craftspersons.

name: Ester Market
description: The Ester Community Market is a weekly market in Ester Community Park just outside Fairbanks, featuring local food and craft vendors along with a weekly live music series and special event days throughout the summer.

name: Farmers' Fresh Market
description: Farmers Fresh Market is a weekly Soldotna market held at the Food Bank on Kalifornsky Beach Road, offering locally grown produce and food items on the Kenai Peninsula.

name: Girdwood Farmers Market
description: The Girdwood Farmers Market (Into the Woods Market), operated by the Girdwood Land Trust, features artisan foods, fresh produce, and cottage goods from Alaska vendors at 148 Holmgren Place in Girdwood's Town Square. The market includes live music, booths, and activities.

name: Haines Farmer Market
description: The Haines Farmers Market is held at Payson's Pavilion at the Southeast Alaska State Fairgrounds, featuring fresh produce, handmade goods, food, and live entertainment.

name: Kenny Lake Harvest Farmers' Market
description: Kenny Lake Harvest Farmers' Market is a summer market in the Kenny Lake area of the Copper River region, offering fresh vegetables, homemade baked goods, flowers, and handcrafted items.

name: KGB Corner Market
street: 2150 S. Endeavor St., S. Knik Goose Bay Rd.

name: North Peninsula Recreation Service Area Farmer's Market
Northway Mall Wednesday Market
description: The Northway Mall Wednesday Market is an outdoor farmers market in the parking area of the Northway Mall in Anchorage, featuring a large variety of Alaska Grown produce, Alaska seafood, and other locally made foods.

name: Saturday Art and Farm Market
description: The Saturday Art and Farm Market is held at the Sutton Library and offers fresh produce, meats, dairy, and other items from local farms and artisans in the Matanuska Valley.

name: Seward Farmers Market
description: The Seward Farmers Market is operated by the Seward Wilderness Collective, a Seward, Alaska community organization that also offers hiking tours and outdoor experiences. The market connects the community with local vendors and produce.

name: South Anchorage Farmers' Market
street: O'Malley Rd. and Old Seward Hwy.
description: The South Anchorage Farmers Market is an outdoor seasonal market held in the O'Malley Sports Center parking lot in Anchorage, Alaska. It hosts over 30 vendors selling local Alaska produce and goods, with live music and a community gathering atmosphere.

name: South Anchorage Wednesday Market
description: The Dimond Center Farmers Market (South Anchorage Wednesday Market) brings together a mix of local produce, handcrafted goods, and prepared foods as a community gathering place supporting Alaskan growers and makers. It is held in the south-side parking lot of the Dimond Center at 800 E Dimond Blvd in Anchorage.

name: Southside Community Farmers Market
description: Southside Community Farmers Market is a farm- and food-focused market in the South Cushman neighborhood of Fairbanks, where vendors sell fresh food and farm products such as vegetables, herbs, meat, eggs, honey, bread, and cut flowers made using locally grown ingredients.

name: Tanana Valley Farmers Market
season: Wednesdays 11am-4pm and Saturdays 9am-4pm, May through September
description: The Tanana Valley Farmers Market in Fairbanks is the oldest established farmers market in Alaska and the only one located in its own permanent building, featuring Alaska Grown produce, Made in Alaska crafts, and Silver Hand Native art.

name: The Farmers Market at Alaska Pacific University
description: The Farmers Market at Alaska Pacific University is run by Spring Creek Farm, APU's ecologically and sustainably managed training farm, selling fresh Alaska Grown produce on the APU campus at 4225 University Drive in Anchorage. Most of the farm's produce is sold through its CSA, with additional vegetables available at this market.

name: The Salt & Soil Marketplace
season: Year-round, weekly online ordering with in-person pickup
description: The Salt & Soil Marketplace is a hybrid online-and-pickup community marketplace connecting Southeast Alaska consumers with local food producers, including growers, fishermen, foragers, and gardeners. Vendors upload weekly inventory online, customers order through the website, and purchases are picked up in person on market day. It is a project of Ecotrust based in Juneau.

name: Abbeville Farmers' Market
season: Thursdays, 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM, May through July

name: Abita Springs Farmers' Market
season: Sundays, 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM, rain or shine
description: The Abita Springs Art and Farmers Market offers locally grown produce, local wild-caught seafood, and local pastured poultry, with some markets featuring live music from the plaza stage.

name: Andalusia Power Plant Farmers' Market
description: The Andalusia Farmers' Market (also known as the Power Plant Farmers' Market) is a community market operated by the City of Andalusia's Leisure Services department, held at the Kiwanis Community Center. It offers locally grown produce and goods during the spring and summer growing season.

name: Arab Farmers' Market
description: The Arab Farmers' Market is a seasonal open-air market in Arab, Alabama, operating under a covered pavilion with twenty vendor booths (some with electrical hookups). It offers local produce, baked goods, and canned goods from area vendors.

name: Bailey Cove Farmers' Market
description: The Bailey Cove Farmers Market is held at St. Thomas Episcopal Church in Huntsville, featuring local vendors offering produce, meats, baked goods, and specialty items.

name: Bessemer Farmers Market
description: The Bessemer Farmers Market offers fresh local produce from local vendors at the Kenneth E. Gulley Recreation Center in Bessemer, Alabama.

name: Butler County Farmers' Market
season: Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Saturdays, 7:00 AM - 12:00 PM, spring through fall

name: Calera Farmers Market
season: Tuesdays, 3:00 PM - 7:00 PM, June through August, rain or shine
description: The Calera Farmers Market is held each Tuesday afternoon in Oliver Park, featuring local baked goods, eggs, herbs, vegetables, honey, jams, plants, and prepared foods.

name: Calhoun County Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, 7:00 AM - 11:00 AM, mid-May through end of September
description: The Calhoun County Farmers Market is a certified Alabama farmers market operating in an open-air structure in downtown Anniston, with offerings split between fresh and prepared food items and original crafts.

name: City Market
description: City Market is a Saturday farmers market in Auburn, Alabama, run in partnership between Auburn Parks and Recreation and local vendors, held at the Harris Center (425 Perry St). It features fresh, local, and handmade products from area farmers, growers, and artisans.

name: City of Centre Farmers' Market
description: The City of Centre (Cherokee County) Farmers Market offers fresh vegetables, local honey, canned homemade soup, pickled okra, pickles, organic eggs, and more. It is held in the National Guard Armory Building parking lot at 635 Armory Road in Centre.

name: Clio Market
season: Thursdays 12:30 PM - 3:00 PM and every 2nd and 4th Saturday 8:00 AM - 11:00 AM, June through September
description: The City of Clio Farmers Market is a small but growing market in the heart of downtown Clio, Alabama.

name: Coastal Alabama Farmers and Fishermen Market
Eastchase Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, 7:00 AM - 12:00 PM, May through September
description: The Shoppes at EastChase Farmers Market brings together local growers, bakers, and artisans every Saturday morning, featuring seasonal produce, homemade goods, and crafts in Montgomery's EastChase shopping district.

name: Enterprise Farmers' Market
description: The Enterprise Farmers' Market is a city-run community market in Enterprise, Alabama, offering locally grown produce and homemade goods. Items include seasonal vegetables, berries, boiled peanuts, canned goods, baked items, jams, honey, plants, and flowers.

name: Fairhope Outdoor Farmers Market
street: Magnolia Avenue and North Bancroft Street

name: Fairview Farmers' Market
season: Monday through Saturday 8:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Sunday 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM, year-round
description: Fairview Farmers Market is a covered, year-round market in Montgomery selling fresh produce, plants, homemade pepper sauces, crafts, and seasonal holiday gift baskets.

name: Florence - Lauderdale Farmers' Market
season: May: Saturdays 6:00 AM - 1:00 PM; June through September: Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Saturdays 6:00 AM - 1:00 PM; October: Saturdays 6:00 AM - 1:00 PM
description: The Florence/Lauderdale Farmers Market is owned and operated by the Lauderdale County Commission, providing access to wholesome, locally grown produce, farm-fresh eggs, meat, honey, and baked goods.

name: Helena Market Days
season: Saturdays, 8:00 AM - 12:00 PM, June through August
description: Helena Market Days is held in Old Town Helena at Amphitheater Park on the bank of Buck Creek, giving local farmers and producers a venue to sell fresh vegetables, flowers, honey, baked goods, and handmade items directly to residents.

name: Historic Langdale Farmers' Market
season: Fridays, 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM, mid-May through mid-August
description: The Historic Langdale Mill Farmers Market is held at the Farmers Market Pavilion in Valley, Alabama, offering locally grown produce and goods from area farmers and vendors.

name: Lawrence County Farmers' Market
season: Tuesdays 6:00 AM - 1:00 PM, Thursdays 1:00 PM - 7:00 PM, and Saturdays 6:00 AM - 1:00 PM, April through November

name: Limestone County Farmers' Market
season: Tuesdays and Fridays, 6:30 AM - 1:00 PM, May through November

name: Macon County Farmers' Market
description: The Macon County Farmers Market, run through Tuskegee University's Cooperative Extension Program, offers fresh, locally sourced produce, eggs, honey, jams, plants, prepared foods, and artisanal goods from local vendors. It is located at the intersection of Elm and Spring Streets in Tuskegee, across from the post office, and accepts cash, card, SNAP/EBT, FMNP, and WIC.

name: Madison County Farmers' Market
description: The Madison County Farmers Market is a long-running market in Huntsville, Alabama, at 1022 Cook Avenue NW, with roots dating back nearly two centuries; it moved to its current location in 1984. It features fresh, locally grown produce such as corn, okra, tomatoes, squash, and beans.

name: Montgomery Curb Market
season: Open year-round on Tuesday, Thursday and Saturday from 5:00 AM to 2:00 PM.
description: Established in 1927, the Montgomery Curb Market is a year-round market with over 30 vendors and farmers selling locally produced seasonal vegetables and fruits, along with artisan crafts, canned goods, candles, soaps, lotions, baked goods, plants and teas.

name: Morgan County/Decatur Farmers' Market
description: The Morgan County/Decatur Farmers Market, established in 1937, is one of the oldest farmers markets in Alabama, located at 211 1st Ave SE in Decatur. It primarily offers farm-fresh seasonal produce along with a selection of locally made goods.

name: Mt Laurel Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, 8:00 AM - 12:00 PM, June through October
description: The Mt Laurel Farmers Market gathers local farmers and vendors in the Town Center offering fresh fruits and vegetables, homemade salsas, jellies, and baked items, plus artisan-made crafts.

name: Northport Farmers' Market
description: The Northport Farmers Market is a seasonal market at 4150 5th Street in Northport, Alabama, offering fresh seasonal produce, locally baked goods, specialty foods, and handmade items from area farmers and artisans.

name: Ozark Farmers' Market
season: Open every Thursday from 3:00 PM to 7:00 PM, mid-May through September.
description: Coordinated by the Ozark Chamber of Commerce, the Ozark Farmers Market has provided a gathering place for local foods and crafts in the Ozark community since 2012, hosting local farmers, bakers and crafters each week during the season.

name: Pinson Farmers' Market
season: Open Thursdays from 2:00 PM to 6:00 PM during the spring, summer and fall season.
description: Held at the Rock School Center on Pinson Boulevard, the Pinson Fresh Farmers Market offers fresh fruits, vegetables and meats from local Alabama farmers alongside soaps, handmade goods, seasonal plants and local honey.

name: Rocky Ridge Church Farmers' Market
season: Open Tuesday afternoons from 3:00 PM to 6:00 PM, May through August.
description: The Rocky Ridge Church Farmers' Market is a seasonal summer market in the Birmingham area, operating Tuesday afternoons during May through August.

name: Ross Bridge Farmers' Market
season: Select Fridays, 4:00 PM - 8:00 PM, spring through summer
description: The Ross Bridge Farmers Market is a free community market in Hoover featuring local farmers, artisans, bakers, and food trucks, with live music and activities for children on select Friday evenings.

name: Selma Farmers' Market
season: Open Tuesday, Thursday and Saturday from 6:00 AM to 2:00 PM during the summer-through-fall season.
description: Located at 108 West Dallas Avenue in Selma, the Selma/Dallas County Farmers Market offers locally grown vegetables such as squash, corn, tomatoes and peas, along with fruits including figs, plums, watermelon and peaches.

name: St. Clair County Farmers' Market
season: Open every Wednesday from 1:00 PM to 5:00 PM, beginning the first Wednesday in June through the last Wednesday of September (weather permitting).
description: Held at Lakeside Park near the boat launch in Pell City, the St. Clair County Farmers Market features anchor produce vendors with farm-fresh fruits and vegetables alongside vendors selling honey, fried pies, flowers and more.

name: The Market at Good Shepherd
description: The Market at Good Shepherd in Decatur is a market for local farmers and artisans sponsored by the Church of the Good Shepherd, Episcopal, and is free of charge to attend.

name: Trussville Fresh Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, 8:00 AM - 12:00 PM, April through November
description: The Trussville Fresh Farmers Market is held at the Trussville Entertainment District, offering produce and goods locally sourced from in and around Trussville, Alabama.

name: Walker County Farmers' Market
season: Open Tuesday, Thursday and Saturday, April through November.
description: Operating since 1981, the Walker County Farmers Market brings fresh, local produce to market in Jasper on Tuesdays, Thursdays and Saturdays from April to November, serving Walker County and surrounding communities.

name: Argenta Certified Farmers Market
city: 7Th And Main Street North Little Rock

name: Arkansas Home Grown Market
city: 8700 N. Rodney Parham Road Little Rock

name: City of Gravette, Parks Department-Farmers Market
Lafayette County Farmers Market at Lewisville
Stone County Farmers Market
city: 121 Main Street (Centennial Bank Park) Mountain View

name: The Central Ozarks Farmers & Artisans Market
Ahwatukee Farmers Market
season: Sundays year-round; winter hours (October-May) 9am-1pm, summer hours (June-September) 8am-11am
description: The Ahwatukee Farmers Market offers fresh, local, seasonal produce, herbs, flowers, value-added foods, fresh baked goods, natural meats and seafood, along with a selection of local arts and crafts.

name: ASU Tempe Farmers' Market
season: Every other Tuesday, 9am-2pm, seasonally in spring and fall
description: The ASU Tempe Farmers' Market sets up at Cady Mall on the Arizona State University Tempe campus, featuring seasonal produce, specialty food items produced in Arizona, and ready-to-eat food and beverages.

name: Cave Creek Farmers' & Crafts Market
description: The Cave Creek Farmers' & Crafts Market is a year-round market in Cave Creek, Arizona, at 6424 E. Cave Creek Rd. It combines farm produce with craft and artisan vendors.

name: Downtown Chandler Farmers' Market
description: The Downtown Chandler Farmers Market is a year-round Saturday market held at Dr. A.J. Chandler Park (West & East Ramadas) in downtown Chandler, Arizona, organized by Downtown Chandler. Hours shift seasonally to help vendors and shoppers beat the summer heat.

name: Downtown Farmers' Market
description: The Downtown Farmers Market Arts & Crafts Mercado is held on the south lawn of the Tucson Main Library, offering fresh fruits and vegetables alongside arts and crafts from local artists.

name: Downtown Mesa Farmers Market
description: The Downtown Mesa Farmers Market gathers local vendors offering fresh produce, artisanal goods, baked items, and handmade crafts in downtown Mesa along Center Street.

name: Downtown Phoenix Public Market
products: Fresh fruits; Fresh vegetables; Baked goods; Bedding plants; Canned or preserved fruits/vegetables; Coffee/tea; Crafts/woodworking items; Cut flowers; Dairy products.; Dry beans; Eggs; Fish/seafood; Fresh/dried herbs; Grains/flour; Honey; Juices/non-alcoholic ciders; Mushrooms; Nuts; Pet food; Prepared foods; Red/other non-poultry meat and products; Soap/body care products; Wild harvested forest products

name: Farmers' Market Cashman Park at Desert...
Flagstaff Community Markets
description: Flagstaff Community Markets operates a seasonal farmers market at City Hall (211 W Aspen Ave) in Flagstaff, Arizona, along with an urban flea market and a winter sleigh market. Dogs are not permitted at the farmers market.

name: Food Bank Farmers' Market
description: The Community Food Bank Market is one of three weekly farmers markets run by the Community Food Bank of Southern Arizona, held at the food bank's Tucson campus on South Country Club Road. The markets accept WIC, SNAP, and AZFMNP as well as cash and credit, expanding access to fresh local produce.

name: Get Local Arizona Events Farmers Markets
Heirloom Farmers Market - Green Valley Village
description: The Heirloom Farmers Market at Green Valley Village is the area's longest-running farmers marketplace, featuring local food vendors and artisans with fresh produce, prepared foods, and handmade goods at the Green Valley Village Shopping Center along the I-19 corridor south of Tucson.

name: Heirloom Farmers Market - Oro Valley (Steam Pump Ranch)
description: The Oro Valley Farmers Market sets up every Saturday morning at Steam Pump Ranch at the base of the Catalina Mountains' Pusch Ridge along the Northwest Oracle Road corridor, featuring local produce, meat, eggs, and artisan goods.

name: Heirloom Farmers Market - Rillito Park
description: The Heirloom Farmers Market at Rillito Park is held at the Food Pavilion in Rillito Park and is run by Heirloom Farmers Markets, a Southern Arizona nonprofit connecting the community to local food producers.

name: Heirloom Farmers Market - Rincon Valley
description: The Heirloom Farmers Market at Rincon Valley sits at the base of the Rincon Mountain District of Saguaro National Park, serving Tucson's rural east side and Vail with locally grown produce, fresh-roasted coffee, baked goods, and handmade artisan goods.

name: Heirloom Farmers Markets - Udall Park
description: The Heirloom Farmers Market at Udall Park is a Friday market on Tucson's east side with over 40 vendors offering local and organic produce, farm-fresh eggs, Arizona grass-fed beef, local honey, freshly roasted coffee, baked goods, and more.

name: Kingman Farmers Market
description: The Kingman Farmers Market is a Saturday market in Kingman, Arizona, that has relocated to Thunder-Rode. It runs every Saturday from spring through fall with seasonally adjusted hours.

name: Market in Carver Park
street: 13th Ave. and 3rd St., Between 3rd and 5th St.

name: Momma's Organic Market
description: Momma's Organic Market is a Saturday farmers market in Peoria, Arizona, at 9744 W Northern Ave (Park West). It is part of a small chain of organic-focused markets in the Phoenix area and also hosts seasonal evening night markets.

name: Oro Valley Farmers Market
description: The Oro Valley Farmers Market has operated since 2004 at historic Steam Pump Ranch (10901 N Oracle Rd) in Oro Valley, Arizona, set among preserved heritage buildings and gardens. It supports local farms, ranches, and food purveyors, selling local fruits, vegetables, beef, pork, and farm-fresh eggs, and accepts SNAP/EBT, WIC, and the Arizona Farmers Market Nutrition Program.

name: Park West Market
season: Saturdays; summer day markets 8am-12pm and summer night markets 6pm-9pm (May-September), fall markets 9am-1pm (October-April)
description: Desert West Market (formerly Park West Market) is a community marketplace at Estrella Mountain Community College in Peoria featuring handmade crafts, artisanal goods, farm-to-table products, local food vendors, and live entertainment.

name: Power Road Farmers Market
description: Power Road Farmers Market is a family-owned produce and specialty market in Mesa, operating for over 50 years with a commitment to local farmers and vendors and the freshest produce in the valley.

name: Rillito Park Farmers Market
description: The Rillito Park Farmers Market is run by Heirloom Farmers Markets, a Southern Arizona nonprofit connecting the community to local food producers and supporting a vibrant regional food system, held at the Food Pavilion in Rillito Park.

name: Rincon Valley Farmers Market
description: Located at the base of the Rincon Mountain District of Saguaro National Park at the distinctive big green and white barn, this market has served the rural east side of Tucson and Vail since 2001 with locally grown produce, freshly roasted coffee, baked goods, tortillas, salsas, ready-to-eat foods and handmade artisan goods.

name: San Tan Valley Farmers Market
season: Second Saturday of each month, 7:00am 'til sold out
description: The San Tan Valley Farmers Market is a monthly market and bazaar held at 2505 East Germann Road in San Tan Valley, Arizona. It brings together local growers and vendors selling fresh produce alongside other goods, running until vendors sell out. The market operates on the second Saturday of each month.

name: Santa Cruz River Farmers' Market
description: The Santa Cruz River Farmers' Market operates on Thursdays at the Mercado District (Mercado San Agustin Annex) in Tucson, connecting local producers with the community to expand access to fresh, local food. Vendors offer locally grown produce and Arizona-made goods, and the market accepts SNAP, Double Up Food Bucks, and WIC nutrition coupons.

name: Sedona Community Farmers Market
description: The Sedona Community Farmers Market is a grower-based market in the Wells Fargo Bank parking lot at 2201 W State Route 89A in West Sedona. Vendors bring only what they grow, with farm inspections held twice per season, offering organic and pesticide-free produce, breads, dairy, meat, eggs, jams, kombucha and other locally made goods.

name: Sedona Community Farmers Market at Tlaquepaque / Creekside
description: The Sedona Community Farmers Market supports local sustainable agricultural activity, with vendors offering locally grown, pesticide-free produce and artisan products.

name: Show Low Main Street Farmers' Market & Art Walk
description: The Show Low Main Street Farmers' Market & Art Walk hosts 60+ booths offering locally grown plant starts, meats, fruits and vegetables, locally produced food items, and handmade arts and crafts, with entertainment and educational programs throughout the season.

name: Sierra Vista Farmers Market
description: The Sierra Vista Farmers Market has operated since 2005 at Veterans Memorial Park in Sierra Vista, Arizona. It features in-season produce, homemade baked goods, fresh eggs, raw milk, locally raised frozen beef, chicken and pork, handcrafted items, food trucks and live music.

name: Sunnyside Farmers Market/Market of Dreams
description: The Sunnyside Farmers Market, run by Flagstaff's Market of Dreams, supports independent growers and producers by providing residents a local alternative in the Sunnyside neighborhood.

name: Super Farm Market
description: Super Farm Market at Superstition Farm is held at a working dairy farm in Mesa featuring farm products and a petting zoo, located at 3440 S Hawes Rd.

name: The Farmers Market at Vincent's on Camelback
The Farmers' Market of Dove Mountain
The Mother Road Farmers Market
description: Seasonal Saturday farmers market in Downtown Winslow at the corner of 2nd and Williamson, transitioning from a City of Winslow program to a non-profit.

name: Udall Park Farmers Market
description: Held at 7202 E. Tanque Verde Rd. in Tucson, this Friday market features over 40 vendors offering local and organic produce, farm-fresh eggs, Arizona grass-fed beef, local honey, fresh locally roasted coffee, baked goods, salsa, tortillas, freshly roasted vegetables, pet products and soaps. The market is accessible and has restrooms on site.

name: Verde Valley Farmers Market
description: One of Arizona's oldest farmers markets, this non-profit market operated by farmers runs from spring through mid-autumn at 75 E. Hollamon St. in Camp Verde, offering local produce, baked goods, preserves, meat, nuts, honey and more, with weekly live music.

name: Carmichael Park Farmers' Market
description: The Carmichael Farmers' Market is a certified market held every Sunday year-round at Carmichael Park, 5750 Grant Ave. It offers freshly picked seasonal produce from small regional family farms along with honey, eggs, meat, cheese, baked goods, prepared foods and handmade crafts.

name: Alemany Farmers' Market
description: Known as 'the People's Market,' the Alemany Farmers Market at 100 Alemany Boulevard opened in 1943 as California's first farmers market and features local farmers, many from multi-generational family farms.

name: Alhambra Certified Farmers' Market
description: The Alhambra Certified Farmers' Market at 100 South Second Street offers fresh, affordable fruits, vegetables, honey, herbs, and baked goods sourced directly from local growers and vendors.

name: Alisal Farmers' Market
description: The Alisal Certified Farmers' Market at 632 E Alisal Street in Salinas is operated by Everyone's Harvest and runs year-round, offering fresh local produce to the East Salinas community.

name: Altadena Farmers Market
description: A 501(c)(3) nonprofit farmers' market in Altadena focused on providing access to local produce with an emphasis on dignity and cultural inclusion, which expanded after the Eaton Fire to include free produce distributions and an Eco-Hub for community resilience.

name: Angels Camp "Fresh Fridays" Certified Farmers Market
Antelope Valley Winery Farmer's Market
description: Billed as the Antelope Valley's oldest farmers market, this market at 42041 20th Street West in Lancaster brings together fresh eggs, local produce, and artisan crafts.

name: Aptos Farmers' Market
description: The Aptos Farmers' Market is a year-round Saturday market at Cabrillo College (6500 Soquel Dr, Aptos), run by the Monterey Bay Certified Farmers Markets. Roughly 90 vendors sell California-grown produce, flowers and specialty foods, including organic items, eggs, cheese, seafood and baked goods.

name: Arroyo Grande City Hall Farmers' Market
Associated Students Farmers' Market
description: Associated Students operates a weekly Farmers Market on the San Francisco State University campus to empower students to make healthy food choices with fresh and organic (when possible) produce and natural food items while supporting local farmers and businesses. Vendors offer hot foods, produce, and more, and the market accepts EBT.

name: Baldwin La Brea Certified Farmers' Market
Bank of America Famers' Market
description: Held in the courtyard of the Bank of America building at 333 S Hope St in Downtown Los Angeles, this weekly certified market (formerly the Bank of America Farmers Market, now the Bunker Hill Farmers Market) brings fresh produce and prepared food to the Bunker Hill district.

name: Belmont Farmers' Market
street: El Camino Real and O'Neill St.

name: Benicia Certified Farmers Market
products: Fresh fruits; Fresh vegetables; Baked goods; Canned or preserved fruits/vegetables; Coffee/tea; Crafts/woodworking items; Cut flowers; Dairy products.; Eggs; Fish/seafood; Fresh/dried herbs; Honey; Mushrooms; Nuts; Poultry/fowl meat and products; Prepared foods; Red/other non-poultry meat and products; Soap/body care products
description: The Benicia Certified Farmers Market is the largest independent certified farmers market in Solano County, active since 1993. It runs along Benicia's historic downtown waterfront and features produce, specialty and artisan goods, and live music.

name: Bishop Farmers' Market
description: The Bishop Farmers' Market, part of the Eastern Sierra Certified Farmers' Markets, is held on Church Street directly behind City Hall and features seasonal produce, baked goods, flowers, and other local farm products.

name: Bonsall Certified Farmer's Market and Open Air Fair
Brea Farmers' Market
description: The Brea Farmers Market at 200 South Birch Street offers fresh produce and handmade items alongside live music, food trucks, and family-friendly activities.

name: Brentwood Farmers' Market
street: 694 1st St., between Chestnut St. and Oak St.

name: Canyon Lake Certified Farmers' Market
description: Active since 2008 at the Canyon Lake Towne Center on Railroad Canyon Road, the Canyon Lake Certified Farmers Market offers locally grown fruits and vegetables, local honey and fresh eggs, handmade items, and prepared foods from various vendors.

name: Capitol Mall Farmers' Market
street: 6th St. and Capitol Mall , Between 5th St. & 6th St.
season: Wednesdays, 9am–1pm, late April through late October
description: The Capitol Mall Farmers' Market is a seasonal certified market held on Capitol Mall Greens in downtown Sacramento (between 5th and 6th Streets). Shoppers can buy farm-fresh produce direct from growers and grab lunch from local food and beverage vendors; CalFresh/EBT benefits are accepted.

name: Carmel Certified Farmers' Market
description: The Carmel Certified Farmers' Market is a seasonal market at The Barnyard Shopping Village in Carmel, run by the Monterey Bay Certified Farmers Markets. More than 25 farmers and vendors offer local fresh farm produce and specialty items.

name: Cayucos Farmers' Market
street: Corner of D and Ocean Ave.

name: Central Certified Farmers' Market
description: The Central Certified Farmers' Market is a Sunday certified farmers' market held in Sacramento under the W-X freeway between 5th and 8th Streets. It is one of the long-running California Grown / Sacramento-area certified markets offering farm-fresh produce direct from regional growers.

name: Cesar Chavez Plaza Farmers' Market
description: The Cesar Chavez Plaza Farmers' Market is a seasonal certified farmers' market held at Cesar Chavez Plaza (10th & J Streets) in downtown Sacramento. It offers farm-fresh produce and goods direct from regional growers in the heart of the city.

name: City of Poway Saturday Farmers Market
Claremont Farmers' Market
description: The Claremont Farmers' Market runs every Sunday in Claremont Village, presented by the Claremont Forum. Shoppers find fresh produce from local farms alongside handcrafted goods.

name: Costa Mesa Farmers' Market
description: The Costa Mesa Certified Farmers' Market is a year-round open-air market held at the OC Fair & Event Center, 88 Fair Drive in Costa Mesa, run by the Orange County Farm Bureau. It is one of the Farm Bureau's Orange County certified markets and offers free admission and parking.

name: Cottonwood Creek Certified Farmers Market & Artisan Fair
CSU Long Beach Farmers' Market
description: The Cal State Long Beach Certified Farmers' Market is a weekly market held on the CSULB campus (near 1250 N. Bellflower Blvd) and is open to the university community and surrounding neighbors. It offers certified farm-fresh produce and goods during the academic year.

name: Daly City Farmers' Market at Serramonte Center
Danville Farmers' Market
street: Railroad Ave. and W. Prospect Ave.

name: Del Monte Farmers' Market
description: The Del Monte Farmers Market in Monterey offers a bountiful array of locally grown produce, cut flowers, and potted plants in the Del Monte Center parking lot. Operated by the Monterey Bay Certified Farmers Markets, it runs rain or shine.

name: Del Paso Heights Community Farmers' Market
Diablo Valley Farmers' Market
street: N. Wiget Ln. and Mitchell Dr.
description: The Diablo Valley Farmers' Market in Walnut Creek's Shadelands Business Park features 50+ farmers and vendors bringing produce from nearby Brentwood-area farms, with a gourmet atmosphere and a Kid's Craft Corner. It was voted #1 in California in the America's Favorite Farmers' Markets contest.

name: Downtown SLO Thursday Night Farmers' Market
East Santa Clara Street Farmers' Market
street: N. 14th St. at E. Santa Clara St.

name: Ecology Center Farmers' Markets
season: Year-round; Tuesday 2:00–6:30pm (Adeline & 63rd), Thursday 3:00–7:00pm (Shattuck & Vine), Saturday 10:00am–2:30pm (Center St & MLK Jr. Way)
description: The Ecology Center operates three weekly Berkeley Farmers' Markets featuring local, sustainably grown produce and farm products. Vendors sell fruits, vegetables, nuts, baked goods, jams, juices, olive oils, meat, cheese, prepared foods, plants and flowers; all locations accept CalFresh EBT and offer Market Match incentives.

name: El Cerrito Farmers' Market
description: The El Cerrito Plaza Farmers' Market is a certified market at El Cerrito Plaza (3020 El Cerrito Plaza), operated by the Pacific Coast Farmers' Market Association. It runs year-round on Tuesdays and Saturdays with state-certified vendors selling fresh local produce and goods.

name: El Dorado Hills Farmers' Market
description: The El Dorado Hills Farmers' Market is one of four markets run by the El Dorado County Certified Farmers' Market Association, a nonprofit supporting local growers under California's certified farmers' market program. Vendors sell just-picked local produce alongside packaged foods and crafts. The El Dorado Hills market operates on Sundays and relocated to the Community Center as of March 30, 2025.

name: Encino Farmers Market
description: The Encino Farmers Market is operated by the nonprofit ONEgeneration as a community fundraiser, held at the organization's site at 17400 Victory Boulevard in Van Nuys. Market proceeds support ONEgeneration's senior and intergenerational programs. It runs year-round on Sunday mornings.

name: Fallbrook Main Ave Certified Farmers Market
description: The Fallbrook Main Avenue Certified Farmers Market is a year-round market held downtown on Main Avenue in Fallbrook, managed by the Fallbrook Chamber of Commerce. It runs on Saturday mornings with a rotating roster of vendors offering fresh produce and local goods.

name: Farmers' Market at The Grove School
description: Certified farmers' market in the parking lot of The Grove School (a Redlands public charter school with a working farm) featuring local farmers and vendors plus the student-run Raven's Cafe.

name: Fontana Farmers' Market
season: Year-round; Sundays 10am–3pm (Fontana Park) and Thursdays 3–7pm (Southridge Park)
description: The Fontana Farmers' Market is a year-round open-air market run by the City of Fontana's Healthy Fontana program, selling fresh produce and local goods. The city operates two weekly markets: a Sunday market at Fontana Park and a Thursday afternoon market at Southridge Park.

name: Fort Bragg Farmers' Market
season: Wednesdays year-round, 2:30–5:30pm (May–October) and 2:30–4:30pm (November–April), in downtown Fort Bragg at Laurel & Franklin

name: Freemont Park Farmers' Markets
description: The Fremont Park Certified Farmers' Market is a seasonal certified market held in Fremont Park at 16th and P Streets in midtown Sacramento. It offers locally grown produce and farm goods during the spring and summer months.

name: Galleria at Tyler Riverside Farmers' Market
Gigi's Farmers Market at The Americana at Brand
Grand Lake - Oakland Farmers' Market
street: Grand Ave. at Lake Park Ave.

name: Gustine Recreation Department Certified Farmers' Market
H&K Farms Rio Linda Sunday Farmers Market
Hayward Farmers Market
description: The Hayward Farmers Market sits at 777 B Street in front of City Hall, one block from BART, and features over 35 local farmers, food purveyors and artisans with a focus on smaller producers (growing to nearly 60 farms in summer). It runs rain or shine with weekly live music and accepts CalFresh EBT/SNAP, Market Match, and WIC.

name: Heritage Farmers Market at The Terra Vista Town Center
description: The Heritage Farmers Market is a program of the nonprofit Heritage Wellness Collective and features 65+ vendors selling fresh produce, prepared foods, and artisan wares; it accepts SNAP/CalFresh (EBT). The Rancho Cucamonga market, formerly at Terra Vista Town Center, now operates Saturdays at the RC Sports Center.

name: Historic Downtown LA Certified Farmers' Market
street: 5th St. and Spring St., between Broadway and Spring St.

name: Historic Folsom Farmers Market
description: The Historic Folsom Farmers Market is a year-round market held in the plaza on Sutter Street in Folsom's historic district. Running rain or shine, it hosts up to 80+ vendors offering produce, hot food, coffee, and pastries.

name: Howard Hughes Center Farmers' Market
description: The Howard Hughes Center Farmers Market is run by the nonprofit Raw Inspiration at the Howard Hughes Center complex on Center Drive in Los Angeles. It features traditional produce and flower stands plus food booths, artisan bread, and pastry vendors, operating weekly on Thursdays.

name: Imperial Beach Certified Farmers' Market
description: The Imperial Beach Certified Farmers' Market is a certified market held at Pier Plaza (10 Evergreen Ave) in Imperial Beach. It runs year-round on Friday afternoons, with extended hours during the warmer months.

name: Kaiser Permanente Fontana Certified Farmers' Market
Kaiser Permanente Irvine Certified Farmers' Market
Kaiser Permanente Los Angeles Medical Center Farmers' Market at Barnsdall Art Park
Kaiser Permanente Ontario Certified Farmers' Market
Kaiser Permanente Redwood City Farmers' Market
Kaiser Permanente Riverside Certified Farmers' Market
Kaiser Permanente San Francisco Farmers' Market
street: Geary Blvd. and St. Joseph's St.

name: Kaiser Permanente San Leandro Farmers' Market
Kaiser Permanente Union City Farmers' Market
Kaiser Permanente Walnut Creek Farmers' Market
Kaiser Permanete Downey Certified Farmers' Market
Kaiser San Jose Farmers Market
description: The Kaiser Permanente San Jose Farmers' Market is operated by the Pacific Coast Farmers' Market Association (PCFMA), founded in 1988 and the oldest and largest farmers' market association on the West Coast, which runs over 30 certified markets across the San Francisco Bay Area in support of California family farms and a healthy local food system. The market sells fresh California-grown produce and other goods sourced directly from regional farmers.

name: Kaiser South San Francisco Farmers' Market
La Jolla Elementary Certified Farmers' Market
description: The La Jolla Open Aire Market (La Jolla Elementary Certified Farmers' Market) has operated as a community gathering place for 25 years, featuring over 120 vendors offering fresh produce, local artisans, and prepared foods. All proceeds benefit La Jolla Elementary School (LJES), a public school.

name: La Quinta Certified Farmers' Market
description: The La Quinta Certified Farmers Market is a seasonal certified market in Old Town La Quinta, run by Coachella Valley Certified Farmers Markets. It offers locally grown flowers, fruits, vegetables, eggs, and honey plus chef-prepared foods and artisan goods sourced within about 100 miles of the Coachella Valley.

name: Laguna Beach Farmers' Market
description: The Laguna Beach Farmers' Market is a Saturday-morning certified market managed by the Orange County Farm Bureau, held in the Lumberyard parking lot on Forest Avenue near City Hall. Over 40 vendors and farmers sell freshly picked produce, flowers, local honey, and homemade foods.

name: Laguna Gateway Center Farmers' Market
street: Laguna Blvd. and Big Horn Blvd.
description: The Laguna Gateway Center Certified Farmers' Market is a year-round market in Elk Grove at Laguna Boulevard and Big Horn Boulevard (8245 Laguna Blvd). Open rain or shine, it accepts CalFresh/EBT and offers Market Match nutrition incentives, closing only on Christmas Day or in extreme weather.

name: Laguna Niguel Farmers' Market
description: The Laguna Niguel Farmers' Market is a year-round certified open-air market operated by the Orange County Farm Bureau in the Plaza de la Paz Shopping Center on La Paz Road. Around 30–40 vendors sell farm-fresh fruits and vegetables plus goat cheese, honey, mushrooms, and baked goods.

name: Lake of the Pines Farmers' Market
Lancaster Farmers' Market
description: The Lancaster Farmers Market, also known as the BLVD Market, is run by the nonprofit Raw Inspiration on West Lancaster Boulevard in downtown Lancaster. The seasonal Thursday-evening market features 15–20 vendors with farm-fresh produce, artisan goods, food trucks, and locally crafted products.

name: Land Park Farmers' Market
description: The Land Park (William Land Park) Farmers Market is a year-round Sunday market in Sacramento, part of the I Love My Farmers Market network, selling locally grown produce, farm-processed foods, and specialty gourmet items from small businesses. It typically operates at the Sacramento City College lot near 21st Street.

name: Local Harvest Certified Farmers' Market Wilmington
description: The Local Harvest Certified Farmers' Market in Wilmington ("Heart of the Harbor") is a year-round certified market at Avalon Blvd. and L St. in Wilmington, with indoor space and produce locally sourced from the Los Angeles area. It operates weekly on Tuesdays.
