name: Local Harvest Garden Grove Farmers' Market
description: The Local Harvest Garden Grove Farmers' Market is a year-round outdoor certified market on Historic Main Street offering California-grown fruits and vegetables along with gourmet specialty foods, arts, crafts, and jewelry from local vendors, with local musicians providing live entertainment.

name: Long Beach Downtown Farmers Market
description: The Long Beach Downtown Farmers Market is operated by Harbor Area Farmers Markets in partnership with the South Coast Interfaith Council, with a shared mission to help people with nourishment for their mind and body. The market offers locally grown California produce directly from farmers to the community and operates rain or shine.

name: Long Beach Southeast Farmers Market
season: Sundays (Long Beach Marina), open rain or shine
description: The Long Beach Southeast (Marina) Farmers Market is operated by Harbor Area Farmers Markets in partnership with the South Coast Interfaith Council, with a shared mission to help people with nourishment for their mind and body. The market offers locally grown California produce directly from farmers to the community and operates rain or shine.

name: Long Beach Uptown Farmers Market
season: Thursdays (Bixby Knolls), open rain or shine
description: The Long Beach Uptown (Bixby Knolls) Farmers Market is operated by Harbor Area Farmers Markets in partnership with the South Coast Interfaith Council, with a shared mission to help people with nourishment for their mind and body. The market offers locally grown California produce directly from farmers to the community and operates rain or shine.

name: Los Gatos Farmers' Market
street: Main St. at N. Santa Cruz Ave

name: Mack Road Valley Hi Farmers' Market
Main St. Canoga Park Certified Farmers' Market
Mar Vista Farmers' Market
description: The Mar Vista Farmers Market is described as a bustling, transient town square offering farm-fresh produce, grass-fed meats, cheeses, seafood, and prepared foods. It features dining tents and a community gathering space and is open year-round, rain or shine, except major holidays.

name: Marina Certified Farmers' Market
description: The Marina Certified Farmers' Market is operated by Everyone's Harvest, a 501(c)(3) non-profit that runs certified farmers markets and community food programs to provide access to healthy, affordable fruits and vegetables. The market takes place on Sundays in Marina, California.

name: Mariposa Farmers' Market
description: Certified farmers' market at 5029 Stroming Road in Mariposa, California, offering locally grown produce, specialty foods, and goods from area artisans and crafters.

name: Market in the Park
description: Market in the Park & Food Truck Frenzy is a seasonal summer event at the Fountain Valley Recreation Center & Sports Park, pairing a certified farmers market with some of the best food trucks in Orange County. It runs on Thursday evenings during the summer; attendees are encouraged to come early and stay for Summerfest or the Summer Concert Series.

name: Melrose Place Farmers' Market
description: The Melrose Place Farmers Market is a weekly certified market on Melrose Avenue/Place in Los Angeles, part of the California Certified Farmers Markets (CCFM) network. The Sunday-morning market features artisan-crafted goods, freshly prepared food, and local produce.

name: Merced Original County Farmers' Market
description: The Merced Certified Farmers' Market is the city's longest-running, year-round certified market, held Saturday mornings near 19th and N Streets in downtown Merced. It offers fresh local produce and farm goods, operating every Saturday except major holidays.

name: Mira Mesa Farmers' Market
description: Weekly certified farmers' market held at Mira Mesa High School, 10510 Reagan Rd., San Diego, with on-site parking in the school lot.

name: Miracle Mile Farmers' Market
description: The Miracle Mile Farmers' Market is a weekly certified farmers' market operated by the non-profit Raw Inspiration in the Miracle Mile district of Los Angeles, set up at 5700 Wilshire Blvd. Raw Inspiration runs a network of Southern California farmers' markets and school garden programs, with vendors bringing California-grown produce and local goods. The market is held on Wednesday afternoons.

name: Modesto Farmers' Market
description: The Modesto Certified Farmers Market sets up on 16th Street between H and I Streets in downtown Modesto, near the Stanislaus County Library, with street parking available. Vendors offer fresh produce, prepared food, and artisan goods from local farmers and makers, alongside crafters and musicians. The market runs on Saturdays and, in season, Thursdays.

name: Monrovia Street Fair and Farmers' Market
description: The Monrovia Street Fair & Market is a weekly evening street fair and farmers' market held year-round in Old Town Monrovia along South Myrtle Avenue. The Friday-night event combines a certified farmers' market with food vendors, artisan stalls, and a community street-fair atmosphere. It is operated in association with Raw Inspiration.

name: Nipomo Farmers' Market
street: corner of Via Concha Rd. and Trilogy Pkwy

name: Noe Valley Farmers' Market
street: 3861 24th St., Between Sanchez and Vicksburg St.
description: The Noe Valley Farmers Market is a California certified farmers' market held at the Noe Valley Town Square (3861 24th St) in San Francisco, operated primarily by community volunteers. Most vendors sell organic fruits and vegetables, with an emphasis on pesticide-free, sustainably grown produce from small local farms. It runs every Saturday year-round.

name: NoHo Arts District Farmers' Market
street: Bakman Ave. between Weddington Blvd.and Chandler Blvd.

name: North San Diego Certified Farmers' Market
Novato Downtown Farmers' Market
street: Grant Ave. and 1st St., between 1st and 4th St.

name: Oceanside Farmers' Market
street: N. Coast Hwy. 101 and Pier View Way

name: Old Town La Verne Fresh Farmers'...
Pasadena Victory Park Farmers' Market
street: Sierra Madre Blvd. and Paloma St.

name: PQ Certified Farmers' Market
street: Fairgrove Ln. and Salmon River Rd.

name: Reedley Farmers' Market
description: The Reedley Farmers' Market is a seasonal open-air market held on G Street in downtown Reedley, between 10th and 12th Streets. Vendors offer fresh local produce and goods on Wednesday evenings during the warmer months. It is organized in connection with the downtown Reedley business district.

name: Rio Vista Certified Farmers' Market
street: Main St. and 2nd St., Between 2nd and 3rd

name: Rossmoor Farmers' Market
street: Tice Creek Dr. at Golden Rain Rd.

name: San Anselmo Farmers' Market
description: Local and regional farmers, bakers and food purveyors showcase their high quality, diverse, seasonal bounty of organic and specialty fruits, vegetables, nuts, fresh-cut flowers, prepared artisan foods, breads, pastries, local rotisserie chicken and more. The market is held in the Elan Fitness Center parking lot at 220 Greenfield Ave, at the intersection of Red Hill Ave and Sequoia Dr in San Anselmo.

name: San Carlos Hot Harvest Nights - Farmers' Market
San Clemente Certified Farmers' Market
street: Avenida Del Mar at S. Calle Seville
description: The San Clemente Certified Farmers' Market is held on Avenida Del Mar in downtown San Clemente, featuring local farmers and purveyors of fresh, locally grown produce. The market has operated for more than two decades and emphasizes supporting the local farm economy and community. It takes place on Sunday mornings.

name: San Dimas Farmers Market
description: The San Dimas Farmers Market is a certified market at 245 E Bonita Ave in downtown San Dimas, featuring California Certified Producers (farmers), prepared foods and food vendors, plus limited arts and crafts and health and beauty vendors. It runs on Wednesday evenings during the spring and summer season on non-school nights.

name: San Francisco Farmers' Market at Crocker Galleria
San Jacinto Valley Certified Farmers' Market
San Leandro Downtown Farmers' Market
street: 1 Parrott St., between E. 14th St. and Washington Ave.

name: San Leandro Farmers' Market at Bayfair Center
San Luis Obispo County Farmers' Market
San Rafael - Marin Farmers' Market
description: Known as the "Chef's Market," this year-round market at the Marin County Civic Center in San Rafael draws professional restaurateurs and home cooks alike, with 85+ participants offering hyperlocal meats, specialty pastries, and breads beside the lagoon with views of Mount Tamalpais.

name: San Rafael Downtown Farmers' Market
description: The Downtown San Rafael Farmers' Market is operated by the Agricultural Institute of Marin on Fourth Street in downtown San Rafael, drawing from a network of 400+ farmers, food purveyors, and artisans across more than 40 California counties. Stalls offer locally grown produce, specialty foods, and handcrafted goods. It currently runs as a seasonal evening market on select Fridays in spring and summer.

name: Santa Cruz Westside Farmers' Market
description: The Westside Farmers' Market is one of the Santa Cruz Farmers Markets, held at the corner of Highway 1 (Mission Street) and Western Drive on the west side of Santa Cruz. It serves the west-end communities including Bonny Doon, the North Coast, and the UC Santa Cruz campus, with local farmers and food vendors. The market runs every Saturday year-round.

name: Santa Maria Town Center Farmers Market
Santa Monica Farmers' Market
street: Arizona Ave. and 4th St., between 4th St. and Ocean Ave.
description: The Santa Monica Wednesday Downtown Farmers Market, established in 1981, is one of the largest and most diverse certified farmers' markets in the country, set up along Arizona Avenue between 2nd and 4th Streets in downtown Santa Monica. More than 60 California farms spread across four blocks bring locally grown produce and flowers direct from farm to table. The market is run by the City of Santa Monica, operates rain or shine year-round, and accepts CalFresh EBT.

name: Santa Monica Sunday Main Street Farmers' Market
description: The Santa Monica Sunday Main Street Farmers Market is one of four weekly certified markets run by the City of Santa Monica, held in the Main Street neighborhood near 2640 Main Street. It features California farmers and small food businesses bringing locally grown produce and prepared foods. The market operates on Sunday mornings and accepts CalFresh EBT.

name: Santa Teresa Farmers' Market
street: Santa Teresa Blvd. and Camino Verde

name: Santee Certified Farmers' Market
street: Corner of Mast and Carlton Hills Blvd
description: The Santee Certified Farmers' Market is held at the corner of Mast Boulevard and Carlton Hills Boulevard in Santee. Vendors offer fresh produce such as strawberries and carrots, breads, baked goods, and items from local artisans. It runs every Wednesday afternoon with seasonal hours, and accepts WIC, EBT, and credit/debit cards.

name: Saratoga Village Farmers' Market
street: Hwy. 9, Big Basin Way at Saratoga Ave.
description: Held at West Valley College in Saratoga, this certified market brings together over 50 local farmers and vendors offering seasonal fruits and vegetables from Santa Clara County, plus Asian vegetables, honey, mushrooms, eggs, and fresh roasted herbed-crusted chicken.

name: Scotts Valley Farmers' Market
description: Saturday market at the Joe and Linda Aliberti Boys and Girls Club, 5060 Scotts Valley Dr., with regional farms and vendors selling organic produce, pasture-raised eggs, flowers, artisanal meats, breads, seafood, and prepared food, plus weekly live music.

name: Seal Beach Village Certified Farmers' Market
Sherman Oaks Farmers' Market
description: The Sherman Oaks Farmers' Market is a weekly certified farmers' market operated by the non-profit Raw Inspiration at 14006 Riverside Dr in Sherman Oaks. Raw Inspiration runs a network of Southern California farmers' markets and school garden programs, with vendors offering California-grown produce and local goods. The market is held on Tuesday afternoons.

name: Sonoma'sTuesday Night Market
description: Held on Sonoma Plaza, this evening market features farm-fresh produce, gourmet packaged foods, handcrafted goods from local artisans, ready-to-eat offerings, and live music, presented by the City of Sonoma with the Sonoma Valley Chamber of Commerce.

name: South Pasadena Farmers' Market
description: The South Pasadena Farmers' Market, established in 1999 and managed by the South Pasadena Chamber of Commerce since 2010, is held under a canopy of large trees near the South Pasadena Metro A Line station. It offers certified California-grown produce direct from local farms, prepared-food vendors, and live music, and accepts CalFresh/EBT. The market runs every Thursday afternoon year-round, except Thanksgiving and major holidays.

name: Southgate Certified Farmers' Market
description: The South Gate Certified Farmers' Market is a year-round Monday market operated by Harbor Area Farmers' Markets, located at the northwest corner of Tweedy Blvd. and Pinehurst Ave. The market accepts EBT, Market Match and WIC payment assistance.

name: Sunrise Mall Farmers Market
description: The Sunrise Mall Farmers Market is a weekly certified market in Citrus Heights, held in front of the Macy's parking lot at Sunrise Mall (6041 Sunrise Blvd) and operated by the Living Smart Foundation. With up to 80+ vendors, it offers organic produce, baked goods, prepared foods, eggs, meat, honey, flowers, plants, and crafts. It runs every Saturday year-round, rain or shine, with free parking and WIC/EBT accepted.

name: Susanville Farmers' Market
street: Richmond Rd. and S. Railroad Ave.

name: Sustainable Organic Local Community Farmers' Market
Tehachapi Farmers' Market
street: S. Robinson St. and E. Tehachapi Blvd.

name: The College of San Mateo Farmers' Market
description: The College of San Mateo Farmers' Market is a certified market operated by the Pacific Coast Farmers' Market Association (PCFMA) on the College of San Mateo campus at 1700 W. Hillsdale Blvd. It features fresh California-grown produce along with other vendors, and participates in CalFresh/EBT and WIC Market Match programs. The market runs Saturdays, 9:00am-1:00pm, year-round.

name: The Market on Kern
street: Kern St. and N St., Between M St. and N St.
description: The Market on Kern is a weekly outdoor market held on Kern Street between M and N Streets, in front of the Fresno Convention Center in downtown Fresno. It features fresh produce, flowers, snacks, food trucks, artisan and craft vendors, and entertainment. The market runs Wednesdays from late spring through fall and is sponsored by PG&E and the Civic Center.

name: The Peoples' Mountain Market
street: Corner of Marshall Rd. & Garden Valley Rd.
description: Seasonal community farmers' market held at Garden Valley Park (4300 State Highway 49) in the Garden Valley area, run by the Georgetown Divide Recreation District.

name: Third Avenue Village Certified Farmers' Market
description: The Third Avenue Village Certified Farmers' Market is held in Downtown Chula Vista along Center Street, offering farm-fresh produce and other vendor goods. It is organized by the Downtown Chula Vista business association and runs as a weekly Sunday community market.

name: Thousand Oaks Farmers' Market
street: Wilbur Rd. & W. Thousand Oaks Blvd.

name: Topanga Farmers' Market
description: The Topanga Farmers Market is a certified farmers market held at the Topanga Community Center, 1440 N. Topanga Canyon Blvd. in Topanga, California. It hosts produce, prepared food, and artisan vendors. The market operates every Friday from 9:00am to 1:00pm.

name: Turtle Bay Certified Farmers' Market
description: The Turtle Bay / Sundial Bridge Certified Farmers' Market is held in the Sundial Bridge parking lot at Turtle Bay in Redding, operated by the Shasta Growers Association, which has run certified Shasta County markets since 1977. It offers locally grown produce and accepts EBT benefits. The market runs Sundays, 9:00am-1:00pm.

name: Tustin Farmers' Market
description: The Tustin Certified Farmers' Market is held at 3rd Street and El Camino Real in historic Old Town Tustin. As a California-certified market affiliated with the Orange County Farm Bureau, vendors are county-inspected to ensure they grow what they sell. Shoppers find fresh produce, citrus, local honey, farm-fresh eggs, flowers, and plants.

name: UC Davis Health System Farmers' Market
Union City Farmers' Market
description: The Union City Farmers' Market is held in Old Alvarado Park at Smith and Watkins Streets and is operated by the Pacific Coast Farmers' Market Association (PCFMA). The market offers fresh produce, fish, breads, and local honey, running weekly on Saturday mornings.

name: USC Farmers' Market
season: Most Wednesdays 11am–3pm during the academic year
description: The Trojan Farmers Market sets up on McCarthy Quad at USC's University Park Campus, offering a wide selection of produce and groceries alongside hot-food options such as freshly grilled sausages, hosted by Undergraduate Student Government and USC Hospitality.

name: Va Palo Alto Farmers Market
description: The VA Palo Alto Farmers' Market is a certified market operated by the Pacific Coast Farmers' Market Association (PCFMA) at the VA Palo Alto Health Care System, 3801 Miranda Ave. in Palo Alto. It offers fresh California-grown produce and other vendor goods and participates in CalFresh/EBT and WIC Market Match programs. The market runs Wednesdays, 9:30am-1:30pm, seasonally.

name: VA San Francisco Farmers' Market
street: Veterans Dr., between Clement St. and 42nd Ave.

name: Visalia Downtown Farmers' Market
street: E. Main St. & N. Church St.

name: Visalia Farmers' Market
street: S. Mooney Blvd & W. Caldwell Ave.
description: The Visalia Farmers' Market is held in the solar-panel-covered parking lot at 2231 W. Main Street near Mooney Boulevard, featuring over 60 merchants with locally grown produce and handmade products. It is a certified market that accepts WIC vouchers, EBT/P-EBT, cash, and cards, and operates rain or shine.

name: Weaverville Farmers' Market
description: The Weaverville Certified Farmers' Market is held at the Highland Art Center Meadow on Main Street in Weaverville. It is a certified market and community gathering space featuring local produce, products, and artisans from the Trinity County area, running weekly on Wednesday afternoons from spring through early fall.

name: West Valley Mall Stockton Farmers' Market
Westchester Farmers' Market
description: Sunday farmers' market at 6200 West 87th Street in Westchester's Downtown Triangle, with farmers, ranchers, specialty growers and artisans selling fresh seasonal produce, herbs and vegetables, plus live music and kids' entertainment.

name: Westlake Village Farmers' Market
description: The Westlake Village Farmers' Market is held at 2797 Agoura Road and is operated by the nonprofit Raw Inspiration, which runs certified farmers' markets across Southern California. The market takes place weekly on Sunday mornings into the early afternoon.

name: Wrightwood Certified Farmers Market
description: Certified community farmers' market at 1275 Park Drive in Wrightwood, California, offering locally grown organic and seasonal fruits, vegetables and foods from area farmers, artisans and businesses.

name: Yuba Sutter Certified Farmers' Market
description: The Yuba Sutter Certified Farmers' Market is held at The Fountain at Townsquare Park, 471 Plumas Blvd. in Yuba City, and is run through the Sutter County Agricultural Department. It is a certified market featuring locally grown fruits, nuts, and vegetables, operating weekly on Saturday mornings during the growing season.

name: Aspen Farmers' Market
street: E. Hopkins Ave. and S. Galena St.
description: The Aspen Saturday Market is held in downtown Aspen, forming a U-shape from the corner of Galena and Hopkins Avenue to Hunter Street. The market features local produce, prepared foods, and artisan goods, running weekly on Saturdays through the summer and early fall.

name: Arvada Farmers Market
description: The Arvada Farmers Market is held in Olde Town Arvada near 57th and Old Wadsworth and offers locally and regionally produced goods including fresh produce, Colorado peaches, honey, breads, cheeses, baked goods, prepared foods, and artisan crafts. It runs as a weekly Sunday market through the summer, with a separate December holiday market.

name: Carbondale Farmers' Market
description: The Carbondale Farmers Market is held at 4th and Main Street in downtown Carbondale and features fresh produce from Colorado's Western Slope, prepared foods, and locally crafted artisan goods from Colorado-based vendors. It runs weekly on Wednesdays through the summer and uses zero-waste, compostable serviceware.

name: Cherry Creek Valley International Farmers Market
City of Edgewater Farmers Market
description: The City of Edgewater Farmers Market is held at 2401 Sheridan Blvd. in Edgewater and is a seasonal outdoor market featuring local produce and vendor goods. It runs weekly on Thursday evenings through the summer months.

name: Cortez Farmers Market
description: The Cortez Farmers Market is held every Saturday morning through October at the Children's Kiva Montessori Charter School parking lot (Empire Street and Mildred Road) in Cortez. It features fresh local produce, artisan goods, live music and family-friendly activities, and accepts debit, EBT/SNAP and Double Up Food Bucks.

name: Creede Farmers, Local Artisan and Antique Market
Drake Road Farmers Market
description: The Drake Road Farmers' Market is a seasonal open-air market at 802 W. Drake Road in Fort Collins, on the northwest corner of West Drake Road and Worthington. It offers a wide range of Colorado produce, fruits, baked goods, eggs, honey, jams, meats, salsas, coffee, and other local goods, and accepts SNAP benefits. The market runs weekly on Saturday mornings.

name: Erie Farmers Market
description: The Erie Farmers Market is an outdoor producer market held on Briggs Street between Wells and Moffat in downtown Erie, Colorado. Vendors offer locally grown produce, meats, eggs, artisan breads, honey, cheese, coffee, baked goods, natural body-care products, flowers, plants, and prepared foods, with an emphasis on Colorado-grown organic and pesticide-free items.

name: Farm and Ranch Market
description: Farm and Ranch Market is a long-running seasonal farmers market in Grand Junction, Colorado, billing itself as continuing 40 years of bringing fresh local agriculture products to the Grand Valley. It features produce from several family-owned local valley farms.

name: Feed Denver: Urban Farms & Markets
description: Feed Denver: Urban Farms & Markets is a nonprofit urban-farming organization and farm market at 2139 W. 44th Avenue in Denver's Sunnyside/Highland area. Its market offers just-harvested produce, fresh-baked breads, homemade jams and marmalades, seeds, soil and plant starts, worm products, and local crafts and gifts, with indoor space available.

name: Florence Farmers' Market
street: N. Pikes Peak Ave. and E. 3rd St.

name: Fort Collins Winter Farmers Market
description: Northern Colorado's indoor home for fresh, local food and handmade goods throughout the colder months, the Fort Collins Winter Farmers Market hosts Colorado growers and makers at the Foothills Mall in Fort Collins.

name: Fort Morgan Farmers' Market
street: Corner of Beaver Ave. and State. St.

name: Four Seasons Farmers and Artisans Market
Fruita Farmers' Market
description: A friendly, hometown farmers' market in Downtown Fruita featuring live music, locally grown fruits and vegetables, and talented local artisans at Fruita Civic Center Park.

name: Granby Farmers Market
description: The Granby Farmers Market (Granby Music & Market) is a summer event in Granby, Colorado pairing fresh local produce with live music on Friday nights at Polhamus Park. Vendors offer peak-season produce and homegrown greens alongside handmade jewelry, baked goods, and art from regional makers.

name: Highland Farmers' Market
season: Sundays, May through October, 9 AM - 1 PM
description: Since 2014, The Highlands Farmers Market has been a Sunday gathering spot for NW Denver, featuring locally grown produce, handmade items, and prepared food alongside live music.

name: Hoffmann Commercial Real Estate Farmers' Market...
Idaho Springs Chamber Of Commerce Farmers Market
Local Brands Farm and Ranch Markets Cooperative
Loveland East Farmers Market at Jax
Northfield Farmers Market
description: The Northfield Farmers Market is held on Sundays at The Green (29th Ave & Roslyn) in Denver's Northfield neighborhood, managed by Jarman and Co. for the MCA. It features Colorado-grown produce, baked goods, specialty meats and gourmet food items, with all products required to be sourced from Colorado or bordering counties.

name: Pagosa Springs Farmers Market
description: The Pagosa Springs Farmers Market is sponsored by the Southwest Organization for Sustainability (SOS) and provides a venue for local and regional growers, food producers and makers to sell their goods in Pagosa Springs, Colorado. The market accepts SNAP/EBT and debit cards and offers Double Up Food Bucks matching up to $20 per visit.

name: Parker Farmers Market
season: Sundays, Mother's Day through end of October, 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM
description: A vibrant Sunday market on Mainstreet with over 100 vendors, including local vegetable growers and western-slope fruit growers, offering fresh produce, flowers, baked goods, and ready-made foods.

name: Pueblo Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, May through October, 7:30 AM - Noon
description: A Saturday-morning market at Mineral Palace Park in Pueblo offering Colorado-grown produce, meat, baked goods, and handmade products alongside local makers and live music.

name: Salida Farmers Market
season: June-October, Saturdays 8am-12pm (9am-12pm mid-September through late October)
description: The Salida Farmers Market is held at Alpine Park in downtown Salida and features 70+ local vendors offering Colorado-grown produce, freshly baked sourdough bread, pastries and baked goods, regeneratively raised meat, eggs, dairy and goat cheese, plus jewelry, art, crafts and clothing. The market also hosts food trucks and hot-food vendors and live music throughout the morning. Dogs are not permitted except for ADA service animals.

name: South Fork Farmers, Local Artisan and Antique Market
South Pearl Farmers Market
description: The South Pearl Street Farmers Market runs along the 1400 and 1500 blocks of South Pearl Street (between Iowa and Arkansas) in Denver's historic South Pearl shopping district. It features 170+ local vendors selling fresh Colorado produce, baked goods, craft coffee, flowers and prepared foods. Products must be grown, raised, produced or gathered by the vendor in Colorado or in counties bordering the state, with limited case-by-case exceptions.

name: The Saturday Market at Tagawa Gardens
Andover Farmers' Market
description: The Andover Farmers' Market is a producer-only market held at Andover Town Park on Bartlet Street, featuring local farmers, artisans, and community partners and operating rain or shine.

name: Bethel Farmers' Market
description: The Bethel Farmers' Market, held at 67 Stony Hill Road, has become a local center for a wide variety of produce ranging from fruit and vegetables to meat products, soap, baked goods, and home-made jams and salsas.

name: Bishop's Orchards Farm Market & Winery
season: Farm Market open year-round; Pick-Your-Own June through the end of October
description: Bishop's Orchards Farm Market, located on Route 1 in Guilford, is a destination for local produce, gourmet grocery items, and Bishop's own bakery and prepared foods, alongside an on-site winery and creamery.

name: Black Rock Farmers Market
description: The Farmers' Market of Black Rock brings together local farmers and vendors from Bridgeport and Fairfield County at St. Ann's Field, offering fresh produce, meats, and seafood alongside handmade goods and live music.

name: Bridgeport Downtown Farmers' Market
description: The Downtown Bridgeport Farmers' Market is held at McLevy Green and is part of the Bridgeport Farmers Market Collaborative connecting residents with fresh local produce in the heart of downtown.

name: CitySeed Indoor Winter Farmers' Market
description: CitySeed's indoor Winter Farmers Market is one of Connecticut's largest urban markets and has been a New Haven staple for over two decades, keeping local produce, dairy, eggs, meat and honey available through the cold months.

name: Danielson Farmers Market
description: Run by the Northeast CT Farmers Market and held at the Killingly Library in Danielson, this Saturday-morning market brings locally grown products straight from area farms. Vendors sell fruits, vegetables, meat, poultry, soaps, fresh-baked items, jellies, jams, pickles and other farm products. Established in 1980, most vendors accept credit/debit cards and the market honors Farmers Market Nutrition Program vouchers.

name: Downtown Farmers' Market
street: Corner of Temple St. and Chapel St

name: Durham Farmers' Market
description: The Durham Farm Market & More is a CT-grown market on the Town Green, meaning all produce and products sold are Connecticut grown or made with Connecticut products.

name: Edgewood Park Farmers' Market
street: W. Rock Ave. and Whalley Ave.
description: The Edgewood Park Farmers' Market is a CitySeed-operated farmers market held in Edgewood Park in New Haven, near the corner of W. Rock Avenue and Whalley Avenue. The Saturday-morning market offers locally grown produce and other goods from Connecticut farmers and vendors. It runs across the warm-weather season as part of CitySeed's network of New Haven markets.

name: Fairfield Brickwalk Farmers Market
description: A family-friendly outdoor market held on the BrickWalk Promenade in downtown Fairfield, part of the Kleban-managed Brick Walk retail and residential complex. Vendors offer fresh-picked organic fruits and vegetables, natural meats, herbs, milk, gluten-free European pastries, homemade baked goods and crepes. The market runs Saturday mornings during the warm-weather season.

name: Farmers Market at Sun Valley Resort
First Presbyterian Church Farmers Market
description: A seasonal farmers market held next to the First Presbyterian Church and The Bushnell at 136 Capitol Avenue in Hartford. The market offers fresh quality organic and non-organic produce, honey, free-range eggs, fresh-cut flowers and homemade jams, with guaranteed CT Grown/Made products. It operates weekly on Monday afternoons through the summer and fall.

name: Hamden Farmers' Market
season: Thursdays, June through September, weather permitting
description: An annual outdoor market held at Hamden's Town Center Park, the Hamden Farmers Market aims to stimulate the town's small-business economy by connecting fresh, quality local produce and artisanal goods directly to residents.

name: Hartford Park Street Farmers' Market
street: Corner of Park St. & Washington St.
description: The Park Street Farmers' Market brings fresh, locally grown fruits and vegetables to Hartford's Park Street neighborhood. It is one of several seasonal markets operated by Hartford to serve different neighborhoods with affordable fresh produce. The market is held on the corner of Park Street and Washington Street in front of the Walgreen's Pharmacy.

name: Hartford's North End Farmers' Market
description: Hartford's North End Farmers' Market offers fresh, locally grown fruits and vegetables from area farms and serves more than 5,000 visitors annually. Run by the Hartford Food System, the market accepts SNAP benefits and offers incentives to help low-income families purchase fresh produce. It is held in front of the North End Senior Center at 80 Coventry Street.

name: Hebron Farmers Market
description: The Hebron Farmers Market is held at One Main Street in Hebron and features local farms and vendors offering fresh, locally-grown produce and other regional products.

name: Higganum Village Farmers' Market
description: The Higganum Village Farmers' Market is held at the Brainerd Memorial Library on Saybrook Road in Haddam, offering Connecticut-grown produce, eggs, meats, baked goods, honey, herbs, cut flowers, and artisanal foods.

name: Hill Market Farmers' Market
street: Corner of Park St. and South St.

name: Homestead Avenue Farmers Market at the Ch...
description: The Homestead Avenue Farmers' Market is a seasonal neighborhood market in Hartford located at 255 Homestead Avenue at the Chrysalis Center. It is one of several community farmers' markets operated across Hartford to bring fresh, locally grown produce to city neighborhoods.

name: Larson's Farm Market
description: Larson's Farm Market in New Milford offers fresh, locally sourced produce along with food products and goods from regional vendors, including USDA Certified organic items. Located on Danbury Road (Route 7), it brings local farm products and artisanal goods to the New Milford community.

name: Ledyard Farmers' Market
description: The Ledyard Farmers Market is a seasonal outdoor market held on the lower town green at 718 Colonel Ledyard Hwy in Ledyard, Connecticut. It runs through the summer months with local vendors, listing a 2026 vendor lineup and weekday afternoon market dates. The market operates from June through September.

name: Lyme Farmers Market
description: The Lyme Farmers Market is located on Ashlawn Farm, a working farm in the rural town of Lyme, Connecticut, offering locally grown and produced goods.

name: Middletown Farmers' Market
street: South Green on Old Church St.
description: The Middletown Farmers Market is held at Union Green on the corner of Main and Pleasant Street, offering local produce and goods to the community.

name: Milford Woodmont Farmers' Market at the Robert Treat Farm
description: Robert Treat Farm is a family-owned farm, market and garden center in Milford, where family farming on the property has been a tradition since Milford was founded in 1639. The farm operates a farm store and garden center and offers a CSA / farm share program, set in a post-and-beam barn built in 2008 just three blocks from Long Island Sound.

name: Naugatuck Farmers' Market
street: Corner of Church St. & Division St.

name: New Canaan Farmers Market
description: A Connecticut Grown Food Hub, the New Canaan Farmers Market is located at the Lumber Yard Lot near the train station at 244 Elm Street in downtown New Canaan.

name: New Canaan Winter Market
description: The New Canaan Farmers Market is a Connecticut Grown food hub operating at the Lumber Yard Lot near the train station in New Canaan.

name: New London Field of Greens L&M Market
New London Field of Greens Markets
New Milford Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, Mother's Day weekend through November, 9:00am-12:00pm
description: The New Milford Farmers' Market is held on the town green on Saturday mornings during the growing season.

name: North End Farmers Market
description: The North End Farmers Market in Middletown offers locally grown produce from area farms and vendors during the summer and fall growing season.

name: Southbury Farmers' Market
description: The Southbury Farmers' Market is a producer-only market on the Southbury Town Hall Green, meaning the farmer or specialty food maker behind the table is the person who grew or made the product, offering plants, veggies, fruit, honey, jam, maple syrup, olive oil, baked goods, and wines.

name: Stamford Downtown Farmers' Market
description: The Stamford Downtown Farmers' Market offers a wide variety of locally grown fruits, vegetables and flowers, plus breads, jams, baked goods, cheeses, oils and more straight from the farm. Held at Veterans Memorial Park, the market accepts WIC and SNAP and features a free morning yoga class and live music. Complimentary parking is available at Landmark Garage with a voucher from the Market Manager tent.

name: Stamford Museum & Nature Center - Friday Farm Market
description: The Stamford Museum & Nature Center hosts a weekly outdoor farm market in the Knobloch Family Farmhouse parking lot (north entrance, 151 Scofieldtown Rd, off the museum's 39 Scofieldtown Rd location). Vendors sell seasonal vegetables, fruit, eggs, locally raised chicken, beef and pork, dairy from Arethusa Farms, honey, artisan breads, baked goods, and handmade crafts. Market admission is free; SM&NC members receive 10% off Heckscher Farm tent purchases.

name: Stratford Farmers Market
description: The Stratford Farmers Market is held at Paradise Green on Huntington Road and features Connecticut-grown produce, local meats, fish, cheeses, baked goods, and crafts.

name: Suffield Farmers' Market
street: N. Main St. and High St.

name: Torrington Farmers' Market
season: Tuesdays 3:00pm-6:00pm and Saturdays 10:00am-1:00pm, June through October
description: The Torrington Farmers' Market is held at the Sullivan Senior Center lot on Charles Street, is free and open to the public, and offers free on-site parking.

name: Trumbull Farmers' Market
description: The Trumbull Farmers' Market is held at the NIA Ballfields on Unity Road and features plants, fruits and vegetables, baked goods, meats, eggs, fresh breads, and prepared foods.

name: Watertown Farmers' Market
description: The Watertown Farmers' Market is a Live Well Watertown community market offering fresh local produce and goods on Wednesday afternoons during the growing season.

name: West End Farmers' Market
description: The West End Farmers Market is located at Clemens Place Green in Hartford and features local vendors offering fresh produce and goods.

name: Whole Foods Market of Westport Winter Farmers' Market
Winter Farmers Market Greenfield Hill Grange, Fairfield, CT
Wooster Square Farmers' Market
street: Corner of Chapel St. and DePalma Ct.
season: Outdoor April 4–Nov 21, Saturdays 9am–1pm; Indoor Jan 3–March 28 and Dec 5–19, Saturdays 10am–1pm
description: The Wooster Square Farmers' Market is a producer-only market run by CitySeed in New Haven, Connecticut, held at 511 Chapel Street in the Russo Park / Wooster Square area. Vendors offer Connecticut-grown seasonal produce, baked goods, dairy, meat, fish, prepared foods and honey. It is one of Connecticut's largest urban markets and accepts SNAP benefits (doubled), cash, credit, and Venmo.

name: Farmers' Market at Sea Colony
season: Wednesdays, 8:00 AM to 12:00 noon, late May through early September
description: The Farmers' Market at Sea Colony is a non-profit, community-run producer-only market featuring vendors who only sell products they grow or produce within 60 miles of Bethany Beach, with most produce harvested the morning of the market.

name: Fenwick Island Farmers' Market
street: Coastal Hwy. and E. Essex St.

name: Loockerman Way Farmers' Market
street: Loockerman St. and S. Bradford St.

name: Milton Farmers' Market
description: The Milton Farmers Market is a seasonal community market held on Union Street in Milton, Delaware. It offers locally grown produce and other goods from area farmers and producers, and accepts EBT/SNAP and FMNP benefits with EBT values doubled up to $20.

name: Smyrna Farmers' Market
description: The Smyrna Farmers' Market is a seasonal market held at 106 W. Commerce St. in Smyrna, Delaware. It features local growers including USDA Certified organic products, with indoor space available, and operates on Saturday mornings through the growing season.

name: Abacoa Town Center Green Market
description: The Abacoa Town Center Green Market is a weekly evening market in downtown Jupiter featuring 30+ local vendors and live music, with offerings ranging from fresh produce and seafood to baked goods and artisan products.

name: Alachua Farmers Market
description: The Alachua Farmers Market is a weekly market in downtown Alachua, Florida, offering fresh produce and farm-fresh foods from local growers. It revives the town's tradition of residents gathering downtown to sell crops and goods.

name: Alliance for the Arts Green Market
description: The Alliance for the Arts GreenMarket is a year-round producer market at 10091 McGregor Blvd. in Fort Myers, Florida, on the Alliance for the Arts campus. Vendors offer locally grown vegetables, fruits, meat, eggs, cheese and honey, and a free yoga session is offered Saturday mornings.

name: Aventura Farmers Market
description: The Aventura Farmers Market is the largest indoor market in South Florida, operating for over 20 years at Aventura Mall. It features a diverse array of exotic foods, fresh produce, handmade goods, accessories, home décor and more, celebrating local small businesses and South Florida's cultural diversity across the mall's Lower and Upper Levels.

name: Beach Baptist Farmers Market Fort Myers Beach
Central Sarasota Farmers Market
description: The Central Sarasota Farmers Market is a year-round, rain-or-shine market at 4748 S Beneva Rd in Sarasota, Florida, at the Suncoast Technical College campus. Vendors offer vegetables, fruits, eggs, meat, poultry, honey, jams, baked goods, coffee, mushrooms, plants, soap and crafts.

name: Chiefland Farmer's Market
description: Operating since 1987, the Chiefland Farmers Flea Market is one of the largest weekend markets in North Central Florida, with hundreds of vendor booths offering fresh produce, antiques, handmade crafts, and collectibles off US-19.

name: Cocoa Farmers' Market
description: The Cocoa Farmers' Market offers fresh produce, local raw honey, farm eggs, organic soap, homemade dips, baked goods, plants and handmade crafts from area vendors.

name: Coconut Point Farmers Market
description: The Coconut Point Farmers Market is a seasonal market at Coconut Point Mall (Panera Bread parking lot), 23106 Fashion Drive in Estero, Florida. It features 60+ vendors offering fresh produce, seafood, meats, cut flowers, locally harvested honey and baked goods, plus handcrafts such as soaps, candles and baskets. Parking is free and the market is pet-friendly.

name: Collier County Government Complex Farmers' Market
Collins Park Sunday Market
description: A weekly Miami Beach gathering place run by The Market Company, the Collins Park Sunday Market brings together vendors offering fresh produce, local honey, hummus and homemade baked goods.

name: Coral Gables Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, January through March, 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM
description: Held at Merrick Park in front of Coral Gables City Hall, the market offers locally sourced produce, gourmet meals, baked products, floristry, educational workshops and children's activities.

name: Downtown Daytona Beach Farmers' Market
description: Located on City Island adjacent to the historic Jackie Robinson Ballpark, this pet-friendly market offers fruits and vegetables, nuts, baked goods, seafood, specialty foods and plants.

name: Downtown Farmers' Curb Market
description: The Downtown Farmers Curb Market is a long-running Saturday market in downtown Lakeland, Florida, along N. Kentucky Ave. from Lemon Street to Oak Street. It offers local, homegrown and handmade produce, plants, artisan gifts, pastries, breads and specialty foods, with live entertainment most Saturdays.

name: Downtown Fort Walton Beach Farmers Market
Downtown MarketPlace Tallahassee
season: Saturdays, March through November, 9:00 AM - 1:00 PM
description: Held at Ponce de Leon Park in downtown Tallahassee, the Downtown Market features local arts and fine crafts, Fresh from Florida produce, hand-raised beef and pork, baked bread, jams, honey and live music.

name: Downtown Melbourne Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM, October through May; 1st and 3rd Saturdays, June through September
description: Held at Riverview Park near downtown Melbourne, the market offers produce, honey, grass-fed meats, bakery items, coffee, prepared foods, artisan soaps and plants from local vendors and food trucks.

name: Downtown New Port Richey Market
description: The Downtown New Port Richey Market is a weekly market held in the courtyard of the New Port Richey Public Library at 5939 Main Street. Open rain or shine, it features locally sourced foods, sustainably grown produce, cottage-industry baked goods, hand-made items and more.

name: Eau Gallie Farmers' Market
street: Eau Gallie Boulevard and Highland Avenue
description: Located at Highland Avenue and Eau Gallie Boulevard in Melbourne's historic Eau Gallie Arts District, this year-round market offers indoor and outdoor vendor space with fresh produce and local goods.

name: Fatto a Mano ArtWalk Market
description: The Fatto a Mano ArtWalk Market is held monthly at the North Beach Restaurants & Shoppes Plaza in Fort Lauderdale, showcasing South Florida artists, artisan vendors, and crafters alongside local produce and food.

name: Fellsmere Farmers Market and Mercado
description: The Fellsmere Farmers Market & Mercado is an outdoor, park-setting market in downtown Fellsmere offering fresh locally grown produce, baked goods, plants and flowers, honey, and handmade artisan goods.

name: Flagler Beach Farmers' Market
description: Held at Wickline Park behind the library in Flagler Beach, the market features more than 30 vendors selling fresh in-season fruits and vegetables, prepared foods, and handmade products from local artisans and crafters.

name: Flamingo Road Nursery and Farmers Market
description: Flamingo Road Nursery is a 10-acre garden center in Davie that has operated for over 20 years in South Florida, billing itself as a place to 'Celebrate Life In The Tropics.' Alongside its plants and nursery stock it offers expert plant advice, custom arrangements, a gift shop, the on-site Flamingo Cafe, and seasonal events and workshops.

name: Four Corners Market
description: Open since 2009, Four Corners Market in Crystal River is an indoor market offering fresh, locally-sourced produce, artisanal goods, and unique items from local vendors.

name: Fresh Friday Night Farmers' Market & Art
Fresh Market at Wiregrass
description: Held at the Shops at Wiregrass on Paseo Drive in Wesley Chapel, this Tampa Bay Markets outdoor market features local vendors and goods on alternating Saturdays.

name: Green Cove Springs Farmers Market
season: 3rd Saturday of each month, 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM, September through June
description: Held on Walnut Street in front of Spring Park and City Hall in Green Cove Springs, this monthly Market in the Park features local vendors selling fresh produce and handmade goods plus live entertainment.

name: Growers' Market at Purple Martin Nurseries, Inc.
Gulfport Tuesday Fresh Market
description: The Gulfport Tuesday Fresh Market is a year-round open-air market along scenic Beach Blvd. S. in Gulfport, Florida. Over 50 vendors offer locally grown and organic produce, breads, cheese, seafood, Florida-raised meats, nuts, desserts, jams, herbs, local honey, pickles, olives and prepared foods.

name: "Health Happens" Farmers' Market
season: Thursdays and Fridays, 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM, year-round
description: The Marion County "Health Happens" Farmers' Market is held year-round at the McPherson Governmental Complex in Ocala, offering fresh local produce to the community.

name: Heart of Christmas Farmer's Market
description: Hosted at the USDA Certified Organic Heart of Christmas Farms in Christmas, Florida, this farmers' market limits vendors to those who grow or make their own products from local ingredients, offering eggs, honey, pet food, and poultry products.

name: Hunter's Creek Farmers Market
description: The Hunter's Creek Farmers Market in Orlando is a Saturday market featuring plants, specialty foods, and handcrafted artisan goods from local vendors.

name: Inverness Farmers Market
season: 1st and 3rd Saturdays; October–February 9:00 AM–2:00 PM, March–September 9:00 AM–1:00 PM
description: Held on the first and third Saturdays at the historic Depot in downtown Inverness, the market offers fresh produce, handmade goods from local artisans, and specialty foods such as baked goods and preserves.

name: Johnson's Family Flea and Farmers Market
Lake Wales Downtown Farmers Market
season: 2nd and 4th Saturday of the month, 9:00am-1:00pm (8:00am-12:00pm in July and August)
description: The Lake Wales Downtown Farmers Market offers locally grown pesticide-free produce and plants, baked goods, cheeses, local honey, homemade jams and jellies, BBQ sauces, and crafts in downtown Lake Wales.

name: Las Olas Outdoor Gourmet Market
description: The Las Olas Outdoor Gourmet Market is a weekly Sunday green market in the heart of downtown Fort Lauderdale, produced by The Market Company, Miami's green-event producer since 1997. Vendors offer locally grown produce, artisan breads and pasta, honey, juices, coffee and prepared foods in a relaxed, neighborly outdoor setting. It runs year-round and supports local producers and small-business entrepreneurs.

name: Lincoln Road Farmers Market
description: A weekly Miami Beach institution operating since 1997, the Lincoln Road Farmers Market is run by The Market Company and brings local produce and vendors to the pedestrian promenade.

name: Mandarin Farmers' Market by the Whole Foods Market
Marco Island Farmers' Market
season: November through mid-April, Wednesdays 7:30 a.m. - 1:00 p.m.
description: The Marco Island Farmers' Market is an open-air market held at Veterans' Community Park (Park Avenue & Elkcam Circle) operated by the City of Marco Island Parks. Vendors offer fresh produce, seafood, baked goods, local honey, sauces, soaps, chocolates and handcrafted jewelry. The market runs seasonally from November through mid-April.

name: Marion Street Farmers' Market
street: Duval Street and North Marion Ave

name: Market at the Depot in Inverness
season: First and third Saturdays; March–Sept 9am–1pm, Oct–Feb 9am–2pm
description: The Market at the Depot is a community market held at the Depot Pavilion, 300 N. Apopka Ave., in Inverness, Florida, run by the City of Inverness. Vendors offer fresh produce, baked goods, fresh seafood, jams and jellies, hot sauce, dried fruits, plants, and crafts from local artisans.

name: Mayfair Green Market
description: The Mayfair Green Market in Coconut Grove is a neighborhood green market produced by The Market Company, Miami's green-event producer since 1997. It brings together vendors offering locally grown produce and quality artisan products with a focus on community and outstanding service.

name: MidTown Farmers Market at Lake Ella
Mid-Week Madeira Beach Market
season: Wednesdays, November through end of May, 10:00 AM–2:00 PM
description: A seasonal makers market on Madeira Way featuring over 50 vendors with fresh produce, artisan goods, local foods, and live music in a beach-adjacent setting.

name: Normandy Village Marketplace
description: Normandy Village Marketplace in Miami Beach is a community green market produced by The Market Company, Miami's green-event producer since 1997. Vendors offer locally grown produce and quality artisan goods in a neighborly outdoor setting serving the Normandy Isle community.

name: Orlando Farmers Market
description: The Orlando Farmers Market is a dog-friendly market at Lake Eola Park in downtown Orlando that has supported community and arts programs each week since 1987. It is much more than an average farmers market, gathering local vendors offering produce, food and crafts. The market is held every Sunday from 10 a.m. to 3 p.m.

name: Orlando Farmers Market at Lake Eola
street: E. Central Boulevard and N. Eola Drive
description: Held every Sunday at Lake Eola Park in downtown Orlando, this dog-friendly market has featured local vendors and supported community and arts programs each week since 1987.

name: Palafox Market
street: N. Palafox St. and Wright St.

name: Plant City State Farmers' Market
street: 1305 West Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd

name: Red Hills Online Market
season: Year-round online ordering; order window Sunday to Tuesday, pickup/delivery Thursdays
description: Red Hills Online Market connects residents of Tallahassee and beyond with products grown or produced at farms within a 100-mile radius of Tallahassee. Customers order online during a weekly window and pick up fresh, locally sourced food and artisan products at designated hubs or via delivery on Thursdays. It is operated by the Red Hills Small Farm Alliance.

name: SaltAir Farmers Market
season: February through first Saturday in December; 1st and 3rd Saturdays, 9 a.m. - 1 p.m.
description: The SaltAir Farmers' Market is held in downtown Port St. Joe at the park area on the corner of US Highway 71 and Reid Avenue, where local farmers and artisans sell food and products they produce directly to consumers. Offerings include seasonal produce, plants, artwork and handmade crafts such as jewelry and pottery, and the market is dog-friendly. It runs from February through the first Saturday in December.

name: Santini Farmers Market Ft. Myers Beach
season: Sundays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays, 9:00am-1:00pm (October through May)
description: The Santini Farmers Market at Fort Myers Beach is a seasonal market on Estero Blvd run by Shop Local Productions, featuring artisans, fresh produce, and food vendors.

name: Seminole Heights Sunday Morning Market
description: The Seminole Heights Sunday Market is a community market held at the Guinn Center in the Seminole Heights neighborhood of Tampa, run by Tampa Bay Markets.

name: Spring Hill Farmers Market
description: The Spring Hill Farmers Market is an outdoor market at 3021 Commercial Way (Towne Square Mall parking lot) in Spring Hill, Florida, with over 40 vendors each week supporting local farms and small businesses.

name: Sumter County Farmers' Market
description: Established in 1937, the Sumter County Farmers' Market in Webster is the oldest and largest flea and farmers' market in Florida, spanning over 40 acres with free admission and parking.

name: Taste of the Gardens Green Market
description: The Gardens GreenMarket (Taste of the Gardens) is run by the City of Palm Beach Gardens at the City Hall Municipal Campus, 10500 North Military Trail, Palm Beach Gardens, Florida (northeast corner of Burns Road and Military Trail). The producer market offers locally grown produce and other goods, rain or shine.

name: The Green Marketplace at Adamson Road
Tioga Monday Farmers' Market
description: The Tioga Monday Market is a seasonal open-air market at Tioga Town Center, 13005 W Newberry Rd, in the Tioga community near Gainesville/Newberry, Florida. It offers local produce and goods from area vendors on Monday evenings.

name: Union Street Farmers' Market
description: The Union Street Farmers Market is a weekly downtown Gainesville market that has been a staple at Bo Diddley Plaza since 1996. Local vendors supply residents with fresh produce, baked goods and all-natural eats at affordable prices, rain or shine. It is held every Wednesday afternoon.

name: University of South Florida Farmer's Market
Volusia County Farm Bureau Farmers' Market
Wellington Green Market
description: The Wellington Green Market (Lakeside Market) is held at the Wellington Amphitheater, 12100 Forest Hill Blvd. in Wellington, Florida, on Thursday evenings in combination with the weekly Food Truck & Music Series. It features over 35 market vendors plus food trucks, live music and family-friendly entertainment.

name: Winter Haven Farmers' Market
street: Third Street NW and Avenue G NW

name: Ybor City Fresh Market
description: The Ybor City Saturday Market is the largest continually operating outdoor market in the Tampa Bay area, running since 2000 in Tampa's Historic District with local artists, makers, and gourmet food vendors.

name: Acworth Farmers Market
season: Fridays and Saturdays, 8:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m.; Fridays spring through fall and Saturdays late spring through fall, rain or shine.
description: The Acworth Farmers Market is held at Logan Farm Park in Historic Downtown Acworth, where area growers and vendors sell fresh produce and local goods on Friday and Saturday mornings.

name: Amicalola Regional Farmers Market
season: Every Saturday, 8:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. (or until sold out).
description: The Amicalola Regional Farmers Market is a regional market in Dawsonville offering locally grown produce and goods to the Dawson County community every Saturday morning.

name: Atlanta State Farmers' Market
season: Open year-round, 24 hours a day, 7 days a week (busiest in early morning, peak 4am-8am)
description: The Atlanta State Farmers Market is a 150-acre state-operated market in Forest Park, just south of Atlanta off I-75 (exit 237), and is one of the largest outdoor markets of its kind in the United States. It serves as a major wholesale and retail produce hub for the Southeast, with a garden center and seasonal Georgia Grown favorites such as peaches, Vidalia onions, and pumpkins. In autumn the market sells pumpkins and fall trimmings, and for winter it offers Christmas trees, wreaths, and garland. The on-site Oakwood Cafe serves meats, vegetables, BBQ, and desserts.

name: Augusta Market at the River
description: The Augusta Market is an open-air market along the Savannah River where area farmers, growers, and artisans sell fresh produce and merchandise, accompanied by live music as a weekly community gathering.

name: Bainbridge Farmers' Market
street: West St between Water & Broughton St

name: Barnesville Farmers' Market
season: Thursdays, noon – 6 p.m., May through October
description: The Barnesville Farmers Market is a seasonal open-air market with fresh fruits and vegetables in season, baked goods, and farm-fresh eggs, plus honey, Amish butter, cheese, popcorn, jams, and jellies.

name: Battlefield Farmers Market
season: Wednesdays 3:00 p.m. to 6:00 p.m. and Saturdays 8:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m., May through November.
description: The Battlefield Farmers Market in Rock Spring sells local and naturally grown farm products including fruits, vegetables, herbs, grass-fed beef, honey, jellies, and fresh baked goods in Walker County.

name: Canton Farmers Market
season: Every Saturday, 9:00 a.m. to 12:30 p.m., late May through August.
description: The Canton Farmers Market is held at Brown Park in Downtown Canton between the Cherokee County History Museum and the police department, where local vendors sell fresh produce and goods on Saturday mornings.

name: Cartersville Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 8:00 a.m. to noon, May through September.
description: The Cartersville Farmers Market is a community market in Downtown Cartersville providing customers a direct connection to vendors selling locally sourced food and agricultural products.

name: Cherokee Market Farm Fresh Produce & Antiques
Cochran Farmers Market
description: The Cochran Farmers Market is held on the Courthouse Lawn in downtown Cochran at 112 N 2nd Street, a seasonal market for local growers and vendors.

name: Common Ground Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 8:30 a.m. – 1:00 p.m., year-round (closed Thanksgiving, Christmas, New Year's Day, and the Saturday of July 4th weekend)
description: Common Ground Farmers Market runs every Saturday year-round, rain or shine, at 7741 Roswell Road in Atlanta, alongside a Sunday Makers & Designer Market.

name: Community Market of Rabun County
season: April through end of October, Saturdays 9am-1pm
description: The Community Market of Rabun County is a producer-and-artisan market in the Rabun County Pavilions at 201 West Savannah Street in Clayton, behind the Rabun County Civic Center and Library. It features local farmers selling produce alongside artists and creators, and runs a Power of Produce Kids Club with weekly activities for children ages 4-12. Special events include chef demonstrations, free sampling, and live music.

name: Decatur Saturday Farmers' Market
season: Wednesdays, 4:00 p.m. to 7:00 p.m., late March through mid-November, rain or shine.
description: The Decatur Farmers Market is a cornerstone of the local independent movement in Decatur, run by Community Farmers Markets and bringing neighbors together around locally grown food.

name: DeKalb Farmers Market
season: Open daily, 9:00 a.m. to 9:00 p.m., year-round (closed Thanksgiving and Christmas Day).
description: Your DeKalb Farmers Market is a large year-round indoor world market in Decatur featuring fresh produce, organic items, international products, and bakery, deli, seafood, and floral departments.

name: Downtown Alpharetta Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 8:30 a.m. to 12:30 p.m., April through mid-November.
description: The Downtown Alpharetta Farmers Market is held around the Town Green in front of Alpharetta City Center, hosting 100+ vendors selling local produce and goods on Saturday mornings.

name: Downtown Commerce Farmers Market
description: The Downtown Commerce Farmers Market is held at Spencer Park in downtown Commerce, at S. Elm Street and Pine Street. Vendors sell fresh local produce and goods, and accept cash, credit, and debit. The market is organized locally and operates on Saturday mornings.

name: Downtown Sandersville Market
season: First weekend in March through second weekend in December, 8:00am-11:30am

name: Dunwoody Farmers Market
description: The Dunwoody Farmers Market at Brook Run Park is one of North Atlanta's most convenient stops for fresh, local, and pesticide-free produce, meats, seafood, eggs, honey, baked goods, sauces, spices, and pet treats.

name: East Lake Farmers Market
street: Hosea L. Williams Dr SE & 2nd Ave SE

name: East Metro Farmers Market at Stonecrest
Ellijay Farmers Market
description: The Ellijay Farmers & Artisans Market is held at 1 Broad Street in downtown Ellijay, between the courthouse and Watkins Hall. North Georgia mountain farmers and craftspeople sell home-grown and organic fruits and vegetables, baked and canned goods, flowers, plants, and handmade crafts including soaps, woodworking, pottery, and etched glassware.

name: Fall Line Farmers Market on First Fridays
Forsyth Farmers' Market
street: W. Park Ave. and Bull St.
season: Every Saturday, 9:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m., year-round, rain or shine.
description: The Forsyth Farmers' Market is held every Saturday in Forsyth Park in Savannah, offering fresh, affordable produce, meats, and locally made foods from farmers and producers across the Coastal Empire and Low Country.

name: Freedom Farmers' Market at the Carter
description: Freedom Farmers Market at the Carter Center was formed in 2014 by a small group of Georgia farmers to bring local, sustainably grown food to intown Atlanta, featuring organic produce, grass-fed meats, dairy, eggs, and baked goods each Saturday.

name: Fresh off the Farm Farmer's Market
Fresh South Market
season: Wednesdays and Saturdays, 8:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m.
description: Fresh South Market in Peachtree City creates an outlet for small farmers to sell locally grown produce and food products directly to consumers, including USDA certified organic products.

name: Gainesville Friday Market on the Square
GHA Farmers Market
description: The GHA Farmers Market is held at Gilliland Heritage Alpaca Farm in Hoschton every Sunday afternoon, offering locally grown products to the community.

name: Hall County Farmers Market
description: The Hall County Farmers Market, operating since 1973, is located at 734 E Crescent Drive in Gainesville. Vendors sell fresh produce (tomatoes, okra, cucumbers, beans, squash, peppers, eggs), baked goods, honey, and handcrafted artisanal items. The market accepts SNAP/EBT with matching programs.

name: Hinesville Farmers Market
season: Thursdays, 3:00 p.m. to 7:00 p.m., May through October.
description: The Hinesville Downtown Farmers Market is held at Bradwell Park on Commerce Street, offering locally grown vegetables and fruits, jams, fresh breads, and arts and crafts on Thursday afternoons.

name: Jonesboro Farmers Market
season: Wednesdays 10:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m. and Saturdays 8:00 a.m. to 12:00 p.m., May through October.
description: The Jonesboro Farmers and Artisans Market is held at Lee Street Park in Jonesboro, where local farmers and artisans sell fresh produce and handmade goods on Wednesdays and Saturdays.

name: Lawrenceville Farmers' Market
description: The Lawrenceville Farmers Market is held on the square across from Lawrenceville's historic courthouse, where vendors sell local produce, grass-fed beef, local milk, honey, baked goods, and crafts.

name: Lenny's Farmers Market
description: Lenny's Farmers Market is held at 110 N. Hampton Street in downtown Americus. Vendors sell produce, honey, farm eggs, baked goods, jams and jellies, meat products (pork, bacon, sausage), plants and flowers, and handmade items such as soaps and cutting boards.

name: Main Street Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 9:00 a.m. – 12:30 p.m., April through November
description: The Main Street Farmers Market is held Saturdays at 222 S. Main Street on Statesboro's Blue Mile, offering produce, meat, cheese, and eggs from local family farms along with handcrafted items, live music, and food.

name: Marietta Square Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 9:00 a.m. – 1:00 p.m., year-round; Sundays, 12:00 p.m. – 3:00 p.m., May through October
description: The Marietta Square Farmers Market gathers an average of over 65 vendors at the Mill Street Parking Lot (41 Mill St.), offering fresh, locally grown seasonal produce and garden products.

name: Market on the Square
season: Saturdays, 8 a.m. – 12 noon, April through October
description: Pike County's Market on the Square is held in a shady pecan grove on the south side of Zebulon's courthouse square, offering fresh local produce, yard eggs, baked goods, cut flowers, nursery plants, homemade soaps, and heirloom crafts.

name: Metro Market Atlanta
season: Fridays 12:00 p.m. – 6:00 p.m., Saturdays 10:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m., Sundays 12:00 p.m. – 5:00 p.m.
description: Metro Market Atlanta is an open-air market at 2133 Piedmont Road in Atlanta operating Friday through Sunday with local vendors and fresh goods.

name: Milledgeville Marketplace Farmers' Market
description: The Milledgeville Marketplace Farmers' Market is held at 222 East Hancock Street in downtown Milledgeville. Vendors sell primarily Georgia Made / Georgia Grown items including fruits, vegetables, peanuts, plants, flowers, honey, baked goods, beverages, beef, seafood, and arts and crafts.

name: Milton GA Farmers Market
description: The Milton Farmers Market is held on the Green at Crabapple Market in downtown Crabapple, opposite City Hall, bringing local vendors and fresh produce to the community.

name: Nam Dae Mun Farmers Market at Cobb International
Northside Farmers' Market
season: Summer market April through October, 4 p.m. – 7 p.m.; Winter market November through March, 4 p.m. – 6 p.m.
description: The Northside Farmers Market serves its Atlanta community year-round with adjusted seasonal hours, focused on increasing food access, building community, and supporting small farmers and vendors.

name: Oconee Farmers Market
description: The Oconee Farmers Market is held at Wire Park in Watkinsville. Vendors sell fresh local produce, artisanal goods, and items from local farmers and food producers. The market runs a Frequent Shopper Program offering market tokens and monthly prize drawings.

name: Old Fourth Ward Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, 9 a.m. – 1 p.m., late May through September
description: The Old Fourth Ward Farmers Market is a seasonal open-air market at 621 North Ave NE in Atlanta featuring Georgia lamb, eggs, produce, kombucha, and peaches.

name: Paulding County Farm Bureau Farmers Market
Peachtree Road Farmers Market
description: The Peachtree Road Farmers Market is a producer-only market held in the grounds of the Cathedral of St. Philip at 2744 Peachtree Road NW in Atlanta. All items are sold directly by the farmer or maker, with no reselling, including fresh vegetables, meats, dairy, and prepared foods from 70+ local farmers and artisans. The market features live music, a kids play area, and twice-monthly storytime, and doubles SNAP benefits at each visit.

name: Perry Farmers Market
description: The Perry Farmers Market is held at 901 Carroll Street in historic downtown Perry and operates year-round. Vendors offer fresh produce, baked goods, crafts, and other local products from regional producers. It is operated in association with Land to Hand.

name: Sandy Springs Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 8:30 a.m. – 12:00 p.m., April through November, rain or shine
description: The Sandy Springs Farmers Market is held Saturday mornings at City Springs, where a unique selection of vendors set up an open-air market offering fresh produce and artisan foods from local farmers and makers.

name: Sandy Springs Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 8:30 a.m. – 12:00 p.m., April through November, rain or shine
description: The Sandy Springs Farmers Market is held Saturday mornings at City Springs, where a unique selection of vendors set up an open-air market offering fresh produce and artisan foods from local farmers and makers.

name: Smyrna Fresh Produce Market
description: The Smyrna Fresh Produce Market is a city-organized open-air market held in the parking lot adjacent to Smyrna Market Village and the First Baptist Church at 1275 Church Street SE. Vendors sell fresh produce, baked goods, salsa, honey, and fresh eggs, alongside items such as picnic tables and birdhouses.

name: Snellville Farmers Market
season: June 6 - September 26, Saturdays 8:30 AM - 12:30 PM (no market July 4)
description: The Snellville Farmers' Market is a producer-only market offering produce, meats, seafood, eggs, honey, baked goods, pizza, ethnic cuisine, soups, and artisan items. Administered by a group of dedicated local residents without city funding, its mission is to nourish the community by providing fresh, local, and seasonal produce. The market is held at the Snellville Towne Green.

name: Square Market of Covington
season: Fridays, 4 p.m. – dusk, April through October
description: The Square Market of Covington is a seasonal open-air market in downtown Covington offering locally produced and hand-crafted products from area vendors.

name: St. Marys Community Market
street: Osborne St. and St. Marys St.

name: Stewards of the Earth Farmers Market
Stone Mountain Farmers Market
description: The Stone Mountain Farmers Market is held in the Municipal Parking Lot at 922 Main Street in Stone Mountain. Vendors sell fresh fruits and vegetables, local honey, goat cheese, and locally made soaps, and the market accepts SNAP/EBT benefits.

name: Swainsboro Emanuel Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 8 a.m. – 2 p.m., June through October
description: The Swainsboro Emanuel Farmers Market is held on South Green Street in Swainsboro and offers local produce, honey, baked goods, elderberry, and arts and crafts, with indoor space available.

name: The Dallas Farmers' Market
description: The Dallas Farmers' Market is held on the Courthouse Square at 25 Courthouse Sq. in downtown Dallas, Georgia. Vendors sell locally grown fruits and vegetables (greens, radishes, carrots, tomatoes, okra, corn, peppers, pumpkins, melons) plus specialty items such as eggs, honey, soaps, dried herbs, and live plants.

name: Tifton Downtown Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays 9am-noon; spring/summer April-July and fall October-November
description: The Tifton Farmers Market is held in Tifton Gardens along 1st Street Extension, next to the Train Depot in downtown Tifton. Vendors sell produce, baked goods, local honey, grass-fed beef and pork, seafood, eggs, jams and jellies, and local crafts.

name: Trenton Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, 9 a.m. – Noon, May through October
description: The Trenton Farmers Market is held at Veterans Park across from the Old Dade County Courthouse in downtown Trenton, on US Hwy. 11.

name: UMOJA Farmers' Market
description: UMOJA Farmers' Market is an outdoor market run by the Georgia Citizens Coalition on Hunger that brings affordable fresh fruits and vegetables directly into Atlanta communities.

name: Village Farmers' Market at Life University
Wayne County Farmers Market
season: Fridays and Saturdays, 10 a.m. – 2 p.m., April through November
description: The Wayne County Farmers Market in Jesup offers a variety of fresh produce, baked goods, crafts, and other items from local vendors at 533 North 1st Street.

name: Whistle Stop Farmers Market
season: Tuesdays, 4:00 p.m. – 8:00 p.m., June through October
description: The Whistle Stop Farmers Market is held at Thrasher Park in downtown Norcross, offering seasonal locally grown fruits and vegetables, fresh local eggs, baked goods, and preserves, with live music and kids' activities.

name: Woodstock Farm Fresh Market
description: The Woodstock Farm Fresh Market is held on Market Street and in Reformation Brewery's Backyard in downtown Woodstock. It is a producer market where vendors must grow at least 85% of what they sell, offering locally grown produce, jams and jellies, honey, locally raised meats, Georgia coast seafood, fresh baked bread and pastries, and bath and beauty products.

name: Kaiser Permanente's Fresh Day Farmers' Market
Keauhou Farmers Market
description: At the Keauhou Farmers Market you will find locally raised produce, honey, flowers, Kona coffee, macadamia nuts, meat and eggs, handmade breads, jams, and much more, all 100% grown and produced in Hawaii. The market is held at the Keauhou Shopping Center in Kailua-Kona every Saturday morning.

name: Waimea Town Market at Parker School
Bonners Ferry Farmers' Market
description: The beautiful Bonners Ferry area is scenic and in the summer many many tourists stop through the market for a taste of local color. We have grown from a few hardy souls selling produce and bare-root plants to an average of nearly 30 vendors per market day with everything from salad mix, herbal vinegar and spices to handmade soaps, and other crafts. In 2004 we added a Holiday Market in November showcasing holiday gifts, winter produce, and local artisans. This was such a success we have added it to our yearly calendar.  Join us Saturdays from 8:00 am - 1:00 pm the last Saturday in Apr

name: Farmers Market @ Liberty Square in Ammon
Farmers' Market at Sandpoint
description: The Farmers' Market at Sandpoint's mission is to support local agriculture through education and a diverse community market. We have been a Farmers' Market in Sandpoint, Idaho for 31 years. Supporting local agriculture through education and a diverse community Market. Treat yourself and family to locally grown fresh produce, baked goods, and handcrafted arts and crafts. Live Music at Every Market. 100% Locally Grown & Made. Join us Saturdays from 9:00 am - 1:00 pm and Wednesdays from 3:00 pm - 5:30 pm from May through October!

name: Henry's Fork Farmers' Market in Ashton
Hope Farmers Market
city: 415 Wellington Place - Hope Memorial Community Center Hope

name: Meridian Farmers' Market and Bazaar
city: 1200 N. Eagle Rd. (Meridian Crossroads Shopping Ctr) Meridian

name: North End Organic Nursery Farmers' Market
Wood River Farmers Market Association - Hailey/Ketchum
Abundant Market - Tuscola's Downtown Farmers' Market
Aledo Main Street Farmer's Market & Bake Sale
Alton Farmers' & Artisans' Market
description: The Alton Farmers' & Artisans' Market is held in the parking lot at the corner of Landmarks Blvd. and Henry Street in downtown Alton, Illinois. Organized by Alton Main Street, the Saturday-morning market is part of the organization's effort to revitalize the Downtown Alton historic district. Live entertainment, artist demonstrations, and other special activities are scheduled most weeks.

name: Antioch's Farmers Market
description: Antioch's Farmers Market is the Village of Antioch's seasonal market held at Sequoit Creek Park near Main Street (Route 83) in Antioch, Illinois. The Saturday-morning market offers fresh produce alongside rotating food trucks and live entertainment as part of a combined weekly community event.

name: Arlington Heights Farmers Market
description: The Arlington Heights Farmers Market is held Saturday mornings at 110 W Fremont St in downtown Arlington Heights, featuring fresh produce, hot food, live music, and family activities. It is managed by the Arlington Heights Historical Society and was voted best farmers market in Illinois by American Farmland Trust.

name: Chicago Nettlehorst French Market
street: N Broadway Street at W. Melrose Street
description: The Nettelhorst French Market is a Bensidoun-operated farmers market at 3252 N Broadway in Chicago's Lakeview neighborhood, billed as Lakeview's premiere farmers market.

name: City of La Salle Farmers Market
Clinton Area Farmers' & Artisans' Market
street: 100 S. Center St., Mr. Lincon's Square

name: Crete European Market
street: Main St. and W. Exchange St.

name: Daley Plaza Farmers Market
description: The Daley Plaza Farmers Market is Chicago's longest-running farmers market, held Thursdays at 50 W Washington St, selling fresh seasonal produce, flowers, prepared foods, and Chicago-made products.

name: Deerfield Farmers Market
street: Park Avenue between Jewett Park Drive and Hazel Avenue
description: The Deerfield Farmers Market is held on Park Avenue between Jewett Park Drive and Hazel Avenue in Deerfield, Illinois. The Saturday-morning market offers locally grown seasonal vegetables and fruit, organic and naturally grown produce and flowers, fresh-cut flowers and potted plants, freshly baked breads and pastries, hand-crafted Wisconsin cheese, maple syrup, honey, and other specialty products.

name: Discover Sycamore Farmer's Market
description: The Discover Sycamore Farmers' Market is a weekly community market held on the front lawn of the DeKalb County History Center (1730 N. Main St.) in Sycamore, featuring local vendors with fresh produce, handmade goods, baked items, and live music.

name: Division Street Farmers' Market
description: The Division Street Farmers' Market is a City of Chicago-operated neighborhood market near Division Street on the Near North Side, around 50–100 W. Division St. The Saturday-morning market sells fresh produce and local goods and accepts SNAP/Link benefits. For the 2026 season it relocated about two blocks west of its original spot due to the Division Street Streetscape Project.

name: Downtown Paris Farmers' Market
street: N. Central Ave. and W. Wood St.

name: DuQuoin Farmers' Market
description: The DuQuoin Farmers' Market is held on the Du Quoin State Fairgrounds in Du Quoin, where local vendors sell fresh produce and homegrown goods during the season.

name: Edgebrook Farmers Market
description: The Edgebrook Farmers Market is hosted by the Edgebrook shopping district at N. Alpine Road in Rockford, Illinois. Local farmers and producers offer free-range meats, vegetables, fruits, flowers and plants, fresh baked breads and sweet treats, honey, farm-fresh eggs, jams and jellies, and more. Around two dozen vendors participate each week.

name: Edgewater Farmers Market
description: The Edgewater Farmers Market is billed as Chicago's only Monday farmers market, held at the corner of Broadway and Thorndale, with over 30 farmers and vendors offering fresh produce, baked goods, meats, prepared meals, and live music.

name: Eli's Cheesecake / Wright College Farmers Market
Elmhurst Farmers Market
