description: The Elmhurst Farmers Market is a Wednesday-morning market in Elmhurst, Illinois. Previously located near York and Vallette Streets, it now operates in the parking lot of the Elmhurst Christian Reformed Church at 149 W. Brush Hill Road, where local vendors sell produce and other goods.

name: Evergreen Park Farmers Market
description: The Evergreen Park Farmers Market is held at Yukich Fields near 89th Street and Kedzie Avenue in Evergreen Park, Illinois. Now in its 21st year, the Thursday market features a variety of vendors offering produce, floral items, food, crafts, and treats.

name: Faith in Place Winter Farmers Markets - Chicago Area
Farmers' Market at Country Fair
description: The Farmers' Market at Country Fair is a producer-only market at the Country Fair Shopping Center (229 S. Mattis Avenue) in Champaign, where everything sold is grown or made by the seller.

name: Frankfort Country Market
description: The Frankfort Country Market is a Sunday open-air market in historic downtown Frankfort, Illinois, drawing nearly 50 vendors selling produce, cut flowers, plants, honey, meat, poultry, seafood, dairy, baked goods, and prepared hot foods. Organized by the Village of Frankfort, it runs as a spring and summer market in the downtown area each season.

name: Freeport Farmers Market
description: The Freeport Farmers Market is held in Memorial Park (19 Bow Street) in Freeport, offering locally grown produce, meats, flowers, baked goods, and homemade products such as soaps and candles.

name: Galena Winter Farmers Market
season: 2nd Saturday of each month, 8am-Noon, November through April
description: The Galena Winter Farmers Market moves indoors to the Eagle Club (237 S. Main St.) on the second Saturday of each month, offering vegetables, fruits, crafts, and organic foods during the cold-weather months.

name: Geneva French Market
season: Sundays, 9:00am-2:00pm, April through October (reduced hours in November)
description: The Geneva French Market is a Bensidoun-operated European-style market held Sundays in the Metra train station parking lot at 4th & South Streets in Geneva, offering food, treats, and merchandise.

name: Glen Ellyn Farmers Market
description: The Glen Ellyn French Market is a Bensidoun-operated market held Sundays in the Main Street Station parking lot at 449 Main St in Glen Ellyn, featuring dozens of vendors offering fresh flowers, produce, artisanal honey, and handmade goods.

name: Grayslake Farmers Market
description: The Grayslake Farmers Market is Lake County's longest-running and largest evening market, located in historic downtown Grayslake on Center Street. Operated by a nonprofit, it fosters a sense of community through a weekly three-season outdoor shopping experience featuring farm-fresh and prepared foods, arts and crafts, music, and children's activities.

name: Highwoods Evening Market
description: The Highwood Evening Gourmet Market, organized by Celebrate Highwood, is a Wednesday-evening market held at Everts Park (111 North Ave.) in Highwood, Illinois. With over 60 vendors each week, it offers local artisan crafts and wares plus food and drinks, with free live entertainment, drawing crowds for an outdoor community gathering.

name: Horner Park Farmers Market
description: The Horner Park Farmers Market is held at Horner Park, 2741 W. Montrose Ave., in Chicago, Illinois. The community market features local farmers and food producers selling fresh produce and other goods.

name: Illinois Products Farmers' Market - Illinois Department of Agriculture
IMAN Health and Wellness Farmers Market
Jacksonville Farmers Market at Lincoln Square
season: Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Saturdays, 7am-Noon, May through October
description: The Jacksonville Farmers Market at Lincoln Square is a seasonal open-air market at the Lincoln Square Shopping Center (901 W. Morton Avenue) in Jacksonville, offering home-grown fruits, vegetables, and baked goods.

name: Jacksonville Main Street Farmers Market on the Square
Jefferson Park Sunday Market
description: The Jefferson Park Sunday Market is a free neighborhood farmers market in Chicago's Jefferson Park, brought back to the neighborhood in summer 2011 by the JPNA, the Copernicus Foundation, and the Chamber of Commerce. Vendors sell produce, meats, baked goods, cheese, specialty sauces and jams, handmade body products, crafts, dog treats, and prepared ready-to-eat food. The summer market runs on the 2nd and 4th Sunday of the month, June through October, near Long and Lawrence Avenues in Jefferson Memorial Park.

name: Jerseyville Farmers and Artisan Market
season: Tuesdays 4:00-7:00 PM, June 2 - September 1, 2026 (no market July 14)
description: The Jersey County Farmers & Artisans Market is a showcase of locally grown produce and locally made artisan goods in Jerseyville, with about 20 vendors offering fresh produce, baked goods, crafts, handmade items, and ready-to-eat food. All items must be handmade, homemade, or homegrown by the person, family, or cooperative operating the booth. It is held at the Jerseyville City Center Plaza on State Street, between Dairy Queen and Germania Brew Haus.

name: Joliet City Center Farmers' Market
street: W. Van Buren Street at N. Chicago Street

name: Junction City Farmers' Market
season: Wednesdays, 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM, late May through September (weather permitting)
description: An outdoor farmers' market held at the Junction City Shopping Center in north Peoria, offering farm-fresh produce, baked goods, specialty foods, and handmade art locally produced in Illinois.

name: Lake Bluff Farmers' Market
street: E. Center Ave. & W. Scranton Ave.
description: The Lake Bluff Farmers' Market is held on the Village Green at E. Center Ave. and W. Scranton Ave. in Lake Bluff, Illinois. The Friday-morning market features local vendors along with live music and children's entertainment, and has run for over three decades.

name: Lake Forest Open Air Market
season: Saturdays, 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM, June through October
description: The Lake Forest Open Air Market is held Saturday mornings at the East Lake Forest Metra station parking lot, with vendors offering locally grown produce, artisan cheeses, fresh-baked breads and other regional goods.

name: Land of Goshen Farmers' Market
street: St. Louis St. & N. Main St.

name: LaSalle Farmers Market
season: Tuesdays, 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM, June through September
description: The La Salle Farmers Market is held on Tuesday afternoons at the northwest corner of Hegeler Park (Route 351 and Maple Road) in La Salle, Illinois, bringing local growers and makers together for the community.

name: Lewistown Farmers Market
season: Tuesdays 3:00 PM - 7:00 PM and Saturdays 8:00 AM - 12:00 PM, May through August
description: The Lewistown Farmers Market sets up in downtown Porter Park, across from the Fulton County Court House, offering locally grown produce and goods on Tuesday afternoons and Saturday mornings.

name: Libertyville's Main Street Farmers' Market
products: Fresh fruits; Fresh vegetables; Baked goods; Bedding plants; Canned or preserved fruits/vegetables; Coffee/tea; Cut flowers; Dairy products.; Eggs; Fish/seafood; Fresh/dried herbs; Honey; Juices/non-alcoholic ciders; Mushrooms; Nuts; Pet food; Poultry/fowl meat and products; Prepared foods; Red/other non-poultry meat and products; Soap/body care products
description: We are the oldest farmers market in Lake County, Illinois. We are a traditional farmers market that only sells food products grown or made by the vendor, and we have great vendors with a very wide variety of products.

name: Lincoln Square Farmers Market
description: The Lincoln Square Farmers Market features over 50 vendors each week, connecting local farmers, artisans, and food purveyors to the Northside of Chicago. Held in the parking lot at W Leland Ave and N Lincoln Ave, it runs a Tuesday morning market and a Thursday afternoon market through the warm-weather season.

name: Loyola Farmers Market
season: Mondays, 4:00 PM - 8:00 PM, June through October
description: The Loyola Farmers Market is a seasonal open-air market in Chicago's Rogers Park neighborhood near Loyola University Chicago at 6590 N. Sheridan Road, offering fresh fruits, vegetables and other local goods.

name: Main Street Farmers Market Downtown Aledo
Main Street Farmers' Market of Olney
Main Street Paris Farmers' Market
description: The Main Street Paris Farmers' Market is held on the south side of the courthouse square at 222 N. Main St. in Paris, Illinois, gathering local growers and makers offering seasonal vegetables, flowers, meats and crafts.

name: Marengo Friday Farmers Market
street: W Washington Street at S. Ann Street

name: Marion Farmers Market
season: Wednesdays 7:00 AM - 11:00 AM and Saturdays 6:00 AM - 12:00 PM, April through October
description: The Marion Farmers Market is a long-running open-air market at 507 W. Main Street in Marion, Illinois, where vendors sell items such as sweet corn, tomatoes, locally made jams, soaps and crafts.

name: Maxwell Street Market
season: Select Sundays, 10:00 AM - 3:00 PM, May through October
description: The historic Maxwell Street Market is a free, open-air market on Maxwell Street between S. Halsted St. and S. Union Ave. in Chicago, featuring an eclectic mix of foods, handmade crafts, resale housewares, clothing and more.

name: McHenry Farmers' Market
description: The McHenry Farmers' Market is held at Veterans Memorial Park, 3400 Pearl Street, in McHenry, Illinois. The Thursday-evening market offers plants, produce, frozen beef, and baked goods from local vendors, with crafters and artisans displaying their work; live music and rotating food trucks round out the evening.

name: Mendota Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 8:00 AM - 12:00 PM, June through October
description: The Mendota Farmers' Market is held in the 700 block of Main Street in downtown Mendota, Illinois, featuring locally grown produce, arts and crafts, and seasonal goods.

name: Midtown Farmers' Market
description: The Rockford Midtown Market (Midtown Farmers' Market) is a community gathering in the heart of Rockford's Midtown District, a partnership between Katie's Cup and the Midtown District. It features ethnic food, farm produce, coffee drinks, kid activities, music, and crafts.

name: Momence Farmers' Market
description: The Momence Farmers' Market is sponsored by the City of Momence and held at 123 W. River St. in Momence, Illinois. The Wednesday-afternoon market offers locally sourced products including produce, baked goods, eggs, and flowers.

name: Monroe County Farmer's Market - Columbia
season: Thursdays, 3:00 PM - 7:00 PM, late April through October
description: The Monroe County Farmers Market's Columbia location is held on Thursday afternoons at 229 N. Main St. in Columbia, Illinois, as part of the vendor-run nonprofit Monroe County Farmers Market.

name: Monroe County Farmer's Market - Waterloo
season: Saturdays, 7:30 AM - 12:00 PM, late April through October
description: The Monroe County Farmers Market's Waterloo location is a vendor-run nonprofit market held Saturday mornings at 901 Illinois Ave. in Waterloo, Illinois, managed entirely by its volunteer vendors.

name: Morton Farmers Market
season: Tuesdays, 3:30 PM - 6:30 PM, June through early September
description: A weekly summer farmers market held at Church Square (225 E. Jefferson St.) in downtown Morton, organized by the Village of Morton.

name: Mt. Carmel Farmers Market
description: The Wabash County / Mt. Carmel Farmers Market has a vision to renew an age-old tradition and support the local economy while strengthening the community. Held in downtown Mount Carmel, it offers fresh local produce and goods every Saturday during the season.

name: Murphysboro Farmers Market
season: Wednesdays, 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM, April through August
description: The Murphysboro Farmers Market is held Wednesday afternoons at the 17th Street Barbecue cook-off site, two blocks north of Walnut Street in Murphysboro, Illinois, offering produce, crafts and vintage home goods along with live music.

name: Museum of Contemporary Art/Streeterville (Chicago) Farmers' Market
Naperville Farmers Market
description: The Naperville Farmers Market is held at 200 East 5th Avenue near the 5th Avenue Train Station in downtown Naperville, offering locally grown food along with products, performances, and family-friendly events.

name: NorthCenter Farmers Market
description: The Northcenter Farmers Market is one of the oldest markets on the North Side of Chicago. Every Saturday, farmers and local merchants set up to offer their freshest fruits, vegetables, flowers, pastries, and other handmade products at Northcenter Town Square on N Damen Ave.

name: Oak Forest Farmers' Market
description: The Oak Forest Farmers' Market is a Saturday-morning market in Oak Forest, Illinois, run in conjunction with the Oak Forest Park District. Local vendors offer produce and other goods through the spring-to-fall season.

name: Old Capitol Farmers Market Holiday/Winter Farmers Market
season: Summer market May–October, Wednesdays & Saturdays 8am–12:30pm; Winter market Nov–April, Saturdays 8am–12pm at Union Station
description: The Old Capitol Farmers Market is Springfield's longest-running market, held in downtown Springfield, Illinois, on Adams Street between 4th and 5th Streets near the Old State Capitol. Now in its 27th season, it operates Wednesdays and Saturdays in the warm months; during the colder months the Winter Old Capitol Farmers Market moves indoors to historic Springfield Union Station.

name: Old Dundee Sunday Farmers' Market
description: The Old Dundee Sunday Farmers' Market is a seasonal, community-centered market held in the municipal parking lots at the corner of Van Nest and Ypsilanti Streets in East Dundee, IL. It gathers local farmers and artisans selling fresh produce and locally made goods, with flat parking including handicapped spaces.

name: Orangeville Farmers Market
street: Ewing Street at W High Road

name: Oregon Park District Farmers Market
description: The Oregon Park District Farmers Market is held on the Ogle County Courthouse lawn at the corner of Route 64 and Route 2 in Oregon, IL. Vendors offer locally grown vegetables and fruits along with baked goods, breads, honey, maple syrup, popcorn, soaps and other handmade items, and many evenings feature live entertainment and food vendors.

name: Palatine Winter Farmers' Market
season: 1st and 3rd Saturdays, 9:00 AM - 12:00 PM (Noon), November through April
description: A winter farmers market held in the Palatine Train Station parking lot (137 W. Wood St.), featuring over 20 vendors offering local produce and goods during the off-season.

name: Palos Heights' Farmers' Market
season: Wednesdays 8:00am-1:00pm, May through mid-October, rain or shine
description: The Palos Heights Farmers Market is an outdoor market held at the Municipal Parking Lot at 12217 S. Harlem Avenue in Palos Heights, offering fresh produce, baked goods, and other local products. It runs every Wednesday, rain or shine, through the spring and summer season.

name: Plant Chicago Farmers Market
season: Outdoor: 2nd Saturday 11am–3pm (warmer months); Indoor (Feb–Apr): 2nd Saturday 10am–2pm at Southside Social
description: Plant Chicago Farmers Market is a community market at 4459 S. Marshfield Ave in Chicago's Back of the Yards neighborhood, hosted by the Plant Chicago nonprofit. It features fresh local produce, skillshares, a free clothing store, live music and cooking demonstrations, and runs a Link-matching program that doubles SNAP benefits up to $25.

name: Polo Chamber of Commerce Farmers' Market
Printers Row (Chicago) Farmers' Market
description: The Printers Row Farmers Market (South Loop Farmers Market) is held Saturdays at 632 S Dearborn St in Chicago's South Loop / Printers Row neighborhood, offering fresh seasonal produce and local goods.

name: Pullman Farmers' Market
description: The Pullman Farmers' Market is a seasonal market held at 111th St and Cottage Grove Ave in Arcade Park on Chicago's historic Pullman district. The market brings together more than 70 vendors selling fresh fruits, vegetables, plants and flowers, and includes indoor space.

name: Quad Cities Growers' Markets
street: Avenue of the Cities and 11th St.

name: Ravinia Farmers Market
description: The Ravinia Farmers Market is a community-focused market in Highland Park, IL, now in its 48th season, connecting local growers, artisans and food makers with shoppers. Located at 486 Roger Williams Ave, it sells fresh foods, handmade goods and products from local vendors, and also runs winter indoor markets in Highland Park.

name: Rockford City Market
street: E. State St. and N. Water St.

name: Sainte Marie Farmers Market
description: The Sainte Marie Farmers Market is held at Picquet Park (200 South Franklin Street) in Sainte Marie, where vendors and shoppers travel from miles around to buy and sell fresh fruits, vegetables, crafts, and other foods. The market is organized in connection with the Sainte Marie Foundation, a nonprofit dedicated to preserving and improving the quality of life in the community.

name: SOAR Farmers Market @ MCA
description: The SOAR Farmers Market at the MCA brings fresh produce, baked goods, gorgeous flowers, and more to the Museum of Contemporary Art plaza in Chicago's Streeterville neighborhood, run by the Streeterville Organization of Active Residents (SOAR). Shoppers can meet friends and grab lunch from vendors on the MCA Plaza.

name: Stade's Farm & Market
season: Open daily 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM, late May through November (through Halloween); pick-your-own begins mid-June
description: A full-service family farm and country market on 2,200 acres in McHenry County, offering homegrown fruits and vegetables, bakery items, pick-your-own harvesting, and seasonal family attractions including a petting zoo and corn maze.

name: Star Farm / Back of the Yards Farmers Market
description: Star Farm Chicago is a non-profit urban farm and landscaping service established in 2016 in the Back of the Yards neighborhood. It increases access to locally grown organic produce through farmers markets, pop-ups, delivery, and a local foods co-op, with a year-round CSA delivered on Wednesday afternoons.

name: Streator Downtown Farmers Market
products: Fresh vegetables; Baked goods; Bedding plants; Canned or preserved fruits/vegetables; Crafts/woodworking items; Cut flowers; Fresh/dried herbs; Honey; Pet food; Soap/body care products
description: We have Artisan breads, candy , canned goods, crafts, and baked goods

name: Sycamore Farmers' Market
description: The Sycamore Farmers' Market is held on the front lawn of the DeKalb County History Center in Sycamore, IL. Visitors can shop a rotating selection of local vendors offering fresh produce, flowers, baked goods, honey, meats, specialty foods, handmade art and crafts, with music and community activities.

name: Twice's Belmont Cragin Farmers Market
description: Kelly Rauch LLC dba Twice is a local farmers market located in Chicago, IL in Cook County. A community farmers market supporting local growers and producers.

name: Uncommon Ground Farmer Friday Farmers Market
Union County Farmers' Market
description: The Union County Farmers Market is held in the East Davie Street parking lot in downtown Anna, IL. The market features locally grown vegetables, fruits, flowers and more from area growers.

name: Urbana Market at the Square
description: Urbana's Market at the Square is a long-running farmers market in downtown Urbana, IL, at the corner of Illinois & Vine Streets, operating since 1979. It offers fresh produce, baked goods, art, spices and handcrafted items from area vendors, rain or shine.

name: Vandalia, Illinois Farmers' Market
description: The Vandalia, Illinois Farmers' Market is held at 4th and Gallatin Streets in historic downtown Vandalia, in front of Charter's Patio. The monthly market draws more than 20 vendors selling fresh produce and locally made goods.

name: Village of Lemont Farmers Market
description: The Village of Lemont Farmers Market is a seasonal market held at the Village Green on Main Street in downtown Lemont, IL. Vendors rotate weekly and offer goods such as salsas, honey, baked goods, spices and fresh vegetables from local growers and bakers.

name: Village of Orland Park Farmers' Market
description: The Village of Orland Park Farmers' Market is held on the grounds of the Village Center at 14700 S. Ravinia Avenue in Orland Park, IL. The seasonal open-air market features over 30 vendors selling fresh fruits, vegetables, flowers and plants, gourmet foods, oils, bakery goods, crafts and more.

name: Village of Park Forest Farmers' Market
Warrenville Farmers Market
description: The Warrenville Farmers Market is held at the Warrenville Park District Rec Center parking lot (Warren & Tracey) in downtown Warrenville, open to growers, producers, craftspersons, artists, and local non-profits. Vendors offer baked goods, cheese, arts & crafts, flowers, eggs, vegetables, jams, meats, plants, and poultry.

name: Watseka Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 7:00 AM - 12:00 PM, mid-June through late October
description: A free outdoor farmers market in downtown Watseka's historic district featuring fresh produce, handmade goods, live entertainment, and family activities held weekly through summer and early fall.

name: Wauconda Farmers' Market
description: The Wauconda Farmers' Market is a seasonal open-air market at 100 N. Main Street in Wauconda, IL, sponsored by the Wauconda Area Chamber of Commerce. It features a mix of vendors from Lake and McHenry counties selling fresh produce, flowers, meats, cheeses and breads, with live entertainment, a kids corner and food vendors.

name: Westmont Open Market
street: W. Irving St. and N Cass Ave.

name: Wheaton French Market
season: Saturdays, 8:00 AM - 2:00 PM (April through October); Saturdays 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM in November
description: A Bensidoun USA French Market held at Main and Liberty in downtown Wheaton, featuring fresh flowers, artisan foods, and music.

name: Wicker Park Farmers Market
description: Fresh, local, and sustainably produced, the Wicker Park & Walsh Park Farmers Markets bring together regional farmers, food producers, crafters, and small businesses in the heart of the neighborhood.

name: Wilmette French Market
street: Green Bay Rd. at Central Ave.
season: Saturdays, 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM, April through October
description: A Bensidoun USA French Market held at 722 Green Bay Rd in Wilmette, featuring fresh flowers, artisan foods, and music.

name: Winfield Farmers Market
street: Geneva Rd. at County Farm Rd.

name: Woodstock Farmers Market
season: Outdoor Tuesdays & Saturdays 8am-1pm, May-October (Woodstock Square); indoor select Saturdays Nov-April
description: The Woodstock Farmers Market is a Producers Only market on the Historic Woodstock Square, where only products grown, raised, or made by local vendors can be sold. It offers farm-fresh produce (including several organic growers), garden plants, pasture-raised pork, chicken, beef and eggs, cheese, local honey, fruit, baked goods, artisan products, flower bouquets, soaps, and grab-and-go eats. In the off-season the market moves indoors to All Seasons Orchard.

name: Yorkville Farmers Market
street: S. Bridge St. at S. Main St.

name: Alexandria Farmers' & Artists Market
season: Fridays 3 p.m.-6 p.m., May through September
description: A venue for area farmers, gardeners, bakers, artisans and fine artists in Alexandria, Indiana to sell locally grown and produced items including fresh fruits, vegetables, herbs, cut flowers, bedding plants, baked goods and fine arts and crafts.

name: Avon Farmers Market
season: Tuesdays 4:00 p.m.-7:00 p.m., June through September
description: Hosted by the Town of Avon in the parking lot of Hendricks Regional Health on US Hwy 36 east of Dan Jones Road, the Avon Farmers Market brings residents together with local food producers and vendors on Tuesday evenings.

name: Bloomington Community Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays April through October (8 a.m.-12:30 p.m. April-September, 9 a.m.-12:30 p.m. October) plus a Tuesday market 4-7 p.m. June-September
description: The Bloomington Community Farmers' Market takes place rain or shine every Saturday and offers fruits and vegetables, eggs, meats and cheeses, honey, seasonal flowers, and annual and perennial plants.

name: Bloomington Community Farmers' Market - Tuesday
description: The Tuesday Market is a smaller weekday version of the Bloomington Community Farmers' Market, held at Hopewell Commons in Bloomington, IN. It offers local farm-grown food and products from area small businesses and food-and-beverage artisans, and accepts SNAP benefits with a Double Market Bucks program.

name: Bluffton Commons Farmers' Market
street: Corner of Main and Washington Streets
season: Saturdays 9 a.m.-1 p.m., May through October
description: A seasonal Saturday market in Bluffton, Indiana featuring fresh local produce, plants, cut flowers, baked goods, local honey and molasses, and homemade crafts.

name: Broad Ripple Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays year-round; 8 a.m.-noon May-September, 9 a.m.-noon October-April
description: A year-round Indianapolis market with 80+ local vendors outdoors May-October at Glendale Town Center and 60+ vendors indoors November-April at Broad Ripple Middle School.

name: Brown County Homegrown & Homemade Village Market
Carmel Farmers Market

description: The Carmel Farmers Market's mission is to provide the community with the freshest and healthiest food that Indiana has to offer while giving small businesses a venue to grow and prosper.

Clark County Farmers' Market
description: The Clark County / Jeffersonville Farmers Market, run by Jeffersonville Main Street, is held at Big Four Station park (222 W. Market Street) in downtown Jeffersonville, IN, next to the Big Four Bridge. It offers locally grown and locally made products with live music, and runs a winter market from November through May.

name: Columbus City Farmers' Market

name: Community Farmer's Market

Offerings include seasonal produce, free-range meats, dairy, eggs, cheese, baked goods, Amish noodles, jams, honey, maple syrup, cut flowers, plant starts and handcrafted goods.

description: Breadbasket Farmers Market is a tobacco-free outdoor market located in the south parking lot of the Furniture Mall of Kansas at West Ridge Mall in Topeka, featuring local vendors and fresh produce.

description: The Capitol Midweek Farmers' Market is a downtown Topeka market at 10th Avenue and Jackson Street, near the Kansas Statehouse, offering seasonal produce, baked goods, eggs, herbs and cut flowers.

description: The Colwich Farmers Market is held at Veterans' Park and offers fresh and canned fruits and vegetables, homemade bakery goods, specialty items such as salsas, pickles and jellies, and handcrafted goods, with ample parking, shade trees and no vendor booth fee.

description: The Dodge City Farmers' Market is held indoors inside Village Square Mall and showcases area producers offering fresh fruit, vegetables, herbs, flowers, farm-fresh eggs, handmade crafts and baked goods.

name: Downtown Farmers' Market of Manhattan Inc.

description: Established in 1979, the Downtown Farmers Market of Manhattan is one of the oldest markets in Kansas, with local vendors offering fresh produce, meats, baked goods and handmade crafts at 3rd and Leavenworth in the Dillard's west parking lot.

name: Downtown Topeka Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, 7:30 a.m.-noon, April through the last weekend in October
description: The Downtown Topeka Farmers' Market has operated since 1930 and features local vendors selling fresh farm products, baked goods, crafts and handmade items at its downtown Topeka location.

name: El Dorado Farmers' Market
season: Select Sundays, 10 a.m.-2 p.m., June through September

season: Saturdays, 8 a.m.-11 a.m., year-round (summer May-September, winter October-April)
description: The Frankfort Farmers' Market is held in downtown Frankfort at Kansas Avenue and 2nd Street, operating year-round across summer and winter seasons.

name: Fredonia, Kansas Farmers' Market

description: The Fredonia, Kansas Farmers' Market offers locally-sourced produce, baked goods and artisan goods, supporting community vendors and nonprofits, with both summer and winter Saturday markets.

name: Fresh Start Youth Garden Market

name: Garnett Farmers' Market
description: The Garnett Farmers' Market is held on Main Street adjacent to the Prairie Spirit Trail between 4th and 5th Avenues, offering home-grown goods, fresh baked items, and farm-raised meats and eggs.

name: Geary Community Farmers Market

name: Girard Farmer's Market
description: The Girard Farmers' Market is held on the Square at the Gazebo in Girard and welcomes all vendors and customers, featuring fresh produce and unique items from local growers.

name: Goodland Open Air Market
season: Saturdays, 8 a.m.-11 a.m., June through October
description: The Goodland Open Air Market is a summer gathering on the 1200 block of Main Street where citizens of Goodland stock up on fresh, locally produced goods.

description: The Harper County Homegrown Farm & Art Market features growers, makers and bakers from all over Harper County offering fresh produce, one-of-a-kind pieces and baked goods, held at the Patterson Health Center in Anthony.

description: The Harvey County Farmers' Market is held at 301 North Main in Newton, behind The Breadbasket in the Old Mill parking lot, offering vegetables, baked goods, honey, herbs, beef, pork, poultry and farm-fresh eggs.

name: Haven Farmer's Market

name: Haysville Hometown Market

name: Hiawatha Farmers' Market
description: The Hiawatha Farmers' Market is held at 199 E Iowa St in Hiawatha and offers fresh produce, baked goods, eggs, meat and hand-crafted items from local vendors.

name: Hillsboro Farmers' Market

name: KC Farm School Farmers Market

name: Kingman Farmers Market @ The Binyard
description: The Binyard is a uniquely Midwest marketplace in downtown Kingman housed in restored grain bins that serve as individual vendor spaces. It operates as both a shopping destination for local makers and specialty goods and as a rentable venue for vendors offering daily, weekly, or monthly bin rentals. It is located at 100 North Main Street in Kingman, Kansas.

name: Lawrence Farmers' Market

description: A 501(c)(3) nonprofit producer market operating since 1976 where shoppers buy directly from farmers, chefs, and artisans located within 50 miles of Lawrence, Kansas.

name: Leavenworth Farmers' Market

Vendors sell fresh produce, meats, breads, and baked goods, along with handmade items such as jewelry, soaps, and woodworking. Leashed pets are welcome.

description: Held at 600 W. Woodside in McPherson, this market offers local produce, eggs, baked goods, meats, flowers, and more.

name: Mission Farm and Flower Market
description: Mission Market is the City of Mission, Kansas's seasonal farm and flower market, now in its 12th season. For 2026 it is held at Broadmoor Park, 5701 Broadmoor Street on the west end of Mission, a temporary relocation due to construction at its regular home. The market features local produce, prepared foods, ceramics, and other vendor goods.

name: Mission Market
season: Thursdays 4:30-8:00 p.m., June through August (Broadmoor Park)
description: Mission Market is a seasonal outdoor community market in Mission, Kansas, featuring local artisans, food vendors, and live music. The market runs on summer evenings at Broadmoor Park, giving the community a place to shop local goods and gather. Vendor listings and a music lineup are published each season.

name: Monday Farmers Market At Your Library
season: Mondays 7:30-11:30 a.m., mid-May through mid-September (library parking lot)
description: The Monday Farmers Market at Your Library is hosted by the Topeka & Shawnee County Public Library to increase access to high-quality, locally grown produce, farm-fresh eggs, baked goods, fresh-cut flowers, and bedding plants within walking distance of downtown Topeka. Held in the library parking lot, it supports the local economy and offers community connection alongside fresh, locally sourced items.

name: Newton Farm & Art Market
season: Saturdays 8:00 AM–12:00 PM, May through October, with occasional once-a-month markets November through April
description: The Newton Farm & Art Market is a local farmers and art market offering wholesome, locally produced, nutritious food for the community.

name: Nomad Market KC
description: Nomad Market KC is a farmers market at 2nd & Mulberry in Louisburg, Kansas featuring a rotating mix of more than 20 local vendors offering farm-fresh produce, local meats and eggs, baked goods, and artisan crafts.

name: Norton Farmers Market
description: The Norton Farmer's Market offers local produce and vegetables, farm-raised beef, breads, micro-greens, baked goods, pies, jams, jellies, and honey from producers within a 90-mile radius.

name: Overland Park Farmers Market
season: Saturdays and Wednesdays, April through November, at Clock Tower Plaza in downtown Overland Park
description: The Overland Park Farmers' Market is held at Clock Tower Plaza in the heart of downtown Overland Park, a community gathering place with public art, picnic tables, and a fountain.

name: Park Place Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays 7:30 AM–12:00 PM, May through October (rain or shine)
description: Held at Park Place Village along W. 116th Place in Leawood, this market features produce vendors, local farmers, specialty food makers, and acoustic performances.

name: Parsons Farmers' Market
season: Tuesdays 3:00 PM–6:00 PM and Saturdays 8:00 AM–11:00 AM, April through October (weather permitting)
description: The Parsons Farmers Market gives residents throughout southeast Kansas the opportunity to purchase locally grown fruits, vegetables, nuts, honey, bedding plants, baked goods, handmade jellies, and crafts with no vendor fees.

name: Phillips County Farmers Market
season: Second Tuesday of the month 4:00 PM–7:00 PM, June through October
description: The Phillips County Farmers Market is held on the square in downtown Phillipsburg with local farm and craft vendors, live music, food trucks, and a kids corner.

name: Pomona Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 8:00 AM–12:00 PM, May through October
description: The Pomona Farmer's Market offers produce, baked goods, eggs, honey, plants, and craft items at 409 W. Franklin Street in Pomona, Kansas.

name: Public Library Monday Market
season: Mid-May–mid-September, Mondays 7:30–11:30am (no market on Labor Day)
description: The Monday Farmers Market is held in the parking lot of the Topeka & Shawnee County Public Library in Topeka, Kansas. Vendors sell locally grown produce, farm fresh eggs, baked goods, fresh cut flowers, bedding plants, potted herbs, seedlings, and honey.

name: Quinter Farmers Market
season: Fridays 8:30 AM–5:00 PM, March through December
description: The Quinter Farmers Market at 329 Main Street is a community-based location for local vendors to sell and promote their businesses while building relationships for a closer-knit community.

name: Reno County Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays 7:30 AM–12:30 PM (May through October) and Wednesdays 10:00 AM–1:00 PM (June through September)
description: Established in 1985, the Reno County Farmers Market is an open-air market in historic downtown Hutchinson offering fresh local produce, honey, baked goods, and handmade crafts from local farmers, artisans, and musicians.

name: Salina Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays 7:30 AM–11:00 AM, May through October
description: The Salina Farm & Art Market in downtown Salina connects farmers, artisans, and area residents while supporting the local food system and small businesses.

name: Silver Lake Farmers Market
season: Tuesdays 6:00 PM–8:00 PM, June through August
description: The Silver Lake Farmer's Market is a gathering place that builds pride and a sense of community, with vendors providing fresh produce and specialty merchandise.

name: Solomon Valley Farmers' Market
season: Wednesdays 4:00 PM–6:00 PM, Memorial Day through Labor Day
description: The Solomon Valley Farmer's Market is a seasonal market at the Lil Red Schoolhouse Roadside Park on Hwy 24 in Beloit, offering local vendors and a community gathering spot.

name: Step Back in Time Food Market LLC
Bounty of the Barrens Winter Market
season: Saturdays 8:00 AM - noon, May through October
description: The Bounty of the Barrens is a local, producer-only farmers' market supported by Sustainable Glasgow. It offers produce, meats, eggs, cheeses, baked goods, and other local food and handcrafted items from Barren and surrounding counties.

name: Boyd County Farmers' Market
season: Tuesday and Thursday 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM and Saturday 7:00 AM - 2:00 PM, June through October

name: Community Farmers' Market
season: Year-round; Saturdays 8 a.m.-1 p.m. and Tuesdays 2 p.m.-6 p.m.
description: The Community Farmers Market is a year-round farmers market in Bowling Green, Kentucky, offering fresh local produce and goods directly from area farmers and producers. It operates two weekly market days and runs outreach programs including Double Dollars assistance and Fresh Rx for Moms to expand access to healthy food.

name: Dawson Springs Main Street Farmers Market
Farmers' Market at Mellwood Arts and Entertainment Center
Farmers' Market of Murray - Calloway County
Grant County Farmers Market - Crittenden
description: The Grant County Farmers Market in Crittenden, Kentucky sets up next to the Whippy Dip off US 25, reached via KY 491 East from I-75 Exit 166. Vendors offer beans, berries, cabbage, summer and winter squash, cucumbers, potatoes, onions, pumpkins, corn, tomatoes, honey, and home-baked and home-canned goods, with availability varying by season.

name: Henderson Farmers' Market
street: Airline Rd. and Garden Mile Rd

name: Henry County Farmers' Market - New Castle
Independence Farmers' Market
description: The Independence Farmers' Market is held at the Kenton County Courthouse, 5272 Madison Pike in Independence, Kentucky, on Saturday mornings. It is organized through the City of Independence and offers seasonal produce and local vendor goods.

name: Jackson County Farmers Market - Sand Gap
La Center Community Farmers' Market
description: The La Center Community Farmers' Market is located at 392 East Kentucky Drive in La Center, Kentucky, about 17 miles west of Paducah. Vendors offer vegetables, fruits, berries, flowering plants, perennials, hanging baskets, jams and jellies, honey, nuts, and relishes. A flea market is held on the first Saturday of each month.

name: Lexington Farmers' Market
season: Saturday (Main St) year-round 8am–2pm (winter 9am–1pm); other days seasonal
description: The Lexington Farmers' Market operates multiple weekly locations across Lexington, Kentucky. Its year-round Saturday market is at the Fifth Third Pavilion at Henry A. Tandy Park on Main Street, with additional markets Sundays at Southland Drive, Tuesdays/Thursdays on National Avenue, and Wednesdays at Greyline Station. The market runs community programs including a Power of Produce (POP) Club and SNAP/Kentucky Double Dollars.

name: Muhlenberg County Farmers' Market
season: Tuesday and Thursday 3:00 PM - 6:00 PM and Saturday 8:00 AM - 12:00 PM, May through November

name: Muhlenberg County Farmers' Market - Central City
Paris Bourbon County Farmers' Market
season: Monday through Friday 11:00 AM - 6:00 PM and Saturday 9:00 AM - 1:00 PM
description: The Paris-Bourbon County Farmers' Market grew from a few farmers selling out of the back of their pickup trucks to a year-round storefront in historic downtown Paris, offering handmade and homegrown goods including eggs, honey, meats, and fresh-baked items.

name: Shelby County Farmers Market - Shelbyville
season: Every Saturday 8:00 AM - 12:00 noon, May through October
description: Since 2003 the Shelby County Farmers' Market vendors have provided fresh, local fruit, vegetables, flowers, eggs, chicken, pork, beef, turkey, honey, woodcrafts, art, cakes and pastries, soap, roasted coffee, and breads to the Shelbyville community.

name: The Louisville Farmers Market - Landis Lakes
Versailles Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays 8:30 AM - noon (May through October) and Wednesdays 3:00 PM - 5:30 PM (June through October)

Covington Farmers Market
city: 609 North Columbia St. (Covington City Hall) Covington

Berkshire Grown Winter Farmers' Market- Williamstown

Carlisle Farmers Market

Kilroy Square Farmers Market in Quincy Center
Leominster Marketplace
season: First Saturday of the month, June 4 to October 1

Plymouth Harbor Market Days-1820 Courthouse Green

Allegany Mountain Fresh Farmers Market - Downtown Cumberland, Maryland
Anne Arundel Medical Center Farmers Market-Luminis Health
Boordy Vineyards Good Life Thursday Farmers Market
description: Boordy Vineyards hosts its Good Life Thursdays farmers' market at the winery in Hydes, featuring vendors with organic produce, organic beef, chicken and pork, sustainable seafood, locally roasted coffee and artisanal cheeses, while Boordy offers its own wines for sampling and purchase.

name: Briggs Chaney-Greencastle Farmers and Artisans Market
description: The Briggs Chaney-Greencastle Farmers' and Artisans' Market is a child-friendly community market serving North Silver Spring and surrounding areas, offering fresh farm meats, eggs, breads, fruits and vegetables alongside prepared foods, live music and local artisans.

name: Catonsville Farmers Market
season: Every other Wednesday, 9 AM to noon

name: Crofton Farmers' Market
description: The Crofton Farmers' Market gathers more than 50 local vendors at the Crofton Library in Gambrills, Maryland, offering produce, meats, dairy, breads, flowers, prepared food, collectibles and crafts, along with food trucks. Now in its 12th season, the market runs on Saturday mornings and continues to accept vendor applications.

name: Downtown Cumberland Farmers' Market
description: The Downtown Cumberland Farmers' Market sets up on Baltimore Street in downtown Cumberland, bringing together regional growers and producers from Maryland and West Virginia offering fresh produce, fruits, plants and other locally sourced goods.

name: Emmitsburg Farmers Market
description: The Emmitsburg Farmers Market is a seasonal market organized by the Town of Emmitsburg, Maryland, held in the warmer months and returning annually. Market coordination is handled through the town office, which maintains the market's schedule and vendor information.

name: Glen Burnie Town Center Farmers Market
Govanstowne Farmers Market
season: Wednesdays, 3 to 6 p.m., June through November
description: The Govans Farmers Market is an inclusive market in the heart of Baltimore's historic York Road corridor that expands access to healthy food, supports local entrepreneurs and brings neighbors together.

name: Greater Dundalk Chamber of Commerce Farmers Market
Hollywood Farmers Market
description: The Hollywood Farmers Market is held every Saturday in the Hollywood Shopping Center at 9801–9803 Rhode Island Avenue in College Park, Maryland, next to MOM's Organic Market. It is a family-friendly market featuring produce, prepared foods, and live music each week, and accepts SNAP, WIC, and other food benefits with matching dollars.

name: Howard County Farmers Market at East Columbia
Howard County Farmers Market at Oakland Mills
Main Street Farmers Market
season: Year-round at the Main Street Pavilion (indoor and outdoor seasons; closed Christmas and New Year's Day)
description: The Main Street Farmers Market is a year-round market in Gaithersburg, Maryland, centered on the Main Street Pavilion at 301 Main Street. It offers fresh produce, flowers, and other local goods with both indoor and outdoor seasons. The market is operated by the City of Gaithersburg.

name: Mount Airy Farmers' Market
season: Wednesdays 3-7 p.m., May through September (Watkins Park)
description: The Mount Airy Farmers Market is a Town-managed market and one of the biggest events of the late spring and summer season in Mount Airy, Maryland. It features local and regional vendors selling produce, honey, handmade jewelry, meats, eggs, wines, and crafts, along with weekly live entertainment and space for nonprofits and community organizations. The market is held at Watkins Park.

name: Mount Rainier Farmers' Market
description: The Mount Rainier (Gateway) Farmers' Market is a community market held at the Bunker Hill Fire Department on Rhode Island Avenue, offering fresh local produce and goods from area vendors.

name: New! Owings Mills Farmers' Market
season: Wednesdays, 3 to 7 PM, June through October
description: Run by the Northwest Chamber of Commerce in the heart of Owings Mills, the market brings together local farms and vendors offering produce, baked goods, crafts and food trucks, with rotating vendors and weekly special events.

name: Ocean Pines Farmers Market
season: Saturdays year-round; 8 a.m.-1 p.m. April-August and 9 a.m.-noon September-March (White Horse Park)
description: The Ocean Pines Farmers & Artisans Market is described by the Ocean Pines Association as Worcester County's largest year-round farmers market. Held at White Horse Park on Ocean Parkway, it features local produce, artisan goods, music, and family-friendly happenings with a 2026 calendar of events running January through December.

name: Taneytown Farmers' Market
season: May 10 – November 1, Saturdays 8 a.m. to 1 p.m.
description: The Taneytown Farmers' Market is held Saturday mornings at the Thunderhead Bowl & Grill parking lot (4337 Old Taneytown Road), offering seasonal farm-fresh vegetables, fruit, baked goods, eggs, herbs, honey, flowers, and plants from local growers.

name: The Village of Cross Keys Farmers Market
description: The Village of Cross Keys Farmers Market is held in the parking lot at the Village of Cross Keys on Falls Road in Baltimore, featuring local farmers and producers offering fresh produce, baked goods and handcrafted items.

name: Tuesday Market in Lauraville
season: June 4 – September 24, Tuesdays 4 p.m. to 8 p.m.
description: The Tuesday Market in Lauraville, run by Hamilton-Lauraville Main Street, is held Tuesday afternoons at 4500 Harford Road (across from Safeway) in Baltimore, featuring local farms, prepared food, artisan products, and live music under a make-it-or-grow-it vendor policy.

name: Wildberry Farm + Market
description: Wildberry Farm + Brewery in Crownsville, Maryland is a land-raised, hand-crafted farm brewery building out its on-site pavilion and grounds, having completed its first brew as it transitions from the former Wildberry shop.

name: YMCA in Frederick Farmers Market
season: May 20 – October 27, Tuesdays 3:30 p.m. to 6:30 p.m.
description: The YMCA of Frederick County Farmers Market sets up in the front lot of the Downtown Y at 1000 N. Market Street, Frederick, MD, offering fresh produce, meats, dairy, and other items from local farms and artisans on Tuesday afternoons.

name: Bridgton Farmers' Market
season: Summer: Saturdays, 8:00 AM–12:00 PM, May through October. Winter: 9:00 AM–12:00 PM, November through April.
description: The Bridgton Farmers' Market works to improve the marketing of local farm, garden and artisan products in the Lake Region area and to stimulate public interest in the consumption of local farm and garden products.

name: Cumberland Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 10:00 AM–1:00 PM, May through October (Cumberland location).
description: The Cumberland & Falmouth Farmers Market is a 501(c)(6) non-profit featuring a diverse group of farmers and artisans providing local, sustainable and organic produce, meats, milk products, baked goods, prepared foods, honey, flowers, plants and handmade gifts.

name: Downtown Waterville Winter Farmers' Market
description: The Downtown Waterville Winter Farmers' Market is a 100% producer-only indoor market in Waterville, Maine, where shoppers buy directly from the farmers and makers. It features fresh and storage vegetables, meat, eggs, cheese, fresh-baked bread and pastries, maple syrup, and homestead crafts during the cold-weather season.

name: Falmouth Farmers Market
season: Wednesdays, 10:00 AM–1:00 PM, May through October (Falmouth location).
description: The Cumberland & Falmouth Farmers Market is a 501(c)(6) non-profit featuring a diverse group of farmers and artisans providing local, sustainable and organic produce, meats, milk products, baked goods, prepared foods, honey, flowers, plants and handmade gifts.

name: Gateway Winter Farmers' Market
description: The Gateway Winter Farmers' Market is the cold-season edition of York, Maine's Gateway farmers market, held indoors in the Fellowship Hall of the First Parish Church at 180 York Street. The indoor market brings together local farmers and Maine makers selling produce, baked goods, and packaged foods during the winter months.

name: Hampden Farmers' Market
description: The Hampden Farmers' Market celebrates what local Maine farmers and artisans have to offer, with products including fresh produce, cheese, meats, baked goods and much more.

name: Saco Farmers' and Artisans' Market
description: The Saco Farmers' & Artisans' Market is one of Maine's oldest farmers' markets, operating since 1975. Held in the Saco Valley Shopping Center at the corner of Spring and Scammon Streets, it offers seasonal produce, seafood, meat, eggs, plants, flowers, honey, maple syrup, and baked goods.

name: Sanford Farmers' Market
description: The Sanford Farmers' Market is a community market in Central Park in downtown Sanford, Maine, connecting local food producers and growers with the community. It features around 20 vendors selling meats, produce, mushrooms, baked goods, and crafts, and supports SNAP/EBT with Maine Harvest Bucks plus senior, veteran, and children's produce vouchers.

name: The Gateway York Farmers' Market
description: The Gateway York Farmers' Market is a summer market held in the lot behind the Greater York Region Chamber of Commerce at 1 Stonewall Lane in York, Maine. Each Saturday it brings together local farmers, artisans, and Maine makers selling produce, baked goods, pre-packaged foods, crafts, and housewares.

name: Union Farmers Market
description: The Union Farmers' Market is held at the Blueberry Building on Common Road in Union, with vendors selling cheese, honey, kimchi, native plants, wood-fired pizza, vegetables, flowers, handmade goods, cider, and blueberry wine.

name: Wells Farmers' Market
description: The Wells Farmers Market is part of the Tri-Town Farmers Markets and is held at the Wells Public Library at 1434 Post Road in Wells, Maine. The summer market runs seasonally; the organization also operates a winter market at a separate South Berwick location.

name: Bangor Farmers Market
season: Fridays, 3:00 p.m. to 8:00 p.m., June through September

name: Bath Township Farmers Market
description: The Bath Township Farmers Market is a seasonal market held at James Couzens Memorial Park in Bath, Michigan, organized by Bath Township. It runs on Thursday afternoons during the warmer months, with vendor and event updates posted by the township.

name: Birch Run Farmers Market
season: Thursdays 3:00 p.m. to 7:00 p.m. and Sundays 10:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m.
description: The Birch Run Farmers Market is a seasonal outdoor market held at the Birch Run Premium Outlets just off I-75 in Birch Run, Michigan.

name: Cadillac Made in Michigan Farmers' Market
Chelsea Farmers Market and Bushel Basket Market
description: The Chelsea Farmers Market, presented by Chelsea Hospital, has operated for over 25 years in Chelsea, Michigan. It runs a Saturday market at Palmer Commons (304 S. Main St.) and a Wednesday market at Chelsea State Bank (1010 S. Main St.), selling seasonal produce, meat, poultry, eggs, honey, maple syrup, baked goods, herbs, and handcrafted items.

name: City of Holland Municipal Farmer's Market
description: The Holland Farmers Market is a municipal market at the Eighth Street Market Place, 150 West 8th Street in downtown Holland, Michigan, hosting over 75 vendors. It offers freshly picked fruits and vegetables, flowers and plants, baked goods, honey, maple syrup, farm-fresh cheese, eggs, and meats, and operates a Saturday indoor market in the off-season.

name: De Tour Village Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 10:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m., May through September
description: The De Tour Village Farmers Market is held under a covered pavilion in the middle of town at the corner of Ontario and St. Mary's Streets, on the eastern tip of Michigan's Upper Peninsula.

name: Downtown Saginaw Farmers' Market
description: The Downtown Saginaw Farmers' Market operates under outdoor pavilions at the SVRC Marketplace, 203 S. Washington Ave. in Saginaw, Michigan. It sells locally grown produce, Michigan fruits and vegetables, plants, cut flowers, baked goods, honey, soaps, meats, eggs, and artisan goods from regional vendors.

name: Downtown Ypsilanti Farmers' Market
description: The Downtown Ypsilanti Farmers Market is an open-air market run by Growing Hope at 16 South Washington Street in Ypsilanti, Michigan. Held every Saturday in season, it brings together local farmers and vendors offering fresh produce and other locally produced goods.

name: Eastside Farmers Market
season: Fridays, 3:00 p.m. to 7:00 p.m., June through September
description: The Eastside Farmers Market is a seasonal market held at Mack Alter Square (14820 Mack Ave) on Detroit's east side, offering local produce, baked goods, eggs, meat, flowers and crafts.

name: Farmington Farmers & Artisans Market
description: The Farmington Farmers & Artisans Market is an award-winning market held at the Sundquist Pavilion, 33113 Grand River Ave. in downtown Farmington, Michigan. Operating rain or shine, it offers fresh local produce, artisan goods, and prepared foods, with weekly themed events and live music.

name: G&A Farmers Market and Garden Center
description: G&A Farmers Market and Garden Center brings local, natural and organic products to the Houghton community, with an on-site garden and fruit stand selling fresh produce, local berries, fruits, eggs, jams, honey and bakery items alongside annual, perennial and shrub plants.

name: Garden Taste Fest and Market Place
Grand Ledge Farmers' Market
description: Market on the Grand is the Grand Ledge farmers market, presented by the Grand Ledge Area Chamber of Commerce at Jaycee Park, 525 E. River Street in Grand Ledge, Michigan. It features fresh local produce, baked goods, handcrafted products, plants, honey, maple syrup, smoked fish, and flowers, with food trucks and live entertainment.

name: Grand Valley State University Farmers' Market
Green Boots Veteran Community Hotriculture Gardens/Marketplace
Green Oak Farmers and Community Market
Hartland Farmer s Market
description: The Hartland Farmers Market offers locally grown fruits and vegetables, fresh meats, flowers and specialty items along with flea-market and artisan craft vendors in Hartland, Michigan.

name: Hazel Park Growers' and Makers' Market
Holt Farmers Market
description: The Holt Farmers Market is a year-round, rain-or-shine market on Cedar Street in downtown Holt, housed in the former Delhi Township fire station, offering a wide variety of foods and hand-made craft items.

name: Hubbard Lake Farmers' Market
description: The Hubbard Lake Farmers' Market is held at the Hubbard Lake Community Center on the north end of Hubbard Lake, Michigan, offering locally grown in-season produce, herbs, Amish-made baked goods, pastured meats, free-range eggs, plants and local crafts.

name: Huron Valley Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, 10:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m., May through October
description: The Huron Valley Farmers' Market is located at 300 W. Livingston in Highland Township, Michigan, serving Milford, White Lake and Hartland with locally-sourced fresh products.

name: Lake Leelanau Farmers Market
season: Summer (Sundays, June through early September), 9:00 AM - 1:00 PM
description: The Lake Leelanau Farmers Market is a seasonal market in Leelanau County, Michigan, held on Sunday mornings during the summer. All products and produce sold are made or grown within 60 miles of the market, featuring regional crops such as tart cherries, blueberries, asparagus, and apples. It is part of the larger Leelanau Farmers Markets network supporting local farms.

name: Leelanau Farmers' Market
description: The Leelanau (Empire) Farmers Market is part of the Leelanau Farmers Markets network and is held in downtown Empire, Michigan, next to the post office at 10234 Front Street. Each Saturday morning in season it offers locally grown produce, meat, flowers, baked goods, and crafts from vendors across Leelanau County.

name: Little Red Shed Farm Market
description: A&A's Little Red Shed Farm Market and Bakery is a family-owned farm market in Lawton, Michigan specializing in fresh, high-quality homegrown produce picked daily along with jams, jellies and baked goods.

name: Lowell Area Farmers Market
season: Thursdays, 2 PM - 7 PM, June through September
description: The Lowell Area Farmers' Market is held in downtown Lowell's green space north of the Kent District Library Englehardt Branch and along the Riverwalk, offering seasonal and weekly vendor spaces.

name: Marysville Farmers Market and Flea Market
Menominee Historial Downtown Farmers Market Association
Milford Farmers' Market
season: May 7 – October 8, 2026, Thursdays 3pm–7pm (no market July 2 or August 6)
description: The Milford Farmers Market features only Michigan-produced goods from local farmers, producers, and artisans, located on East Liberty Street between S. Main St. and Union St. in Milford, Michigan. The market includes children's activities, cooking demonstrations, special events, and weekly live music, and partners with local non-profit organizations.

name: Muskegon Indoor Winter Market
description: The Muskegon Indoor Winter Market is the cold-season edition of the Muskegon Farmers Market, held in 'The Barn' at 242 West Western Avenue in downtown Muskegon, Michigan (next to the Post Office). It runs on Saturdays through the winter months.

name: New Baltimore Farmers Market
season: Sundays, 9:00 am - 2:00 pm, May through October
description: The New Baltimore Farmers Market is a 501(c)(3) non-profit community organization that provides a link to local foods, farmers, and artisans, with supportive access to fresh foods for all consumers.

name: Niles Bensidoun French Market/Niles Main Street
Novi Farmers' Market & International Street Fair
Oakland County Farmers Market
season: May through December: Tuesday, Thursday and Saturday, 7 AM - 1:30 PM; January through April: Saturday, 7 AM - 1:30 PM
description: The Oakland County Farmers Market in Waterford hosts more than 100 farmers and artisans representing 17 Michigan counties, offering fresh vegetables, fruits, baked goods, flowers, and artisan crafts year round.

name: Ortonville Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 9:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m., June through September
description: The Ortonville Farmers Market gathers local farmers, artists, crafters and food producers in downtown Ortonville, Michigan, with a different theme featured each week.

name: Owosso Farmers Market
description: The Downtown Owosso Farmers Market is the largest farmers market in Mid-Michigan, providing locally grown produce and artisan-made goods from over 40 vendors at Exchange & Water Street in front of the Owosso Armory.

name: Redford Township Market at the Marquee
Scottville Farmers' Market at Miller's Marketplace
Shelby Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 9 A.M. - 2 P.M., May through October
description: The Shelby Farmers Market is an outdoor market situated on the historic Packard Proving Grounds in Shelby Township, offering fruits, vegetables, eggs, honey, maple syrup, bread, and bakery items.

name: South Lyon Farmer and Artisan Market
description: The South Lyon Farmers and Artisans Market is a recurring downtown event held in South Lyon, Michigan, organized through the Downtown South Lyon (DDA) office at 335 S. Warren Street.

name: Southgate Farmer s Market
season: Saturdays and Tuesdays, 6:00 a.m. to noon (outdoor from late spring through October; indoor on Saturdays through the winter)
description: The Southgate Farmers Market is a year-round market in Southgate, Michigan offering in-season produce, honey, eggs and crafts, moving indoors during the winter months.

name: Sowing Seeds Growing Futures Farmers Market
season: Tuesdays, 3:00 p.m. to 6:00 p.m. through early October
description: Sowing Seeds Growing Futures is the annual seasonal farmers market hosted by the Joy Southfield Community Development Corporation at 18900 Joy Road in Detroit, Michigan.

name: St. Ignace Farmers Market
description: The St. Ignace Farmers Market (Bayside Farmers Market) is held on the waterfront in historic downtown St. Ignace at 13 S. State Street, with a view of Moran Bay. Vendors offer fresh produce from regional farms along with baked goods, arts and crafts, flowers, eggs, herbs, honey, jams, and maple products. The market is organized through the St. Ignace Chamber of Commerce and is paired with free Thursday-evening Bayside Live waterfront concerts in July and August.

name: Sweetwater Local Foods Market
description: Sweetwater Local Foods Market is a Muskegon-area market operating since 2005 that supports small, organic, local food producers under the motto 'Healthy. Humane. Homegrown.' It is held at Mercy Health Lakes Village, 6401 Harvey St. (at US-31/Pontaluna Rd.), and features local farm vendors offering produce and other homegrown goods with a preorder option.

name: Tecumseh Farmers' Market
description: Tecumseh's Farmers Market is held at the Market on Evans, 213 North Evans Street, about 1.5 blocks north of Chicago Boulevard in downtown Tecumseh, Michigan. Vendors offer locally grown produce, farm-fresh eggs, herbs, flowers, baked goods, handmade crafts, organic items, and antiques. Barrier-free parking is available across the street at Logan and Evans Streets.

name: The Market at SCA (Saugatuck Center for the Arts)
Three Rivers - Scidmore Park Farmers' Market
Traverse City Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays (first Sat of May–October) 7:30am–12pm; Wednesdays (first Wed of June–October) 8am–12pm
description: The Sara Hardy Downtown Farmers Market in Traverse City has been operated by the Downtown Development Authority since 1984 and is one of the three largest markets in Michigan, hosting over 115 local farmers. It is located in Parking Lot B at the corner of Cass Street and E. Grandview Parkway, offering flowers, herbs, vegetables, fruits, cheeses, meats, honey, and breads. The pet-free, smoke-free market accepts EBT/SNAP and Double Up Food Bucks.

name: Walled Lake Farmers' Market
season: Wednesdays, 3:00 p.m. to 7:00 p.m., May through October

name: Westland Farmers' Market
season: Thursdays 3:00 PM - 7:00 PM, late May through October (2026 season includes June dates 6/4, 6/11, 6/18, 6/25)
description: The Westland Farmers & Artisans Market is a Thursday-afternoon market at 1901 N. Carlson in Westland, Michigan, organized by the Westland Downtown Development Authority and the City of Westland Parks & Recreation. It connects local farmers with residents and features fresh produce, baked goods, artisan crafts, prepared foods, and local musicians. A Power of Produce Club gives children ages 4-12 $2 weekly tokens to buy fresh produce.

name: Almelund Farmers' Market
description: The Almelund Farmers' Market is held in the parking lot of Rod's Country Corner and offers locally grown vegetables, freshly baked bread and other baked goods, honey, maple syrup, flowers, and more.

name: At the Farm - Roadside Farmers Market
Goose Lake Artisan & Farmers Market
description: The Goose Lake Artisan & Farmers Market is a seasonal open-air market at 6760 213th Avenue NW near Nowthen, featuring local and regional artists, original artwork, craftsmen demonstrations, fresh produce, specialty foods, and more.

name: Isanti Family Farmers Market
description: Held at 17 Main Street West in Isanti, this market features locally and naturally grown fresh produce, a variety of food vendors, artisans, local acoustic musicians, weekly youth educational activities, and themed market days.

name: Kee Town & Country Centre's Farmers' Market
Kunnaris Farm Market
description: Kunnaris Farm Market in Virginia sells the freshest fruits and vegetables, fresh baked goods, pies, syrups, and salsas, with produce coming directly from their family farm in Gilbert as well as Amish and Mennonite farms across the Midwest.

name: Le Roy Farmers' Market
season: Thursdays 4:00 to 6:30 PM, roughly June through early October
description: The LeRoy Farmers' Market is an outdoor producers' market held on Main Street (MN Highway 56) at the main intersection of the business district in Le Roy, Minnesota, moving one block west to the LeRoy Community Center in bad weather. It is a producers' market where all items sold must be produced by the vendor, drawing both local shoppers and recreational visitors. The market reopened in 2022 after a COVID-19 suspension.

name: Lexington Farmers' Market
description: The Lexington Farmers' Market is held at 4175 Lovell Road in Lexington and offers organic foods, baked goods, arts and crafts, flowers, vegetables, jams, plants, and fruits.

name: Norm's Farm Market
season: Open 7 days a week (weather permitting), approximately late June through early September
description: Norm's Farm Market is a family farm stand at 10568 125th Ave SE in Becker, Minnesota, known for home-grown sweet corn and a wide range of in-season vegetables. Offerings include potatoes, melons, squash, pumpkins, tomatoes, and many other vegetables, plus jam, honey, maple syrup, and wild rice.

name: UMD Market Day in the Plaza
Watertown Farm & Craft Market
description: Established in 1977, the Watertown Farm & Craft Market is an open-air market at 300 Lewis Avenue North that directly connects farmers and crafters to consumers, offering food products and specialty goods locally grown, raised, crafted, or produced in and around Watertown.

name: Anacostia Farmers Market
city: 14Th Street Between U & V Streets Se Washington

name: FreshFarm Market by the White House
New Morning Farmers Market (Saturday)
city: 36Th Street & Alton Place Nw (Sheridan School) Washington

name: Stanton & Turner Open Air Farmers Market
Tenley Town Farmers Market @ Whole Foods Market
US Dept of Health & Human Services FRESHFARM Market
Ward 8 Farmers Market at St. Elizabeth's Hopital East
Serenbe Farmers Market
description: The Serenbe Farmers Market is a weekly pop-up where shoppers can buy produce grown at Serenbe Farms, located at 8715 Atlanta Newnan Road in Chattahoochee Hills. It runs on Tuesday afternoons.

name: 3rd Saturday Ferguson Winter Farmers' Market
season: Third Saturday of each month, November through April, 9:00 AM to noon.
description: The Ferguson Winter Farmers Market offers fresh produce, arts and crafts, coffee, and snacks, serving as a gathering place that fosters community in Ferguson and North St. Louis County.

name: Boone County Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays 8:00-11:00 a.m., May through October; Thursdays 3:00-5:30 p.m. starting July 2
description: The Boone County Farmers Market is a producer market in Columbia, Missouri, operating at two locations: the Columbia Mall parking lot (near Wendy's) and the Forum Christian Church parking lot at Forum and Nifong. It runs a Saturday morning market and a Thursday afternoon market.

name: Briarcliff Village Organic & Locally Grown Farmer's Market
Camdenton Farmers' Market
season: Wednesdays, May through September, 3:00 PM to 7:00 PM.

name: Cass County Farmers' Market
description: Cass County Farmer's Market offers fresh produce direct from the farm, with vendors selling locally-grown vegetables, melons, eggs, homemade breads and baked goods, plus handcrafted gifts and plants. It is held in the Family Center parking lot in front of the movie theater near I-49 and M-291 in Harrisonville, MO. Only growers and makers may sell; resellers are not permitted, and all items must comply with Cass County Health Department and Missouri state regulations.

name: City of Union Farmers Market
season: April through October on select Fridays, 4-7 pm
description: The City of Union Farmers Market is a community market organized by the City of Union Community Development Department, held on select Fridays from 4-7 pm at Oak & Main Street adjacent to Hansen Park in downtown Union, MO. Vendors sell produce, meat, cheese, eggs, plants, honey, jams and jellies, baked goods, salsa, freeze-dried treats, bath and body products, and artisan crafts including jewelry, quilts, pottery, and photography.

name: Farmers' Market of Laclede County
season: Wednesdays 11am-3pm and Saturdays 8am-1pm, April through October
description: Located in downtown Lebanon, this producer-only market in partnership with the Downtown Business District features locally grown produce, baked goods, meats, specialty items, and handcrafted goods, with vendors growing or making everything they sell.

name: Farmers' Market of Wildwood
season: Every Saturday, May through October, 8:00 AM to 12:00 PM.

name: Fenton Community & Farmers' Market
season: Mid-May through end of September, every Thursday 4:30-7:00 pm
description: The Fenton Community and Farmers' Market is organized by the City of Fenton Parks and Recreation and held every Thursday evening from 4:30-7:00 pm at the Meramec Greenway Pavilion in Fenton City Park, 995 Larkin Williams Road, Fenton, MO. Vendors showcase fresh and locally grown produce, prepared food, flowers, baked goods, and handmade crafts, with live music on the 1st and 3rd Thursday of each month.

name: Gladstone Farmers Market
description: Held in downtown Gladstone at The Heights at Linden Square, this market offers fresh produce, eggs, canned goods, bakery items, and more from local vendors.

name: Hermann Farmers' Market
street: 208 E. 1st St., First Bank parking lot

name: Joplin Farmers Market
description: The Joplin Farmers Market is a producer-only market in downtown Joplin, located in front of Joplin Memorial Hall. Vendors sell vegetables, fruits, meat, flowers, and plants, with all products grown or produced within a 50-mile radius of Joplin. The market includes indoor space and most vendors accept cash, credit, and debit.

name: Lincoln University Community Market
season: Saturdays 8:30am-11:30am in the summer; winter market first and third Saturdays 9am-11am, moving indoors when cold
description: Sponsored by Lincoln University Cooperative Extension, this community market in Jefferson City runs seasonally outdoors and moves indoors during the colder months.

name: Midtown Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 8:30am-12:30pm, May through November; first and third Saturdays in March-April; indoor winter markets second and fourth Saturdays 9:30am-12:30pm
description: Held at the Historic Market in the Loop on Delmar Boulevard in University City, this Saturday-morning market operates year-round with seasonal outdoor and indoor winter schedules.

name: Rolla Saturday Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 8am-12pm, April through October; winter market Saturdays November through March

name: Southwest Farmers' Market at Great Southern
St Clair Farmers Market
season: First and third Saturdays 8am-12pm, May through October

name: St. Charles Lions Club Farmers Market
West Plains Area Farmers Market
season: Tuesdays 2pm-6pm and Saturdays 9am-1pm, April through October; Saturdays 9am-1pm November through March

name: City of Gautier's 2nd Saturday Farmer's Market
season: May through October, 2nd Saturday monthly, 8:00 AM to 12:00 PM
description: The City of Gautier's 2nd Saturday Farmer's Market is held at George Martin City Park in Gautier, MS. Local vendors offer fresh vegetables, jellies, pickles, breads, pastries, honey, dairy products, and handmade goat milk soaps, with live entertainment. The market accepts cash and card, and some vendors accept SNAP, EBT, FMNP, and WIC.

name: Clinton's Fresh at Five Farmers Market
description: Fresh at Five is a weekly summer farmers' market held on the brick streets of Olde Towne Clinton, on Jefferson Street in front of City Hall in Clinton, Mississippi. The market features seasonal produce, milk and cheese, farm-fresh eggs, and other local goods from area farmers and vendors. It is organized by Main Street Clinton.

name: Earth's Bounty Farmers' Market
season: First Saturday of the month, April–November, 8am–noon
description: Earth's Bounty is a monthly market in downtown Meridian at The MAX (2155 Front Street) featuring fresh local produce, locally made products, and plants, alongside live music, kids' activities, and seasonal demonstrations. It was created to support local farmers and encourage the community to buy local, healthy foods.

name: Greater Belhaven Market
description: The Greater Belhaven Market is a Saturday farmers' market in Jackson, Mississippi, located in the parking lot at 904 East Fortification Street in the Belhaven neighborhood. It is run by the Greater Belhaven Foundation and offers fresh produce and goods from local vendors. The market accepts both cash and card payments.

name: Green Market at the Corinth Depot
season: Open 9am-3pm on market days (held at the Crossroads Museum / Corinth Depot)
description: The Green Market at the Corinth Depot is an open-air market in Corinth, Mississippi, hosting an eclectic mix of local and regional artists, farmers, gardeners, and craftsmen selling handmade and homegrown goods. Shoppers can find items from pottery and woodwork to local honey, food goods, clothing, soaps, and lotions, with food trucks and local entertainment on site. It is the banner fundraiser for the non-profit Crossroads Museum and was voted Best Small Event by the Mississippi Tourism Association.

name: McComb Farmers Market
description: The McComb Farmers Market is a seasonal open-air farmers market in downtown McComb, located at the downtown parking garage on North Railroad Boulevard.

name: Mississippi Farmers' Market
description: The Mississippi Farmers Market is a state-run market on High Street near the Fairgrounds in downtown Jackson, Mississippi, operated by the Mississippi Department of Agriculture and Commerce. The all-weather building offers 18,000 square feet with 32 stalls selling farm-fresh fruits and vegetables grown by Mississippi farmers. Attractions include Mississippi craftsmen and artisans, cooking demonstrations, live entertainment, and the on-site Farmers Market Cafe serving breakfast and lunch.

name: Mississippi Market
description: The Mississippi Farmers Market in Jackson is the largest of its kind in the state, an 18,000-square-foot all-weather building with 32 stalls featuring 100 percent Mississippi-grown, raised and processed products, plus crafts, cooking demonstrations, and an on-site cafe.

name: Natchez Farmers' Market
description: The Downtown Natchez Farmers Market is held every Saturday on North Broadway Street, featuring fresh produce, baked goods, fresh dairy, and arts and crafts from local growers and artists.

name: Newton County farmers market
season: Saturdays, late June through mid-September, 7:00 a.m. to 11:00 a.m.
description: The Newton County Farmers Market is held at ESCO Park on Highway 15 in Newton, near the baseball fields and walking track, with the Newton County Show Barn serving as the rain location. It offers fresh local fruits and vegetables and homegrown seasonal produce straight from the field.

name: Square Market
description: The Square Market is a seasonal market in historic downtown Batesville, Mississippi, organized by Batesville Main Street and held on the town square. The market is part of downtown Batesville's slate of community events, where visitors can shop and stroll the shops around the square.

name: The Pass Market
season: Year-round on Saturdays; May–October 8:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m., November–April 9:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m.

name: Water Valley Main Street Market
season: First Saturday of each month during spring and summer, 8 a.m. to 1 p.m.
description: Each first Saturday during the spring and summer, Railroad Park in Water Valley comes alive with a bustling farmers market under the grand magnolia trees, making it a delightful destination for both locals and visitors alike.

name: Annual Farmers' Market
season: Annual, held in late August/early September, 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM
description: The Annual Farmers' Market in Absarokee, MT is hosted by the Cobblestone Preservation Committee. It features a wide range of items such as produce, handmade articles, pieces of art, baked goods, and jams & jellies. The event is held at The Cobblestone in downtown Absarokee.

name: Bogert Farmers' Market
description: The Bogert Farmers' Market is a seasonal community market in Bozeman, Montana, showcasing locally grown fruits and vegetables, artisan foods, baked goods, local meats, handmade crafts, and ready-to-eat food, plus a beverage and libations garden. It is held on Tuesday evenings during the summer.

name: Clark Fork Market
description: The Clark Fork Market is a Saturday market in downtown Missoula, Montana, located at 225 S Pattee Street next to Caras Park. It operates outdoors from May through October and moves indoors (208 Ryman St.) from November through April, featuring local vendors and agricultural products with an emphasis on shopping local.

name: Deer Lodge Community Garden Pick-Your-Own Market
Dillon Farmers' Market
description: The Dillon Farmers' Market is a seasonal Saturday market in Dillon, Montana, held outdoors during the summer with local vendors offering produce and goods. The market is run by a volunteer board and recruits vendors each season.

name: Downtown Whitefish Farmers Market
description: The Downtown Whitefish Farmers Market gathers local farmers and craftsmen to showcase their products alongside live music, prepared food, and the season's freshest produce.

name: Great Falls Farmers' Market
description: The Great Falls Farmers' Market is a non-profit market in downtown Great Falls, Montana, with the mission of 'Growing Commerce Through Community.' Over 100 vendors participate weekly, selling fresh goods and merchandise. Only service animals (and approved ponies) are permitted, and it operates as a non-smoking area.

name: Missoula Valley Winter Market
description: The Missoula Valley Winter Market is an indoor Saturday farmers market featuring local produce, meats, baked goods, and handmade items, with live music and family activities throughout the cold season.

name: Apex Farmers Market
season: Saturdays year-round; 8 a.m. to noon (April–October) and 9 a.m. to noon (November–March)
description: The Apex Farmers Market is a non-profit, volunteer-led community organization committed to the health and wellness of the town and surrounding areas, held at The Depot in downtown Apex. It also provides education on food production, accessibility, sustainable living, and nutrition.

name: Blowing Rock Farmers' Market
description: The Blowing Rock Farmers Market is held on the lawn of the Chamber of Commerce building at 132 Park Ave. in Blowing Rock, NC. Local vendors offer fresh seasonal produce, local meats and eggs, artisan breads and baked goods, honey, maple syrup, handmade crafts, and prepared foods. The market is held rain or shine.

name: Burlington Farmers Market
