description: The Burlington Downtown Farmers' Market is a farmer-owned, producers-only market featuring locally grown produce and handmade artisan crafts and foods. It is held at the corner of S. Spring Street and E. Front Street in downtown Burlington.

name: Cape Fear Farmers' Market of Bladen County
Cary Downtown Farmers Market
season: Saturdays year-round; 8 a.m. to noon (April–October) and 9 a.m. to noon (November–March)
description: The Cary Downtown Farmers Market is a vibrant gathering place giving the community direct access to multiple farmers and unique craftsmen, with an emphasis on local sourcing and quality agricultural and craft products. It is held on Saturdays in downtown Cary.

name: Chatham Mills Farmers Market
description: Chatham Mills Farmers Market is a growers-only farmers market featuring a variety of fresh local products at Historic Chatham Mills in downtown Pittsboro, NC. The market offers produce, meats, eggs, baked goods, preserves, and handmade crafts from local vendors.

name: Claremont Tailgate Market
description: The Claremont Tailgate Market is a farmers market held in the Claremont City Hall parking lot at 3288 E. Main St. in Claremont, NC. Vendors offer locally sourced baked goods, craft items, eggs, herbs, vegetables, honey, jams, plants, prepared food, and trees/shrubs, including some USDA Certified organic products. The market has indoor space available.

name: Deans Farm Market
description: Deans Farm Market in Wilson, NC features products from their own farm and farming partners, with an on-site kitchen. They offer fresh local produce along with educational field trips and summer camps led by a master's-level educator, aiming to make a memorable farm-to-table experience for visitors.

name: Efland Farmers Market
season: April through warmer months, Saturday mornings 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM
description: The Efland Farmers Market is hosted by the Efland Ruritan Club, with vendors selling fresh produce and other goodies on the Ruritan club grounds off Hwy 70 in Efland, NC, between Efland Supermarket and Newbergs Automotive. It is a seasonal market focused on local growers and fresh produce.

name: Enka-Candler Tailgate Market
season: Thursdays, 3:30 p.m. to 6:30 p.m., May through October
description: The Enka-Candler Tailgate Market promotes local agriculture and heritage crafts in Western North Carolina, providing the community with fresh local produce and quality products. Run by the non-profit Asheville Farmstead School, it is held at the A-B Tech Enka Campus on Sand Hill Road in Candler.

name: Eno River Farmers Market
season: Saturdays year-round, April through November 8am-12pm; December through March 9am-12pm
description: Eno River Farmers Market in downtown Hillsborough offers farm fresh vegetables and fruit, cheese, pasture raised meat and eggs, wood fire baked goods, prepared foods and crafts from local growers and producers.

name: Farmers Market at Center United Methodist Church
Farmers Market at the Wallace Depot
Farmington Farmers Market
description: The Farmington Farmers Market is held at 1723 Farmington Road in Mocksville, NC. Local vendors offer a wide variety of items including fresh fruits and vegetables, baked goods, eggs, cheese, honey, jams, flowers, herbs, meat, plants, prepared foods, and crafts, including organic products.

name: Foothills Farmers Market
description: The Foothills Farmers' Market provides easy access to food produced in Cleveland County and the surrounding 50 miles, showcasing family-owned farms and small businesses. Vendors offer fresh fruits and vegetables, meat and poultry, baked goods, artisan cheeses, honey, nuts, herbs, and specialty foods, plus ornamental and edible plants and handmade arts and crafts. The main market is at the City Pavilion in uptown Shelby, NC.

name: Foothills Farmers' Market Kings Mountain
season: Year-round Saturdays 8am-Noon; peak season April 25 through September
description: The Kings Mountain Farmers' Market is the hometown market of Kings Mountain, North Carolina, operated by the Foothills Farmers' Market. It provides access to food produced in Cleveland County and the surrounding 50 miles, with family-owned farms and small businesses offering fresh fruits and vegetables, meat and poultry, baked goods, artisan cheeses, honey, nuts, herbs, ornamental and edible plants, and handmade arts and crafts. It accepts SNAP/EBT.

name: French Broad Co-op Wednesday Tailgate Market
Greensboro Farmers Curb Market
season: Saturdays year-round, 8:00am-12:00pm; Tuesday Weekday Market 4:00pm-7:00pm (April through October).
description: Greensboro's original farmers market, operating since 1874, is a covered indoor/outdoor market connecting farmers, food, and friends with ample parking, restrooms, a cafe, and winter heating.

name: Haywood's Historic Farmers Market
description: Established in 2008, Haywood's Historic Farmers Market is a producer-only market, featuring produce sourced from Haywood or an adjacent county, meats, fish, eggs, honey, dairy, flowers, nursery plants, preserves, baked goods, heritage crafts, and much more.

name: Huntersville Market
description: The Huntersville Growers' Market is held at Veterans Park at Main & Maxwell Street (103 Maxwell Street) in Huntersville, NC. The market brings local growers and vendors together to offer fresh, locally grown produce and goods to the community on Saturday mornings during the season.

name: Jones County Farmers Market
description: The Jones County Farmers Market in Trenton, NC offers local, fresh, seasonal produce from regional vendors. The market is located in downtown Trenton near the Civic Center and collaborates with a local cafe that sources produce from its vendors.

name: Market Square Farmers Market
description: The Market Square Farmers' Market is a long-running market in downtown Reidsville, North Carolina, giving local and regional farmers, bakers, cooks, and entrepreneurs a place to sell their goods. After 30 years it has expanded to include artisans and various food producers, with fresh produce, baked goods, prepared foods, and artisan items available. EBT cardholders receive double their spending amount (up to $10 daily) in Fresh Buck tokens.

name: Meeting Street Farmers Market at Morriosn Plantation
Meeting Street Market at Elon Park Elementary School
Midtown Farmers Market
description: The Midtown Farmers' Market is a vibrant farmers market in the heart of Midtown Raleigh, NC, located at The Commons at North Hills. It connects the community with local, family-farmed foods and nutritious options while providing educational opportunities about sustainable farming practices.

name: Mocksville Farmers' Market
description: The Town of Mocksville provides a community market for consumers, farmers, and artisans to buy and sell local fruits, vegetables, plants, and handmade crafts, with a focus on eating fresh, healthy, and local.

name: New Bern Craven County Area Farmers Market Inc.
description: The New Bern Farmers Market is located in downtown New Bern, North Carolina and offers local produce, plants, farm-raised meats, baked goods, and arts and crafts.

name: Oak Island Farmers' and Artisans' Market
Peachtree Farmers Market
season: Fridays, April through October, 3:30 p.m. to 6:30 p.m.
description: Now known as the Bermuda Run Farmers Market, this Davie County market gives consumers, farmers, and artisans a community space to buy and sell local fruits, vegetables, plants, and handmade crafts. It runs every Friday afternoon at 190 NC Hwy 801 South in Bermuda Run.

name: Piedmont Farmers Market/Concord
description: The Piedmont Farmers Market at 518 Winecoff School Road in Concord, NC is Cabarrus County's only year-round farmers market. It provides locally grown, fresh, high-quality produce and products while creating an economic outlet for local farmers, producers, and artisans across Cabarrus and surrounding Piedmont counties. The Saturday market features a diverse selection of vegetables, herbs, and artisanal goods.

name: Piedmont Farmer's Market/Harrisburg
description: The Piedmont Farmers Market operates a Harrisburg location as part of Cabarrus County's Piedmont Farmers Market network. During the growing season it offers locally grown, fresh, high-quality produce and products from local farmers, producers, and artisans in Cabarrus and surrounding Piedmont counties of North Carolina.

name: Pleasant Garden Farmers' Market
season: Early June through mid-September; Saturdays 7am–12pm and Wednesdays 3pm–6pm
description: The Pleasant Garden Farmers' Market is held at the Town Hall picnic shelter (4920 Alliance Church Road) in Pleasant Garden, NC. Local farmers and artisans sell fresh produce, baked goods, canned goods, wooden crafts, homemade jewelry, soaps and lotions, holiday decorations, hand-painted goods, gift baskets, and knit/crochet items. Admission is free and everyone is welcome.

name: Riverfront Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 8 a.m. to 1 p.m. (rain or shine)
description: Established in 2003, the Riverfront Farmers Market is Wilmington's longest-running farmers market, featuring locally grown, locally produced, and locally handmade goods. It is held on Dock Street between 2nd and Water Streets in historic downtown Wilmington.

name: Sandhills Farmers Market
season: Wednesdays 3 p.m. to 6 p.m. and Saturdays 10 a.m. to 1 p.m., mid-April through September
description: The Sandhills Farmers Market sets up at the Village Green sand parking lot on Village Green Road West in Pinehurst, bringing local growers and makers to the heart of the village. It runs on Wednesday afternoons and Saturday mornings during the warm-weather season.

name: Sandhills Farmers Market of Spring Lake
description: Located at 230 Chapel Hill Rd off Highway 87/Bragg Blvd, this market features fresh and local produce, crafts, local wares and artisans, along with storytelling sessions and educational demonstrations.

name: Shallotte Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays through September, 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM
description: The Shallotte Farmers Market offers locally grown fruits and vegetables along with handcrafted items such as pottery, jewelry, painted glass, and homemade breads, cakes, and cookies. Vendors also sell fresh eggs, herbs and plants, jams, jellies, pickles, honey, and fresh lemonade. The market is held in Shallotte, NC, with locations including Mulberry Park and Riverwalk.

name: Siler City Farmers Market
description: The market was established to serve local farmers and residents seeking fresh produce without traveling outside the county, with a 9:00am opening that allows vendors to harvest or bake that morning.

name: South Durham Farmers Market
description: The South Durham Farmers' Market is an outdoor producer-only market at Greenwood Commons Shopping Center in Durham, NC. Vendors are qualified farmers, prepared-food artisans, or craftspeople who reside and grow or create their products within a 50-mile radius. The market accepts SNAP and FMNP cards and participates in the Triangle Double Bucks program, matching customer dollars.

name: The Evening Farmers' Market
description: The Evening Farmers Market is a non-profit group of area farmers, bakers, and crafters in Statesville, NC, offering their products for sale for the convenience of early-evening shopping. It is held at Pecan Park in downtown Statesville. Products include locally grown food and goods from local producers.

name: The Friendly Market
description: The Friendly Market is the Crystal Coast's farmers market and nursery, offering high-quality packaged foods, North Carolina vendor items, and plants. It grew from a roadside farm stand into a community gathering space at 205 Friendly Road in Morehead City.

name: Union County Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 8 a.m. to noon, April through December
description: The Union County Farmers Market has served Union County for over 30 years, connecting local farmers and artisans with the community at 805 Skyway Drive in Monroe. It is open on Saturday mornings through much of the year.

name: Uptown Wadesboro Farmers' Market
description: The Uptown Wadesboro Farmers' Market gathers local growers and vendors at the Town Square in uptown Wadesboro. It is held on Thursday afternoons during the growing season.

name: Uptowne Market
season: Saturdays, 8:30 a.m. to 1 p.m., April through October
description: The Uptowne (High Point) Farmers Market is held at the High Point Public Library, 901 N. Main Street, featuring local fresh produce, seasonal plants and herbs, handmade crafts, and cooking demonstrations. It runs every Saturday through the growing season.

name: Wake Forest Farmers Market
season: Saturdays year-round; 8 a.m. to noon (April–October) and 9 a.m. to noon (November–March)
description: The Wake Forest Farmers Market runs every Saturday year-round in all weather, with local vendors selling fresh produce, prepared foods, crafts, and other goods. It emphasizes community connection and supporting local agriculture.

name: Watauga County Farmers' Market
description: The Watauga County Farmers' Market has served as Boone's town square since 1974, offering quality goods directly from local producers. Vendors sell local produce, fruits and berries, jams, ferments, honey, baked goods, eggs, goat cheeses, meats, herbs, microgreens, mushrooms, flowers, wreaths, and farm-based crafts such as wool, woodcarvings, jewelry, pottery, and metalwork. It is held at Daniel Boone Park, 591 Horn in the West Dr., Boone, NC.

name: Waynesville Tailgate Market
season: Wednesdays and Saturdays, 8 a.m. to noon, mid-May through the end of October
description: The Waynesville Tailgate Market brings Haywood County growers and makers together on Wednesday and Saturday mornings during the warm-weather season. It is a producer-focused market in Waynesville, separate from the town's Haywood's Historic Farmers Market.

name: Wendell Farmers' Market
season: First Thursday of the month, April through November, 5:30pm-8:30pm
description: The Wendell Market is a producer-only market on Campen Street in downtown Wendell that welcomes growers, harvesters, bakers, makers of prepared food, and artisans to sell directly to consumers.

name: Winton Farmers' Market
season: Fridays, 3 p.m. to 6 p.m., first Friday in May until the Friday before Thanksgiving
description: Established in 2012, the Winton Farmers Market was created by regional farmers as a community venue and is governed by Hertford County Cooperative Extension, the Town of Winton, and local food producers. It is held at 205 South Main Street in Winton.

name: Wrightsville Beach Farmers' Market
season: May through December, Mondays 8:00 a.m. to 1:00 p.m.
description: The Wrightsville Beach Farmers' Market gives residents and visitors the chance to buy fresh, locally grown produce alongside craft vendors. Held at the Wrightsville Beach Park softball field, it offers a welcoming community atmosphere through the warmer months.

name: Farmers' Market at Dike East Park
Corps of Discovery Welcome Center Farmers Market
Fremont Main Street Downtown Farmers Market
Grand Island Ace Hardware Farmers Market
Sunday Farmers' Market at College View
Bethlehem Local Works Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, 10 AM to 2 PM, June through September
description: The Local Works Farmers' Market at WREN is held on Main Street in Bethlehem, NH, offering locally sourced produce, meats, and other farm products from area vendors.

name: Bradford NH Community Farmers Market
season: Thursdays, 3 to 6 PM, June through the end of October, rain or shine
description: Local NH vendors present their wares on the front lawn of the Bradford Area Community Center, offering fresh produce, fruits, flowers, eggs, and meats alongside crafters working in fiber, wool, wood, pottery, and body care.

name: Canterbury Community Farmers Market
season: Outdoor: Wednesdays, 4:00 to 6:30 PM, first week of June through the last week of September; Indoor winter market: one Saturday in November, 10:00 AM to 1:00 PM
description: The Canterbury Community Farmers Market sets up in the parking area and meadow next to the Elkins Public Library in Canterbury's historic center, with an indoor winter market at the Canterbury Elementary School gym.

name: Danbury Winter Market
season: November through May, first Saturday of the month, 9am-1pm
description: The Danbury Winter Market is held on the first Saturday of each month from November through May at the Blazing Star Grange. Vendors sell locally made, grown, and produced products, with a hot breakfast and lunch served on site.

name: Deerfield NH Farmer's Market
season: Every 2nd and 4th Saturday, 11 AM to 2 PM, June through October
description: The Deerfield NH Farmer's Market is held at 9 Church Street, across from the Old Town Hall, featuring over 30 vendors along with live music and food.

name: Derry Farmers' Market
description: The Derry Homegrown Farm & Artisan Market is a volunteer-run, 501(c)(3) nonprofit market in Derry, NH, aiming to create a vibrant, inclusive marketplace and make wholesome local food choices accessible. It is held at 1 West Broadway in Derry with free parking, featuring locally sourced farm products and artisan goods. The market is registered with the NH Department of Agriculture and the NH Secretary of State.

name: Derry Homegrown Farm & Artisan Market
season: Wednesdays, 3 to 7 PM, June through September
description: Derry Homegrown Farm & Artisan Market is a volunteer-run nonprofit at 1 West Broadway in Derry, NH that aims to create a vibrant, inclusive marketplace fostering community connections and making wholesome local food choices accessible.

name: Derry Main Street Farmers' Market
description: The Derry Homegrown Farm & Artisan Market is a community farmers' market held at 1 West Broadway in Derry, NH, run by a volunteer board of directors as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit. It offers local farm products and artisan goods with an emphasis on wholesome, local food choices, plus free parking.

name: Dover Farmers' Market
description: The Dover Farmers' Market is run by Seacoast Eat Local, a nonprofit that connects local producers and consumers, with vendors offering fruit, vegetables, meat, seafood, eggs, honey, maple syrup, and baked goods.

name: Franklin Farmers Market
season: Thursdays, 3 to 6 PM, June through September
description: The Franklin Farmers Market is held at Marceau Park on Central Street in Franklin, NH, with vendors offering fresh produce, flowers and plants, dairy, maple, baked goods, meat, and more.

name: Great North Woods NH Farmers' Markets
description: The Colebrook Farmers Market is the Colebrook-area market within the Great North Woods region of New Hampshire, located at 43 Colby Street, Colebrook, NH 03576. It runs Saturday mornings during the summer and fall season.

name: Lisbon Farmers' Market
season: Thursdays, 3 PM to 6 PM, year round
description: The Lisbon Farmers' Market sets up in the lot next to the Sunoco on Main Street in Lisbon, NH, bringing local vendors to the downtown each week.

name: Local Works Farmers' Market at WREN
Nashua Farmers' Market
description: The Nashua Farmers Market is organized by the Downtown Nashua Association and held on the Nashua Riverwalk from Bicentennial Park to the Pavilion Stage in Renaissance Park, Nashua, NH. Shoppers find fresh produce, baked goods, flowers, and handcrafted items, with rotating local musicians and food trucks each week, and the market accepts SNAP via Granite State Market Match.

name: New Durham Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, 8:30 AM to 12:30 PM, mid May through mid October, rain or shine
description: The New Durham Farmers' Market is located off Depot Road next to the New Durham Post Office, offering locally raised chicken and rabbit, alpaca fiber and yarn, fresh vegetables, fruit and berries, plants, baked goods, fresh eggs, cut flowers, and local artisan pottery and jewelry.

name: Peterborough Farmers' Market
season: Wednesdays year round: 3 to 6 PM April through October (outdoors) and 3 to 5 PM late October through March (indoors)
description: Peterborough Farmers' Market bills itself as Fresh, Local, Friendly, offering the freshest local produce, meats, fish, eggs, baked goods, jams and jellies, and handmade artisan goods at Bowling Acres and the Community Center.

name: Portsmouth Farmers' Market
description: The Portsmouth Farmers' Market is run by Seacoast Eat Local, a nonprofit connecting local producers and consumers, with vendors offering fruit, vegetables, meat, seafood, eggs, honey, maple syrup, and baked goods.

name: Rochester Downtown Farmers' Market
season: Tuesdays, 3 to 6 PM, June through September, rain or shine
description: The Rochester Downtown Farmers' Market is held at the Rochester Community Center lower parking lot at 150 Wakefield Street, featuring vegetables, fruits, flowers and plants, dairy, maple, baked goods, meat, fish, soaps, and herbals.

name: Rye Farmers Market
season: Summer: Wednesdays 2:30-5:30pm, June-October; Winter: 3rd Saturdays 10am-2pm, November-March
description: The Rye Farmers Market is a seasonal open-air market at 580 Washington Road in Rye, featuring locally grown vegetables and fruits, bison, pork, beef, poultry, locally caught fish, lobster and more. A winter market continues on third Saturdays at Rye Junior High.

name: Seacoast Eat Local Winter Farmers' Markets
description: The Seacoast Eat Local Winter Farmers' Markets are made up of a variety of vendors selling fruit, vegetables, meat, seafood, eggs, honey, maple syrup, baked goods, prepared food, crafts, and body care products, where every vendor makes, grows, harvests, catches, or crafts 100% of what they sell.

name: Stratham Farmers' Market
season: Selected Saturdays, 10 AM to 1 PM, November through April
description: The Stratham Farmers' Market is a Seacoast Eat Local winter market at the Cooperative Middle School, 100 Academic Way, featuring over 40 food and craft vendors who make, grow, harvest, catch, or craft 100% of the products they sell.

name: Summer Farmers Market at Rolling Green Nu...
Sunapee Farmers Market
description: The Sunapee Farmers Market is an outlet for local farmers, craftspeople, bakers and producers of prepared foods. It is held every Saturday, rain or shine, at Ben Mere Park in historic Sunapee Harbor with plenty of free parking.

name: The We Are One Farmers Market
description: The We Are One Farmers Market in Weare, NH is a year-round market with online ordering, offering vegetables, prepared vegan meals, raw milk, organic breads and pastries, fruits, pork, beef, chicken, turkey, maple syrup, raw honey, fibers, and jewelry.

name: Warner Farmers Market
description: The Warner Area Farmer's Market is held at Warner Town Hall, 5 East Main St, Warner, NH 03278, every Saturday during the warm season, rain or shine. Vendors offer organic produce, fruits, maple syrup, mushrooms, eggs, teas, meats, jams, honey, soap, natural cosmetics, baked goods, jewelry, and flowers.

name: Wentworth Greenhouses Summer Farmers Market
description: The Wentworth Greenhouses Summer Farmers' Market in Rollinsford, NH features local vendors including Maine Herb Farm, Lasting Legacy Farm, Anderson's Mini Maples, Hackelboro Farm, Root Seller, NH Mushroom Co., and Small Town Girl.

name: Winter Farmers' Market at Cole Gardens
season: Saturdays, 9:30 AM to 12:30 PM, November through April
description: The Winter Farmers' Market at Cole Gardens in Concord, NH offers a selection of local goods including fresh produce, meat, dairy, bread, baked treats, soap, and crafts.

name: Blue Moon Acres Farm Market- Pennington, NJ
Collingswood Farmers' Market
season: First Saturday in May through the Saturday before Thanksgiving, Saturdays 8:00am-noon
description: The Collingswood Farmers' Market has operated since May 2000, growing from 12 farms to a large vendor base offering produce, meat, flowers, and local coffee alongside live music. It is held outdoors underneath the PATCO High Speedline between Collings and Irvin Avenue in Collingswood, NJ.

name: Cowtown Farmers' Market
description: Cowtown Farmers Market is a twice-weekly, year-round indoor and outdoor farmers market and flea market in Southern New Jersey, operating since 1926 with over 400 vendors. It is located at 780 Harding Highway (Route 40) in Pilesgrove, NJ, and the facility also serves as a rodeo venue.

name: Edison Farmers Market
description: The Edison Township Farmers Market is a seasonal community market held on Thursday afternoons at Swales Park, offering fresh local produce and goods to Edison residents.

name: Fort Lee Farmers Market
description: The Fort Lee Farmers Market gathers vendors from across Northern New Jersey to sell fresh produce, homemade baked goods and much more. The free market is held every Sunday at 1355 Inwood Terrace through the summer and fall.

name: Haddonfield's Farmers' Market
description: Haddonfield Farmers' Market is a weekly outdoor market at 2 Kings Court in Haddonfield, NJ, offering Jersey-fresh produce, food and drinks, baked goods, candles, and handmade accessories.

name: Holland Township Farmers' Market
season: Wednesdays and Saturdays, 9am-12pm, June through mid-September
description: The Holland Township Farmers' Market is held at the Holland Firehouse on Route 519 in Milford, featuring farm-fresh seasonal produce, eggs, herbs, baked goods, and artisanal crafts from Holland Township and Hunterdon County vendors.

name: Homestead Farm Market
description: Homestead Farm Market in Lambertville is a trusted source for fresh, locally grown produce and farm-made goods. Rooted in tradition and dedicated to quality, it offers fresh, local and handmade goods.

name: Lacey Township Farmers' Market
season: Fridays, 11am-5pm, late May through late September
description: The Lacey Farmers' Market is held at the Lacey United Methodist Church on Lacey Road in Forked River, featuring fruits, vegetables, cheeses, desserts, and Italian bread from local vendors.

name: Maggie's Ringwood Farmers' Market
description: Maggie's Ringwood Farmers' Market is an open-air market at 139 County Road 692 in Ringwood featuring farm-fresh fruits and vegetables, baked goods, fresh meats, and plants.

name: New Brunswick Community Farmers Market
description: The New Brunswick Community Farmers Market supports the development of a sustainable community by bringing healthy food from local farms to families and households across the city.

name: NOFA-NJ Farmers Market at Duke Farms
Pennsylvania Dutch Farmers' Market
description: The Pennsylvania Dutch Farmers' Market in Princeton offers a taste of Amish country, with vendors from local Amish communities in Lancaster County, PA since 1992. The indoor market sells baked goods, hot foods, fresh produce, poultry, seafood, meats, cheese, bulk foods, and handcrafted furniture, quilts and leather goods.

name: Point Pleasant Beach Farmers Market
description: The Point Pleasant Beach Farmers Market is held Sundays at the NJ Transit train station lot at Route 35 North and Arnold Avenue, offering fresh locally grown produce, handmade goods, and food trucks.

name: Rutgers Gardens Farmers market
description: The Rutgers Gardens Market (also known as Cook's Market) is held Fridays at 112 Log Cabin Road in New Brunswick, part of the Rutgers Community Farmers Market system bringing healthy food from local farms to area households.

name: South Orange Village Farmers' Market
description: The South Orange Farmers Market is a seasonal market held in the Sloan Street Parking Lot (12 Sloan Street) in downtown South Orange, NJ, across from the NJ Transit station. Organized by South Orange Downtown, it offers Jersey Fresh produce, fresh bread, baked goods, pickles and olives, kettle corn, and grab-and-go meals, plus rotating vendors selling body care items and giftable goods. The market hosts special programming including giveaways and family-friendly events.

name: Springfield Farmers' Market
description: The Springfield Farmers' Market is held Mondays on the lawn of the Springfield Free Public Library at 66 Mountain Avenue, offering fresh produce and baked goods from local New Jersey farmers.

name: Sussex County Farmers' Market
season: 2nd and 4th Saturday of the month, 9am-2pm, June through October
description: The Sussex County Farmers Market is held in the Farmer's Market Building at the Sussex County Fairgrounds on Plains Road in Augusta, offering Jersey Fresh and Sussex County-grown produce, meat, cheese, eggs, honey, and baked goods.

name: Thursday Organic Pavilion at the Columbus Farmers Market
UMDNJ-The University Hospital Auxiliary's Farmers Market
Washington Borough Farmers' Market
description: The Washington Borough Farmers' Market is held Saturdays at 44 East Washington Avenue and features fresh local produce alongside weekly themed events throughout the season.

name: West Orange Farmers Market
description: The Downtown West Orange Farmers Market is held Saturdays in the 80 Main Street parking lot (North End) and is sponsored by the Downtown West Orange Alliance.

name: Bosque Farms Growers' Market
season: Saturdays until noon, April through October (April starts 9 AM, remainder of season starts 8 AM)

name: Carlsbad Downtown Farmers' Market
season: Thursdays 5-8 PM (May-October) and every other Saturday 8-10 AM starting in June; closed in winter
description: A Carlsbad MainStreet project, the Farmers & Makers Market transforms the downtown courthouse lawn into a vibrant festival with local produce, live entertainment, food trucks, and youth activities seven months out of the year.

name: Cedar Crest Farmers' Market
description: A seasonal market located three miles north of I-40 on N Highway 14, the Turquoise Trail National Scenic Byway, in Cedar Crest.

name: Dixon Farmers' Market
description: The Dixon Farmers Market is a community market in downtown Dixon, NM, located at Zuly's Cafe (new location as of 2025). It offers fresh produce, greens, chiles, eggs, flowers, herbs, prepared foods, baked goods, ceramics and crafts, with live music. The market participates in the Double Up Food Bucks program, matching SNAP/EBT benefits up to $20 for produce purchases.

name: Farmers and Crafts Market of Las Cruces
First Street Market
season: Saturdays starting 1:00 PM until vendors sell out; spring through fall harvest, final market the first Saturday in October
description: A downtown Raton farmers' market on historic First Street offering locally grown foods and other locally produced products.

name: Gallup Farmers' Market
description: A seasonal market at the Gallup Downtown Courthouse Plaza featuring local farmers and produce from the region.

name: Hobbs Farmers' Market
description: A non-profit community market in Hobbs offering healthy organic produce, local vendors, unique handcrafted gifts, and community events while promoting a healthier, locally-focused lifestyle.

name: Jemez Springs Community Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays 9:00-11:00 AM, late April through early October

name: Mile-Hi Farmers' Market
season: Sundays 10 AM - 1 PM, late June through late September
description: A community-centered market in Albuquerque's Alvarado Park offering fresh produce, baked goods, handcrafted items, and prepared foods in a family-friendly environment.

name: Mimbres Valley Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays 10 AM - 12 PM, July through September
description: Operating since 2006, the Mimbres Valley Farmers' Market offers fresh locally grown food, giving growers better returns by eliminating middlemen and connecting buyers with healthy local produce.

name: Silver City Farmers' Market
products: Fresh fruits; Fresh vegetables; Baked goods; Bedding plants; Canned or preserved fruits/vegetables; Crafts/woodworking items; Cut flowers; Dairy products.; Eggs; Fresh/dried herbs; Honey; Juices/non-alcoholic ciders; Nursery stock; Nuts; Prepared foods; Red/other non-poultry meat and products; Soap/body care products

name: South Valley Growers' Market
season: Saturdays 8 AM - Noon, late May through mid-October; closed in winter
description: A seasonal farmers' market held at the Historic Gutierrez-Hubbell House in Albuquerque's South Valley.

name: E. Center St. Farmers Market & Street Celebration
Farmers' Market at Kahle Community Park
Farmers Market at The District
season: Year-round, every Thursday; summer hours 9:00 AM-1:00 PM, winter hours 10:00 AM-2:00 PM
description: The Las Vegas Farmers Market at The District at Green Valley Ranch is a year-round Thursday market offering fresh fruits and vegetables, artisan breads and pastries, and local artisanal goods at 2240 Village Walk Dr in Henderson.

name: Fresh52 Farmers And Artisan Market
season: Sundays, 8:30 AM-1:30 PM at St. Rose Corporate Center, 2580 St Rose Pkwy, Las Vegas NV 89123
description: Fresh52 Farmers' & Artisan Market is a lively open-air market operating at multiple locations throughout Southern Nevada, offering locally grown produce and handcrafted artisan goods.

name: fresh52 Farmers' Market
season: Year-round; varies by location (e.g., Tivoli Village Saturdays 8am-1pm, Sansone Park Place Sundays 8:30am-1pm)
description: Fresh52 Farmers' & Artisan Market is an open-air market operating at multiple locations throughout Southern Nevada, including Las Vegas and Henderson. Running for over a decade, it features local and regional vendors selling fresh produce, handcrafted artisan goods, and ready-to-eat foods. Locations include Tivoli Village (Saturdays), Sansone Park Place (Sundays), and Town Square (Fridays), among others.

name: Gardens Park Farmers' Market
description: The Gardens Park Farmers Market is a year-round community market in Summerlin featuring regional certified produce, breads, soaps, scents, artists and crafters. It runs every Thursday afternoon and accepts EBT, SNAP, cash and credit.

name: Las Vegas Farmers Market at Bruce Trent Park
Las Vegas Farmers Market at Floyd Lamb Park / Tule Springs
Sierra Chef Farmers Market @ Lampe Park
Berkshire-Rome Vendors' Market
description: Held in the parking lot on the 100 block of W. Dominick Street in Rome, the Berkshire-Rome Vendors' Market offers baked goods, cheese, crafts, flowers, herbs, vegetables, honey, jams, maple, plants and prepared foods.

name: Brewster Farmers Market
description: Brewster's only year-round farmers market, held at the Hudson Valley Regional Farmers Market on Mt. Ebo Road, featuring vendors offering vegetables, meat, baked goods, organic breads, prepared foods and Italian specialty items.

name: Canandaigua Farmers' Winter Market
season: November through May, every other Saturday 10:00am-11:00am
description: The Canandaigua Farmers' Market is an association of approximately 30 farmers and small-scale food processors who live within a limited radius of Canandaigua, NY, and is Ontario County's only farmer-run market. The winter market offers a variety of vegetables, fruits, flowers, meats, eggs, baked goods, sauces, pickles, jams, maple products and honey. It is held under the Mill Street Pavilion in historic downtown Canandaigua.

name: Capital District Farmers Market in Menands (Wholesale)
Carmel Farmers' Market at Lake Mahopac
season: June 7 to October 18, 2026, Sundays 9:00am-2:00pm (no market July 5 and September 6)
description: The Carmel Farmers' Market at Lake Mahopac is run by the Town of Carmel Recreation and Parks Department and is held outdoors at Chamber Park on South Lake Boulevard in Mahopac, NY. It runs on summer Sundays with local vendors.

name: Caroline Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 10am-2pm, first Saturday in May through last Saturday in October (plus some November/December dates)
description: The Caroline Farmers Market is held in the front truck bay of a former fire station, now owned by the Community Center, in historic Brooktondale. It features organically grown and farmers-pledge produce grown in Caroline or adjacent towns, plus local artwork, photography, crafts and prepared food.

name: Catskill Farmers Market
description: An open-air market held every Sunday rain or shine at 145 Water Street in downtown Catskill, offering local vegetables, fruits, meats, dairy, mushrooms, honey, flowers, crafts and baked goods with free parking next to Catskill Creek.

name: Central New York Regional Farmers Market (Saturday & Sunday)
Chappaqua Farmers Market
description: The Chappaqua Farmers Market is a community market at the Chappaqua Train Station (Allen Place) offering local fruits and vegetables, prepared foods, baked goods, fish, meats, spirits and more from over 40 vendors. It accepts SNAP/EBT with a matching program and features live music and kids' activities.

name: Cold Spring Farmers Market
description: Held outdoors every Saturday at Boscobel House and Gardens in Garrison, the market overlooks the Hudson River and scenic Constitution Marsh, offering breathtaking views of the Hudson Highlands.

name: Delaware Area Neighborhood Farmers' Market
description: A lively producer-only neighborhood market at the Delaware Avenue branch of the Albany Public Library, offering a variety of fresh produce and farm products affordably.

name: Down to Earth Mamaroneck Winter Farmers Market
description: The Down to Earth Mamaroneck Winter Farmers Market brings favorite farmers and food makers from the Larchmont and Rye markets indoors during the cold months, with locally grown fruits and vegetables, meats, seafood, pantry items and prepared foods.

name: Down to Earth Ossining Winter Farmers Market
description: Run by Down to Earth Markets at the corner of Spring and Main Streets in downtown Ossining, this Saturday market brings small-scale farmers and food makers to the heart of the village.

name: Downtown Farmers' Market
description: Held at Recreation Park on Binghamton's West Side with free, convenient parking, this Sunday market offers seasonal produce, baked goods and handmade items.

name: East Greenbush Community Library Farmers Market
East New York Farmers Market
season: Last week of June through mid-November, Saturdays 9:00am-3:00pm
description: The East New York Farmers Market is a community-run market of local gardeners, vendors and regional farmers selling fresh, affordable produce grown around East New York. It features craft and hot food vendors, cooking demonstrations, free books, music and special events at its Saturday markets.

name: Eastside Market
products: Fresh fruits; Fresh vegetables; Baked goods; Bedding plants; Canned or preserved fruits/vegetables; Crafts/woodworking items; Dairy products.; Eggs; Honey; Maple products; Mushrooms; Poultry/fowl meat and products; Prepared foods; Red/other non-poultry meat and products; Soap/body care products

name: Eden Farmers Market
description: Held at 2912 Legion Drive in Eden, this Tuesday market (rain or shine) features local farm produce, meats, dairy and cheeses, honey, maple syrup, baked goods, local artisans and food trucks.

name: Elizabethtown Farmers' Market
description: The Elizabethtown Farmers' Market is run by the Adirondack Farmers Market Cooperative, which supports small family farms and artisans. It is held on Hand Avenue in Elizabethtown, NY behind the Adirondack History Museum under the pavilion. Vendors offer farm produce, mushrooms, plants, crafts, and books.

name: Empire State Plaza Summer Farmers Market
Fairport Farmers' Market
description: The Fairport Farmers' Market is managed by the Village of Fairport and located off South Main St. in the Village Landing Parking Lot. It operates Saturday mornings during the warm-weather season.

name: Farm & Artisan Market at the Villa
Farm to Preschool Market: Mercy Cares for Kids
Farmers' Market at the Williamsville Mill
First NY Federal Credit Union Farmers' Market
Fort Edward Canal Street Marketplace Farmers Market
Fredonia Farmers Market
season: Saturdays; summer (mid-May through October) 9am-1pm, winter (November onward) 10am-1pm
description: The Fredonia Farmers' Market offers farm-fresh produce and local goods on Saturdays on Church Street in front of Village Hall, with an indoor winter market at the Masonic Lodge on East Main Street.

name: GOOD OLE DAYS Indoor Farm Market (Macedon Center)
Gossett's Farmer's Market
description: A year-round farmers market hosted at Gossett Brothers Nursery on Route 35 in South Salem, set outdoors among the nursery plants in the warmer months and moving into the greenhouse in late fall and winter.

name: Guilderland Farmers Market
description: Located at 457 NY-146 in Guilderland Center, the market offers farm-fresh goods from local farmers along with crafters, homemade beverages, cooked food, live music and vintage items.

name: Harvest Home Murray Hill Farmers Market
High School For Public Service Farmers Market
Horseheads Farmers Market
description: Known as 'Friday Nights at Thorne,' the Village of Horseheads evening farmers market is held at Thorne Street Park, featuring local food vendors, artisans, community organizations, live entertainment and family-friendly activities each week.

name: John Jay Homestead Farmers Market
description: The John Jay Homestead Farm Market is organized by the Friends of John Jay Homestead and held in downtown Katonah village at Lot 2 (190 Katonah Ave). It features certified organic and biodynamic produce, ethically-raised organic meat and eggs, seasonal fruits and vegetables, seafood, breads, and specialty items, rain or shine.

name: Joseph Addabbo Family Health Center's Farmers Market
Katonah/Lewisboro Farmers' Market
description: A friendly outdoor community market held on the grounds of John Jay High School in Cross River, offering farm-fresh fruits and vegetables, baked goods, meats and poultry, cheeses, plants and herbs, and prepared foods.

name: Kennedy Plaza Farmers' Market
description: The Kennedy Plaza Farmers' Market in Long Beach, NY is operated by LI Green Market Inc. and held at Kennedy Plaza (1 West Chester Street). It offers fresh locally grown produce, artisanal goods, homemade treats, handcrafted items, and flowers.

name: Lake Placid Farmers Market
season: Wednesdays, 9am-1pm, summer (early July through early September)
description: Bringing together local growers and makers in the heart of Lake Placid at The Oval on Main Street, this seasonal market offers fresh produce, artisanal foods, and handmade goods.

name: Lancaster Market
season: Saturdays and Sundays, 7am-1pm, May through October
description: An outdoor market at Broadway and Aurora streets in Lancaster where local farmers and vendors gather to sell fresh, locally grown fruits, vegetables, wine and produce during the summer and fall months.

name: Macedon Farmers Market
description: The Macedon Farmers Market is held at 32 Main Street in Macedon, offering fresh produce, fruit, maple syrup, fresh-cut flowers, crafts and homemade baked goods. It runs on Wednesday afternoons through the summer and fall.

name: Malone Farmers' Market
description: The Malone Farmers' Market is run by the Adirondack Farmers Market Cooperative, which supports small family farms and artisans. It is held under the pavilion at the Malone Town Office, across from Walmart on Rt 11.

name: Massena Farmers And Artisans Cooperative Market
Mount Morris Farmers Market
description: The Mount Morris 'Market on Main' is held at the Old Firehall at 119 Main Street, offering seasonal vegetables and fruit, farm-fresh eggs, honey, maple syrup, baked goods, plants, soaps and handcrafted items, with Friday-evening live music in summer.

name: New Paltz Farmers Market
description: A Sunday market in New Paltz offering local produce, fruit, meats, cheese, baked goods, coffee, flowers and more, located near the New Paltz Community Center.

name: New York Hall of Science Farmers' Market
North Amherst/ BNHV Farmers Market/ Amherst Museum
Oneida County Public Market
description: The Oneida County Public Market is a year-round farmers market in downtown Utica, NY, operating as a pop-up community rooted in history and good food behind Union Station at 321 Main Street. Vendors offer locally sourced food and goods, and the market runs a P.O.P. Kids Program and accepts SNAP and Double Up Food Bucks (DUFB).

name: Oneonta Farmers Market (Saturday)
description: A year-round market connecting the community with farmers and artisans who produce and raise their food, the Oneonta Farmers Market has operated for 50 years, moving between an indoor winter location and Huntington Memorial Park in summer.

name: Ontario Orchards Farms and Farmers Market
description: Ontario Orchards in Oswego features a farm market, bakery, cider mill, nursery and fresh fruit and produce farm, family-operated since the property was converted into a farm store in 1965.

name: Parker's Historic Cider Mill and Farmer's Market
Philmont Farmers' Market
description: The Philmont Farmers Market is a Local Food program of Philmont Beautification Inc. (PBInc) in Philmont, NY, run in collaboration with the Philmont Cooperative, a producer/consumer cooperative at 116 Main Street offering year-round local foods, a bakery, retail, commercial kitchen access, and a cafe. It connects a network of local farms and emerging food businesses within roughly 30 miles.

name: Pulaski Farmers' Market & Music in the Park
Rochester Public Market
description: For more than 120 years the Rochester Public Market, operated by the City of Rochester at 280 North Union Street, has been one of the city's most diverse and vibrant gathering places. Vendors offer fresh produce, meats and seafood, eggs and dairy, baked goods, beverages, ethnic and specialty foods, and general merchandise. The market accepts SNAP benefits and hosts 50+ special events annually.

name: Scarsdale Farmers Market
season: Sundays, May through November, 8:30 AM to 1:30 PM
description: The Scarsdale Farmers Market features a rotating selection of regional farmers, food artisans, and local craft makers, offering fresh produce, prepared foods, and handmade goods in the heart of downtown Scarsdale.

name: Schenectady farmers Market Ellis Health Center
Schenectady Greenmarket - Winter
season: Year-round, Sundays 10am-2pm; winter market indoors at Proctors Dec-March, outdoor market outside City Hall May-Nov
description: The Schenectady Greenmarket is a year-round community farmers market featuring local vendors selling produce, prepared foods, craft beverages, and artisanal goods. For the winter season the market moves indoors to Proctors at 432 State Street, while the outdoor season is held outside City Hall at 105 Jay Street. It accepts SNAP and runs food-box and FreshConnect assistance programs.

name: Schoharie Country Music Festival Farmers Market (Saturday)
description: Held at the Hall of Agriculture on the Schoharie County Sunshine Fairgrounds in Cobleskill, this Sunday market features a diverse lineup of vendors offering locally produced items alongside live music and family-friendly activities.

name: Scottsville Farmers Markets
description: The Scottsville Farmers' Market is held under the Farmer's Market Pavilion in downtown Scottsville, bringing local growers and producers to the village each Saturday from spring through fall.

name: Shiloh Community Farmers' Market - Grand Opening
Side Track Farmers Market
description: The Side Track Farmers Market on Route 31 in Historic Olde Clay is located on the original site of the old Cigarville Train Station next to the Clay Historical Park, in a mill originally built in 1921.

name: St. John's Church Farmers Market
description: Set at St. John's Church in Getty Square, Yonkers, this outdoor market offers farm-fresh fruits and vegetables, homemade pies and breads, jams and prepared foods alongside clothing, books and collectibles.

name: St. Josephs Hospital Health Centers Mobile Market
St. Vincent de Paul Church Farmers Market
Staten Island Mall Greenmarket
description: Conveniently located in a commuter lot at the Staten Island Mall with plenty of parking, this GrowNYC Greenmarket has served the Richmond neighborhood since 2009 with locally grown foods and family activities.

name: Sullivan Fresh Mobile Farmers Market at Wurtsboro
season: July through October, weekly summer and fall stops
description: The Sullivan Fresh Mobile Farmers Market is a Cornell Cooperative Extension initiative that makes affordable fresh fruits and vegetables available to communities across Sullivan County while creating a sustainable sales market for local farmers. Residents shop for fresh produce, join cooking demonstrations and connect with their community, and the market accepts SNAP/EBT and other incentive programs.

name: Sunday Putnam Market
description: The Sunday Putnam Market is held at Temple Beth Shalom on Route 6 in Mahopac, featuring local produce and other vendor products such as handmade soaps and lotions.

name: The City of Poughkeepsie Water Front Farmers' Market
description: The Poughkeepsie Waterfront Market is a weekly farmers market managed by the Mid-Hudson Discovery Museum at 75 North Water Street on the Poughkeepsie waterfront. It offers fresh, locally-sourced seasonal fruits and vegetables, meat, eggs, poultry, and baked goods, with complimentary onsite parking. The market accepts SNAP and WIC/FMNP benefits.

name: The Cross Roads Farm and Craft Market
description: A seasonal open-air market on County Road 21 between Westfield and Sherman, organized by a cooperative of dozens of vendors known for crafts, artwork, woodwork, imported gifts, collectibles, foodstuffs and home decor.

name: The Mall At Greece Ridge Farmers Market
Vestal Farmers Market
season: Wednesdays and Saturdays, late May through late November, 9:00am-1:00pm
description: The Vestal Farmers Market welcomes farmers, artisans and gardeners from across the region, featuring farm-fresh fruits and vegetables, baked goods, flowers and homemade items.

name: Village of Fredonia WINTER Farmers Market
Village of Tuckahoe Farmers Market
description: The Village of Tuckahoe Farm & Maker Market is a weekly community market organized by the Village of Tuckahoe, bringing together local farmers, food producers, and skilled makers offering fresh, high-quality goods. It is held at Depot Square (the train station parking lot) in Tuckahoe.

name: Walton Farmers' Market
season: Fridays, July through September, 9:30 AM to 2:30 PM
description: The Walton Farmers' Market is held by Cornell Cooperative Extension at Veterans Plaza in the Town of Walton, offering a weekly market through the summer season.

name: Waterford Harbor Farmers Market
description: Held at 1 Tugboat Alley by the Erie Canal Visitor Center in Waterford, the Waterford Harbor Market brings together local farmers, artisans and vendors with fresh produce, baked goods and handmade crafts plus weekly live music.

name: Watertown Mobile Farmers' Market - Curtis Apartments
Watertown Mobile Farmers' Market - Leray Apartments
Watertown Mobile Farmers' Market - Midtown Towers Housing
Watertown Mobile Farmers' Market - North Country Children's Clinic
West Point/Town of Highlands Farmers' Market
Westhampton Beach Farmers Market
description: The Westhampton Beach Farmers' Market is organized by The Greater Westhampton Chamber of Commerce and held at the Village Green, located between Mill and Beach Roads in Westhampton Beach. It hosts over 60 vendors, growers, and producers offering locally grown, caught, and homemade items including breads, cheese, free-range poultry, eggs, fish, flowers, fruits, herbs, honey, and vegetables. The market is held rain or shine.

name: White Plains International Open Air Farmers Market
season: Wednesdays, late April through late November, 8:30am-2:45pm
description: The White Plains International Open Air Farmers Market runs on Court Street between Main Street and Martine Avenue, where local farmers and vendors offer fresh produce, plants, home-baked goods, breads, honey, fish and prepared foods.

name: Winter Farmers Market at Circa Restaurant
Yonkers Down to Earth Market
description: The Yonkers Down to Earth Market is held at Van der Donck Park on Dock Street in downtown Yonkers, part of the Down to Earth Markets network of local farmers and vendors. It runs every Sunday from late spring through fall.

name: Euclid Community Farmer's Market
description: The Euclid Community Farmers Market is a seasonal open-air market at the Shore Cultural Center on East 222nd Street, offering locally grown and organic produce, baked goods, bedding plants, herbs, jams, honey, tea and more. It runs Friday afternoons from late spring through fall.

name: Farmers Market of Medina County
description: The Farmers Market of Medina County is home to over 25 local vendors offering fresh produce and locally-made foods from Northeast Ohio.

name: Mt Gilead Farmer's Market
season: Every other Saturday 9:00am-12:00pm, late May through early October
description: The Mt. Gilead Farmers Market is a community market held on the public square by the fountain in Mount Gilead, Ohio, with more than 20 participating vendors. Vendors sell meats, eggs, produce and vegetables, baked goods, crafts, natural products, and fresh flowers. Special event days include a 'Christmas in July' market and a 'Boo-tiful Harvest Bazaar.'

name: Mt. Washington Farmers' Market
season: May-October, Fridays 4pm-7pm (autumn hours subject to change)
description: Mt. Washington Market in the Park is a local farmers and makers market held at Stanbery Park in the Mt. Washington neighborhood of Cincinnati, Ohio. It features urban farmers and local makers, with live music on the first Friday of the month and family-friendly activities.

name: Ohio Valley Farmers' Market
description: The Ohio Valley Farmers' Market (established 1979) is a seasonal producer market offering fresh local produce, baked goods, and artisan crafts. For the 2026 season it operates Saturdays 9am-noon from May 30 through October 31 at the lower parking lots of the WVU Bellaire Health Center, 3000 Guernsey Street, Bellaire, Ohio. The volunteer board-run market encourages the growth and support of local farms and small businesses through workshops and educational opportunities.

name: Ralph's Joy of Living Farmers' Market
Union County Farmers Market - Richwood Location
description: The Richwood location of the Union County Farmers Market is a satellite of the OSU Extension-coordinated Union County Farmers Market, held at 4 East Ottawa St in Richwood, Ohio. It operates every Thursday from 3:30 to 6:30 pm, June through October. Vendors offer locally grown produce, baked goods, and homemade items fresh from their gardens, ovens, and woodlands.

description: The Cherokee Farmers' Market is a seasonal market in Cherokee, Oklahoma, organized by Cherokee Main Street and located at 121 E. Main St. Vendors offer fresh fruits and vegetables, salad greens, herbs, honey, watermelons, peaches, farm fresh eggs, meat, cut flowers, and Made in Oklahoma products. Most vendors accept cash, credit, and debit, and some accept SNAP/EBT, FMNP, and WIC.

season: Located at Central Park Multipurpose Pavilion 700 S. Broadway Ave., Moore, OK 73160. June 5th – August 28th, Saturdays, 8:00AM – 12:00PM

description: The Rogers County Farmers Market is an Oklahoma Grown Market, meaning everything you find is grown or made right here in Oklahoma, held in the Rogers County Courthouse parking lot in Claremore.

description: The Cleveland Farmers Market is held at Arlington Park at the intersection of Pawnee Street and Broadway in Cleveland, offering locally grown seasonal produce.

name: Guthrie Farmers' Market
season: Open every Saturday 8 a.m. to 12 p.m., June through September.
description: Hosted by the Guthrie Chamber of Commerce in cooperation with the City of Guthrie, the market features vendors with produce, meat, cheese, baked goods and crafts, with a different food truck each week.

season: Open Wednesday and Saturday, 7:30 a.m. to noon, June through September.

description: Hugo Farmers Market offers a wide array of fresh fruits and vegetables along with plant materials, located at 1509 E. Kirk in Hugo.

name: Muskogee Farmers' Market
season: Open Saturdays 8 a.m. to noon from the first Saturday in April through the last Saturday in October, and Wednesdays 8 a.m. to 1 p.m. from mid-May through mid-August.
description: At Muskogee Farmers Market, the focus is on real people selling goods they've produced with their own hands, at 425 W. Okmulgee Ave. in Muskogee.

season: Year-round; Summer (April-July): Saturdays 8am-1pm and Tuesdays 4-7pm; Winter (November-March): Saturdays 9am-1pm
description: The Norman Farm Market is Oklahoma's longest running farmers market, a mission-based market organized by Cleveland County to strengthen the local food system by supporting farmers and educating consumers about local, seasonal food. It operates year-round at the climate-controlled Cleveland County Wellness Square (The Well), 210 S. James Garner Ave., Norman, OK, and accepts SNAP, Double Up Oklahoma, and Senior Farmers Market Nutrition Program benefits.

season: Open every Saturday 8 a.m. to 12 p.m., April through October.
description: Held in historic downtown Poteau inside the renovated Town Square, the market's booths range from produce and crafts to outdoor furniture and health and wellness products.

description: The Ponca City Farmers Market is held at 14th Street and Lake Road at the Pioneer Woman Museum, offering locally grown produce and Made in Oklahoma products.

All vendors must grow or produce their goods in Payne County or the surrounding Logan, Lincoln, Creek, Pawnee, and Noble counties. Offerings include locally produced fruits and vegetables, beef, pork, chicken, lamb and goat, farm-fresh eggs, goat cheese, hand-crafted breads and jellies, cut flowers, herbs, seasonal plants, and goat milk soaps and lotions.

name: Allison Hill Farmers Market
description: The Allison Hill Farmers Market is held at the Heart of the Community Garden, 1421 Derry Street in Harrisburg, where local vendors offer seasonal produce such as tomatoes, peppers, sweet corn, kale, strawberries and squash alongside fresh herbs, honey and tea.

name: Anselma Farmers and Artisans Market
description: A weekly Wednesday producer market at the historic Mill at Anselma (1730 Conestoga Rd, Chester Springs), where local farmers and artisans sell their own food and handmade goods directly. The market sits on the site of a stone mill that has been grinding flour since 1747.

name: Artisan Exchange West Chester Indoor Market
Back Mountain Library Farmers' Market
description: The Back Mountain Library Farmers' Market is organized by Back Mountain Memorial Library board members to raise funds for the library, a tradition that began over 15 years ago at the library building itself. It has since relocated to the Dallas High School campus parking lot outside Mountaineer Stadium to accommodate growing demand. Vendors sell produce, meat, and other food products, with regulars including Brace's Orchard, Dymonds Farm Market, and Beta Bread Bakery.

name: Bellevue Farmers Market
description: A community market at Bayne Park (34 North Balph Ave., Bellevue) featuring fresh, healthy food and artisan goods alongside a rotating food-truck program during market hours.

name: Bethel Park Municipal Farmers Market
description: A municipal market held rain or shine in South Park, off Corrigan Drive near the ice-skating rink in Bethel Park, with ample parking nearby. Vendors sell fresh and prepared foods including baked goods, kettle corn, hummus, honey, cheese, maple syrup, locally made wine, and more.

name: Boscov's Fairgrounds Farmers' Market
description: Boscov's Fairgrounds Farmers' Market in Reading, PA features about 65 family-owned merchants offering locally grown produce and goods, and was voted best farmers market in Berks County for 2019. The market also hosts seasonal events, classes, and demonstrations. It operates year-round at 2934 N 5th Street Hwy.

name: Butler City Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays 8:30am–12:30pm, late May through end of October
description: The Butler City Farmers Market sets up at 205 South Chestnut Street in Butler (behind the courthouse, across from Rotary Park), with vendors selling fresh produce like corn, tomatoes, peppers and lettuce plus grass-fed beef, pork, eggs, baked goods, honey, jams and cut flowers.

name: Carlin Farm/4 Seasons Farm Market/Carlin Farm
Central Market
description: Lancaster Central Market, housed in an 1889 Romanesque Revival market house at 23 North Market Street, is one of the country's oldest continuously operated public farmers' markets, with standholders selling local produce, meats, dairy, baked goods, prepared foods, flowers and herbs.

name: Clarion Farmers' Market
description: A producer market established in 1986, held in the parking lot next to the historic Clarion County Courthouse just off Main Street in Clarion. Vendors offer produce, meat, eggs, jams, baked goods and more.

name: Corn Crib Main Street Market
description: Corn Crib Main Street Market at 35 W. Main Street in Mount Joy is a small market offering local farm-fresh produce, flowers, baked goods, deli meats and salads alongside crafts and handmade items from area vendors.

name: Downtown Lititz Farmers Market
description: The Downtown Lititz Farmers Market gathers at 24 North Broad Street in Lititz, where vendors offer seasonal produce, handcrafted goods and treats alongside live music in the heart of downtown.

name: Emmaus Farmers' Market
season: Sundays 10am–1pm, May through November; winter market Sundays 10am–12pm in December and on the 2nd and 4th Sundays January through April
description: The Emmaus Farmers' Market, established in 2003, is held rain or shine at Triangle Park in Emmaus, offering locally produced fresh produce and other goods from area growers.

name: Farmers Market at Morgan Log House
Farmers' Market Cooperative of East Liberty
Farmers Market on 4th
description: The New Cumberland Farmers' Market on 4th is organized by the New Cumberland Business & Professional Group and held at 4th and Bridge Streets in the Olde Towne Commons parking lot. Vendors offer locally grown produce, fresh meats, homemade baked goods, flowers, plants, jams, cider, and handcrafts from local artisans and makers.

name: Farmstead Farmers Market
description: A year-round market built on a five-acre farmette with a restored original barn at 1976 Laudermilch Rd, Palmyra. Vendors sell baked goods, chocolates, honey, produce, prepared foods, and pet supplies.

name: Fox Chapel Farmers' Market
description: The Fox Chapel Farmers Market is held at Shady Side Academy's Senior School campus at 423 Fox Chapel Road in front of the McIlroy Center for Science and Innovation. It features locally sourced seasonal produce, food and specialty items from farms and vendors within roughly a 120-mile radius of campus.

name: Frankie & May Farmer's Market in the Courtyard
Fresh Marketplace @ New Wilmington Center
description: A Saturday market at the New Wilmington Center featuring organic vegetables, local farmers, artist vendors, breakfast and lunch options, and a community dining area, with live morning music each week throughout the season.

name: Green Tree Farmers' Market in the...
Hatboro Farmers' Market
season: 1st and 3rd Fridays, 5:30pm until dusk, May through October
description: The Hatboro Farmers' Market is a seasonal open-air market at 32 N. York Road in Hatboro, with vendors selling locally grown and made products alongside food vendors, live music and family activities.

name: Kutztown Main Street Growers Market
season: Thursdays 3:30pm–7pm, June through end of October
description: The Kutztown Main Street Growers Market is a seasonal open-air market in downtown Kutztown where local growers and producers sell fresh-picked produce and other locally made goods on Thursday afternoons.

name: Lancaster Central Market
description: Lancaster Central Market, established in 1730, bills itself as America's oldest continuously operated public farmers market and is managed by the Lancaster Central Market Trust at 23 N. Market Street in downtown Lancaster. Standholders sell fresh local produce, meats, dairy, baked goods, ready-made dishes, flowers, and gifts.

name: Lebanon Farmers Market
description: The Lebanon Farmers Market at 35 S. 8th Street in Lebanon offers fresh produce, meats, candies, pastries, hot pretzels, and a coffee bar, with several on-site restaurants. It features wide aisles and local vendors.

name: Lititz Farmers' Market
description: The Downtown Lititz Farmers Market is an outdoor community market organized by Venture Lititz and held at Lititz Springs Park. It offers seasonal produce, handcrafted goods, and treats alongside live music.

name: Local Organic Market
season: Mondays 11am–6pm; artisan bread and limited produce also Fridays 11am–3pm
description: Local Organic Market in Kutztown sources local, organic food from independent farms within 150 miles, offering weekly orders of organic meat, eggs and cheese plus morning-baked artisan breads and seasonal organic produce from several area farms.

name: Market and Penn Street Farmers Market
Millheim Farmers' Market
description: A producer-only market with about 15 local vendors, held in a former storefront on Main Street in downtown Millheim. Offerings include vegetables, fruits, flowers, herbs, cider, maple syrup, pastured poultry and beef, eggs, cheeses, baked goods, and soaps.

name: Monroeville Lions Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays 9am–12pm, from the Saturday before Mother's Day through the Saturday before Thanksgiving
description: The Monroeville Lions Farmers' Market is held at Monroeville Mall (between Dick's and Best Buy), where vendors sell locally grown and handmade items and play an active role in running the market.

name: Mt. Lebanon Lion's Club Farmers' Market
New Eastern Market
description: New Eastern Market at 201 Memory Lane in York is an indoor farmers market offering farm-fresh produce, baked goods, meat, fish, deli items, and dining options. It was voted one of the 2020 Best of York County for Local Produce and Fruit.

name: New Freedom Farmers' Market
season: Every other Saturday 9am–1pm, May–October (winter market first Saturday of December, February, March and April)

name: Ngozi Lets Get Dirty! Farmers Markets Festivals & Events
Norristown Farmer's Market
description: A market in the center of Norristown on Main Street between Swede and DeKalb (113 East Main Street), with indoor space and street parking. Vendors including Lone Wolf Farm offer fresh fruits and vegetables, meats, flowers, and baked goods.

name: Olde Town Grove City Farmers' Market
Oxford Circle Farmers' Market
description: The Oxford Circle Farmers' Market is hosted by the Oxford Circle Mennonite Church at 900 E. Howell Street in Northeast Philadelphia and is operated by The Food Trust in partnership with the Oxford Circle Christian Community Development Association. Established in 2004, it brings fresh, locally grown produce to one of the city's most ethnically diverse neighborhoods. The market accepts WIC and Senior Farmers' Market Nutrition Program vouchers and distributes Food Bucks.

name: PA Dutch Farmers' Market of Wyomissing
description: The PA Dutch Farmers' Market of Wyomissing is a local indoor market at 845 Woodland Road in Wyomissing, PA, between Best Buy and the Shoppes of Wyomissing. It offers farm-fresh produce, baked goods, meats, cheeses and regional specialties, plus a food court serving breakfast, lunch and dinner.

name: Paulus Farm Market
description: Paulus Farm Market at 1216 S. York Street in Mechanicsburg sells locally grown fruits and vegetables, home-raised beef and fresh-cut meats, baked goods, deli items, and seasonal plants, with pick-your-own flowers and pumpkins and a farm park on site.

name: Pennsylvania Farmers' Open Air Market
season: Tuesdays and Fridays 9am–4pm, May through November, rain or shine
description: The Pennsylvania Farmers' Open Air Market at 2300 N. Cameron Street in Harrisburg is a producers-only market operating since 1974, with 25-plus vendors selling fresh-picked fruits and vegetables, European-style breads, pasture-raised meats, poultry, eggs, cheese and baked goods.

name: Phoenixville Farmers' Market
description: The Phoenixville Farmers' Market is held outdoors at 200 Mill Street in Phoenixville, PA, under the Gay Street Bridge along the French Creek Trail. Local producers sell hand-picked produce, pasture-raised eggs, baked goods, artisan foods and artwork directly to shoppers. The dog-friendly market features live music and a children's area.

name: Quakertown Farmers' Market
description: The Quakertown Farmers' Market at 201 Station Road in Quakertown, PA, features more than 100 departments selling fresh local produce, meats, baked goods, crafts and household goods. It is a long-running indoor market open three days a week year-round.

name: Sideling Hill Service Plaza Farmers' Market
description: A farmers' market located at the Sideling Hill Service Plaza along the Pennsylvania Turnpike (Milepost 172.4) in Fulton County, serving travelers at 3744 N Hess Rd, Waterfall.

name: Simeral Square Farmers Market
season: Every other Saturday, June through September
description: The Simeral Square Farmers Market is held at 101 N. Water Street in West Newton, offering fresh locally sourced produce and artisanal goods from area vendors, with indoor space available.

name: Strite's Orchard Farm Market and Bakery
description: Strite's Orchard Farm Market and Bakery at 1000 Strites Road in Harrisburg sells fresh vegetables, baked goods, dairy, honey and soups, with U-pick blueberries and CSA memberships, on a farm with over 100 years of family farming.

name: Summer Marketplace at Mohegan Sun Arena
description: The Summer Marketplace at Mohegan Sun Arena at Casey Plaza (255 Highland Park Blvd., Wilkes-Barre) features vendors selling fresh produce, food, crafts, jewelry and clothing, with free parking and free admission.

name: Sunbury Market House
description: The Sunbury Market House, built in 1897 at 436 Market Street in downtown Sunbury, is one of the largest indoor markets in the Susquehanna Valley, with vendors offering fruits, vegetables, baked goods, meats, crafts and a lunch stand.

name: The Minted Market, Grampian's Community Farmers'...
description: The Minted Market is Grampian's community farmers market at 258 Main Street, offering local honey and maple syrup, homemade breads and baked goods, organic produce, farm-raised pork and local meats, and goat-milk soaps, with live local musicians and an annual fall festival.

name: Trexlertown Farmers' Market
description: A producer-only, family- and pet-friendly market held rain or shine at Grange Park (360 Grange Rd). Vendors sell fresh fruits and vegetables, local honey, locally roasted coffee, fresh baked goods, and more.

name: Vandergrift Farmers' Market
season: Thursdays, June through October, 4:00 PM to 6:30 PM
description: The Vandergrift Farmers' Market is a weekly outdoor market with the motto "Always Fresh . . . Always Local!" It offers fresh fruits and vegetables, locally raised meats, breads, baked goods, maple syrup, hot foods, and more. The market is held in the Columbia Avenue parking lot in Vandergrift.

name: Waynesburg Farmers' Market
description: The Waynesburg Farmers' Market is held in downtown Waynesburg, PA, near the Courthouse, giving area residents the chance to buy fresh, locally grown fruits, vegetables, flowers and herbs directly from the farmers and gardeners who grow them. Vendors also offer baked goods, meats, eggs and wine.

name: West Reading Farmers' Market
description: A year-round, producer-only outdoor market on Penn Avenue in West Reading where every vendor is a hand-picked small farmer or food business located within 50 miles. Offerings include seasonal produce, pastured meats, poultry and eggs, cheeses, raw local honey, artisan breads and pastries, fresh flowers, and locally roasted coffee.

name: West Shore Farmers' Market
description: The West Shore Farmers Market is an indoor market at 900 Market Street in Lemoyne, PA, that first opened in 1950 and moved into its current two-story, 60,000-square-foot facility in 2000. The lower-level farmers' market houses roughly 50 vendors selling fresh fruits and vegetables, seafood, meat and prepared foods.

name: Williamsport Growers' Market
description: The Williamsport Growers' Market is a producer-only farmers market in downtown Williamsport, held in the parking lot at Little League Boulevard and Pine Street. It features more than 20 vendors selling fresh, local produce and goods.

name: Sankofa Market
products: Fresh fruits; Fresh vegetables; Fresh/dried herbs

description: The Anderson County Farmers Market is an open-air, handicap-accessible market at Murray Avenue & Tribble Street in Anderson, with a May Craft Market and December Holiday Market on Saturdays.

name: Ashland United Methodist Church Farmers Market
Cheraw Farmers' Market

description: The Landrum Farmers Market is a community market at 221 W. Rutherford Street featuring farm vendors, a children's Little Sprouts Garden Club, and a holiday craft fair in November and December.

name: Soda City Market
description: Soda City Market is Columbia's weekly producer-only street market held along Main Street (blocks 1300-1700), inspired by European street markets. It features an average of 200 vendors per week and over 400 unique vendors annually, all producing in the Southeast. Offerings include locally-grown produce, handcrafted goods, art, baked goods, cultural foods, beverages, candles, jewelry and vintage items.

name: Sunday Brunch Farmers Market
description: The Sunday Brunch Farmers Market is a year-round Sunday market at 1977 Maybank Hwy on James Island (behind The Pour House), gathering local farmers, makers, and artisans.

name: Travelers Rest Community Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, May through September, 8:30 AM - 12:00 PM
description: The Travelers Rest Farmers Market is an independently run, nonprofit market at Trailblazer Park with 70+ vendors offering fresh produce, local goods, and artisan products.

name: Tree of Life Congregation Farmers Market

Virginia Wingard United Methodist Farmers' Market
Washington Street United Church Farmers Market

name: Freeman Farmers' Market

description: Local farmers present fresh naturally grown produce, farm-fresh eggs, dairy and meat, while bakers, makers, and artists offer artisan breads, sweets, home health and beauty items, and handmade crafts at this family-friendly community market held at Grace Episcopal Church.

name: Cookeville Green Market

description: The Cookeville Green Market is a year-round, producer-only, member-run farmers market featuring organically grown produce and local artisans' goods.

name: Dixie Lee Farmers Market
description: Home of the Dixie Lee Farmers Market in Farragut, offering locally grown produce, grass-fed meats, handcrafted goods, and artisanal baked products from regional vendors.

name: Downtown Green Market - Cookeville
season: Saturdays, 8:00am-12:00pm (spring/summer/fall); winter Saturdays 9:00am-12:00pm; third-Thursday night market 5:00pm-8:00pm
description: The Cookeville Downtown Green Market is a year-round, producer-only, member-run farmers market featuring organically grown produce and local artisans' goods.

name: Farm Market - Trenton TN

name: Farmers Market at the Livery Stables
Goodlettsville Farmers' Market
