description: The Goodlettsville Farmers & Artisan Market gathers local farmers, bakers, makers, and artisans offering fresh food and handmade goods on the grounds of the Visitor's Center at Moss-Wright Park.

name: Greeneville Farmers' Market
description: Greeneville Farmers Market is described on its own site as Greene County's oldest, established farmers market, offering fresh, locally-produced foods alongside work from local artists and direct-sales vendors. It operates through the Locally Grown online ordering platform.

name: Johnson City Farmers' Market
description: The Johnson City Farmers Market operates at the Pavilion in Downtown Johnson City, offering local produce and goods during its seasonal Saturday hours.

name: Johnson County Farmers' Market
description: Johnson County Farmers Market is a nonprofit, producer-only market located in Ralph Stout Park in Mountain City, TN, providing access to locally-produced food, meat products and handmade crafts to Mountain City and surrounding areas.

name: Kingston Springs Farmers and Artisans Market
season: Saturdays, 9:00 AM to noon, late May through late August
description: The Kingston Springs Farmers and Artisans Market offers a source for fresh, local produce and hand-crafted goods in Kingston Springs, Tennessee, serving as a community gathering place featuring items made by local residents.

name: Loretto Farmers' Market
description: Loretto Farmers Market was established in 2018 to provide a venue for customers to shop from local growers, craftsmen and bakers, featuring produce, meat, honey, flowers, handmade crafts, and homemade goods.

name: Main St Farmers Market
description: Located on the corner of W. 20th St. and Chestnut St. in Chattanooga, this vendor-run market features locally-sourced food from farms within 100 miles of downtown that practice environmentally sustainable methods.

name: Market Park Farmers' Market of Athens
Millington Farmers' Market
season: 2nd and 4th Tuesdays in June and July, 5:00pm-7:00pm
description: The Millington Farmers Market brings together local makers, growers, and producers in the heart of downtown Millington.

name: Nolensville Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, 8:00am-12:00pm, May through October; winter market November through April, Saturdays 9:00am-12:00pm
description: A producer-only, year-round market at the Historic Nolensville School offering organic and naturally grown produce, farm-fresh eggs, meats, baked goods, and artisanal crafts.

name: North Nashville Farmers Market at Davidson Farmers Co-op
Northside Farmers' Market on Mississippi
description: The Northside Farmers Market on Mississippi Avenue offers non-certified organic and sustainably grown fruits and vegetables, locally roasted coffees, woodland-raised heirloom pork and chicken, and grass-fed beef.

name: Onion County Farmers Market
description: The Obion County Farmer's & Artisan's Market promotes local farmers and artisans in downtown Union City, offering produce, baked goods, free-range eggs, and local grain-fed beef to support local growers.

name: Ooltewah Farmers Market
description: The Ooltewah Farmers Market is a community service initiative by Ooltewah Nursery, operating since 2013 to provide access to fresh, local foods while supporting small family farms. It is held every Thursday afternoon at the nursery in Ooltewah, TN.

name: Pikeville Farmers Market
description: The Pikeville Farmers Market features freshly harvested produce, farm-raised eggs, melons, sweet corn, and homemade baked goods alongside handcrafted items like jewelry, pottery, and artwork from local artisans.

name: Richland Park Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, 9:00am-12:00pm (April through December); January through March, 10:00am-12:00pm
description: A Nashville farmers market at the historic Richland Park location with 80+ peak-season vendors offering pasture-raised local meat, farmstead cheese, fresh organic vegetables, sourdough bread, baked goods, and international fare.

name: Rutherford County Farmers' Market 2021
season: May (second Friday) through October, Tuesdays & Fridays 7am-1pm; November-April Fridays only 8am-12pm
description: The Rutherford County Farmers' Market is a producer-only market held in the Community Center at Lane Agri-Park in Murfreesboro, TN. Vendors from over a dozen Middle Tennessee counties sell seasonal fruits, vegetables, meats, eggs, baked and canned goods, flowers, plants, shrubs and trees.

name: Springfield-Robertson County Farmers' Market
description: The Springfield-Robertson County Farmers Market is held at the Robertson County Fairgrounds, where all items sold are grown or produced on Robertson County farms.

name: St. Albans Hixson Farmer's Market
description: Held on the grounds of St. Albans Episcopal Church in Hixson, this Saturday market transforms the church grounds into a hub of local goodness featuring fresh produce, handmade goods, and community gardening.

name: The Farm Market Day
season: Second Saturday of each month, April through October
description: Held the second Saturday of the month at The Farm community in Summertown, Market Day offers fresh produce, baked goods, arts and crafts, yard sale items, and live music.

name: Thompson's Station Farmers' Market
description: Thompson's Station Farmers Market is a producer-only market featuring produce, meat, eggs, cheese, and crafts from local farms and makers.

name: 6701 Burnet Road Market
season: Thursdays 4:00-8:00 PM and Saturdays 9:00 AM-1:00 PM, year-round
description: A Texas Certified Farmers' Market at 6701 Burnet Road in Austin offering on-site parking, restrooms, a kids' activity area, covered picnic area, and live music.

name: All Natural and Certified Organic Farmers Market (HOPE farm)
Angelina County Farmers' Market
description: A farmers market on South Medford Drive in Lufkin serving Angelina County and the surrounding East Texas area.

name: Arlington Foodies Farmer's Market.
season: 4th Saturday Jan-May 2-6pm, Jun-Sep 5-9pm; 3rd Saturday Nov 2-6pm, Dec 5-9pm
description: The Arlington Foodies Farmer's Market is held at Bicentennial Park, 3206 Smith Barry Rd in Pantego, TX, with over 60 vendors. It runs on select Saturdays throughout the year.

name: Athens Farmers Market
description: The Athens Farmers Market provides the local community with fresh, local, seasonal produce from area farmers plus baked goods and hand-crafted artisanal items. It is held on Saturdays at 100 Cardinal Drive in Athens, TX.

name: Barton Creek Farmers Market
description: Barton Creek Farmers Market is Austin's original farmers market, located at the back of Barton Creek Mall at 2901 S. Capital of Texas Hwy. Over 90% of vendors sell what they grow or produce, including grass-fed meats, organic produce, artisan cheeses, prepared foods and handmade crafts.

name: Bayou City Farmers Market
description: Part of Urban Harvest's Houston farmers market program championing fresh, real food, with all vendors sourcing their products from within 180 miles of Houston.

name: Baytown Farmers Market
season: Seasonal, select Saturdays 10am-2pm (Spring: Mar 21, Apr 18, May 16; Fall: Sep 19, Oct 17, Nov 21, 2026)
description: The Market at Town Square is an outdoor community market run by the City of Baytown Parks & Recreation at 213 West Texas Avenue, featuring local vendors with hand-made and hand-crafted merchandise, food trucks, live music, and children's activities. It operates rain or shine.

name: Bedford Farmers Market
description: An indoor market in Bedford featuring locally sourced meats and seasonal produce, plus specialty foods and plants.

name: Bell County Farmers Market Association II
Brazos Valley Farmers Market
season: Open Saturdays year-round, 8:00 AM to noon, rain or shine
description: A year-round Saturday farmers' market in Bryan that supports regional farms by connecting consumers directly to local food producers and educating the public on the benefits of locally produced food.

name: Burleson Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 8:00 AM-1:00 PM March through October; once monthly November through February

name: Charlie Thornberry Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 9:00 AM-1:00 PM January through April; Tuesdays and Saturdays 8:00 AM-1:00 PM May through September; Saturdays 9:00 AM-1:00 PM October through December
description: A downtown Wichita Falls market providing healthy, fresh produce, non-commercial foods, and artisan wares while supporting both small and large farmers.

name: Collin County Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays 9:00 AM-1:00 PM, March through November
description: A seasonal Saturday farmers market held at the City Hall Complex in Murphy, Texas, offering locally grown produce and goods.

name: Community Fresh Farmers' Market
description: A year-round Saturday farmers market in College Station offering fresh local produce and farm goods at 2275 Dartmouth Street.

name: Coppell Farmers Market
description: The Coppell Farmers Market provides farmers and makers a venue to sell locally grown and produced food, and serves as a community gathering place that increases access to clean, fresh food for Coppell residents. Vendors sell fresh produce, meat, eggs, dairy, honey, bread, baked goods, pantry items, flowers, plants, and prepared foods.

name: Cowtown Farmers Market
season: Open year-round; Saturdays 8:00 AM-12:00 PM, with smaller Wednesday markets mid-June through mid-August during peak season
description: Cowtown Farmers Market is Tarrant County's only producer-only farmers market, operating year-round since 1986 in Fort Worth, Texas. It features exclusively locally-produced foods from vendors within 150 miles. The market accepts SNAP, seasonal FMNP/WIC and SFMNP vouchers, plus cash and card payments.

name: Dallas Farmers Market
season: The Shed: Saturdays 9am-5pm, Sundays 10am-5pm; Market Building open 7 days 10am-7pm
description: The Dallas Farmers Market, operating since 1941, is a downtown Dallas hub featuring The Market Building, a 26,000 sq ft food hall with restaurants and artisanal vendors, and The Shed, an open-air pavilion for seasonal produce, meats, and eggs. It serves as a community gathering space for fresh food and local goods in the heart of downtown Dallas.

name: Deaf Smith Chamber of Commerce Farmers Market
Denton County Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays 8 AM-1 PM April through November; Tuesdays, Thursdays and Saturdays 8 AM-1 PM June through September
description: The Denton County Farmers Market, run by the Denton County Farmers Market Association, is located at the corner of Carroll Blvd and Mulberry St in the parking lot of the Denton County Historical Park. It offers locally grown produce and farm products to the Denton, Texas community.

name: Dignowity Hill Farmers' Market
description: A grassroots, 100% resident-run farmers market on San Antonio's east side focused on organic food, with health and wellness education and community fitness classes.

name: Downtown Brenham First Fridays Farmer & Artisan Market
description: The Downtown Brenham First Friday Farmers & Artisan Market is an open-air market in Historic Downtown Brenham featuring local artisan and craft, cottage food, farm-to-market, small business, and retail vendors, with live musical performances. Vendors sell produce, prepared foods, crafts, and artisan goods.

name: Downtown Denison Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, late April through early October, 9:00 AM to noon
description: Since 2011 the Downtown Denison Farmers Market has offered residents a wide range of homemade and homegrown goods and serves as both a shopping venue and a community gathering destination.

name: East Texas State Fair Farmers Market
Ella Austin Community Center Farmers' Market
Farmers Market at Imperial
season: Saturdays 9:00 AM-1:00 PM, year-round, rain or shine
description: A Saturday farmers market at Imperial Park Recreation Center in Sugar Land featuring local vendors with live music, cooking demonstrations, and seasonal entertainment.

name: Farmers Market at the Cibolo
description: A Saturday market at the historic Herff Farm in Boerne where vendors offer produce, farm-fresh eggs, sustainably raised meat, wine, baked goods, preserves, and locally roasted coffee.

name: Grass Roots Farmers Market
description: A Saturday farmers market in Gilmer whose mission is to connect the community and visitors to local farmers, food producers, and artisans.

name: Green Avenue Farmers Market
season: Tuesdays and Fridays 3:00-6:00 PM (or until sell-out), May through September
description: A farmers market operated by the Killeen Area Growers Association, whose members grow and sell produce and products from Bell County and surrounding counties.

name: Grow'n Growers Farmers Market
description: A Saturday farmers market in San Juan offering locally grown fruits, vegetables, and farm products, including USDA Certified organic items, to support Rio Grande Valley agriculture.

name: Grow'n Growers Farmers Market
description: A Saturday farmers market in the Edinburg/San Juan area offering locally grown fruits, vegetables, and farm products, including USDA Certified organic items, to support Rio Grande Valley agriculture.

name: Hill Country Farmers' Market
description: A community-based association operating year-round farmers markets in the Texas Hill Country around San Antonio, supporting local growers and producers and nurturing appreciation of wholesome local foods.

name: Hill Country Farmers Market Association
description: A community-based association operating year-round farmers markets in the Texas Hill Country around San Antonio, supporting local growers and producers and nurturing appreciation of wholesome local foods.

name: Historic McKinney Farmers Market at Chestnut Square Historic Village
season: Saturdays 8:00 AM-12:00 PM April through December; 9:00 AM-12:00 PM January through March
description: A Saturday market at Chestnut Square Historic Village in McKinney featuring over 70 local vendors with homegrown produce, handmade goods, and live music, face painting, and pony rides on select dates.

name: HOME sweet FARM Market
description: HOME sweet FARM is a small family farm run by homesteaders living on 22+ acres for over 30 years. In 2004 they established Houston's first and largest CSA farm, with a mission to share the farming life, build community, and equip, encourage and inspire others. They operate a farm market in Brenham, Texas.

name: HOPE Farmers Market
description: A weekly Sunday market at Plaza Saltillo in East Austin showcasing local farmers, food artisans, and artists alongside educational programming, community outreach, and live music.

name: Jefferson Farmers Market
description: A downtown Jefferson farmers market offering produce, eggs, jams, jellies, pickles, baked goods, and crafts raised or produced within a 50-mile radius of Jefferson, Texas.

name: Keller Farmers Market
description: A year-round farmers market in Keller offering fresh local produce, eggs, and other locally grown and made goods.

name: Lubbock Farmers' Market
description: The Lubbock Downtown Farmers Market supports local food producers and artisans in Lubbock, Texas, operating at 5th Street & Avenue J. It provides a venue for the community to buy from local growers and makers.

name: Midland Downtown Farmers' Market
season: Saturday mornings; 8:00 AM to noon (April through October), 9:00 AM start (November through March)
description: A community-focused Saturday market in downtown Midland run as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit that keeps vendor fees low to support local growers, makers, and food entrepreneurs.

name: Navarro County Farmers' Market
season: Tuesdays and Saturdays, May through October, 7:00 AM to noon
description: A seasonal farmers market in Corsicana, open Tuesdays and Saturdays from May through October with locally grown produce and farm goods.

name: North Central Texas Farmers Market Corporation I
North Central Texas Farmers Market Corporation III
Pearl Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 9:00 AM-1:00 PM, year-round, rain or shine
description: A producers market at the Pearl in San Antonio where vendors grow, produce, harvest, or raise everything they sell, drawing over 50 local vendors from within 150 miles with free cooking demos and live music each Saturday.

name: Pearland Old Townsite Farmers Market
season: 2nd and 4th Saturday of each month, 8:00 AM-12:00 PM
description: A twice-monthly Saturday farmers market held at Zychlinski Park in the Old Townsite district of Pearland, Texas.

name: Pflugerville Pfarmers' Market
season: May through October, with special markets in November, December, March and April
description: The Pflugerville Pfarmers Market is held at Heritage Park (The Green/Red Barn), 901 Old Austin-Hutto Road, and is organized by the City of Pflugerville. Vendors sell seasonal produce such as tomatoes, cucumbers, squash, okra, melons and peaches, plus artisan baked goods, honey, flowers, eggs, free-range meats, and jarred salsas, pickles and jams, with live music on selected dates.

name: Rose City Farmers Market
season: April 4 - November 28, Saturdays 8am-noon; Winter Market 9am-1pm on select Saturdays (Dec-Mar)

Vendors offer fresh seasonal vegetables, microgreens, pasture-raised meats, baked goods, sourdough breads, local honey, herbs, goat milk soaps and handmade products. It runs a main Saturday season plus a winter market on select dates.

name: Saint Michael's Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, April 18 – September 5, 8 AM to 12 PM
description: Saint Michael's Farmers Market is a non-profit, award-winning community market in Dallas featuring all-edible products from nearby farmers, ranchers, and artisans dedicated to sustainable, natural, or organic practices. Products are sourced within 150 miles of Dallas County and include seasonal produce, meats, and flowers.

name: Sam Houston High School Farmers' Market
San Antonio Farmers Market Association III
San Antonio Farmers Market Association IV
San Antonio Farmers Market Association V
San Antonio Farmers Market Association VI
San Antonio Farmers Market Association VIII

San Marcos/New Braunfels Farmers Market Association
Sealy Farmers Market

description: A monthly farmers market in Sealy, held the third Saturday of each month and organized by the Sealy Eastside Foundation.

name: South Texas Farmers Market Association I
South Texas Farmers Market Association II
South Texas Farmers Market Association IV
South Texas Farmers Market Association V
South Texas Farmers Market Association VI
Temple Farmers' Market
season: Tuesdays and Thursdays 7:00 AM-12:00 PM, May through September
description: A seasonal Temple farmers market at West Temple Park offering local fruits and vegetables, craft vendors, jams, jellies, baked goods, and family activities.

description: The Texarkana, Texas Farmers' Market is a seasonal open-air market in historic downtown Texarkana, operated by the City of Texarkana, Texas, promoting locally grown food and locally produced goods. Vendors sell fresh eggs, herbs, home-baked goods, jams, peaches, berries, heirloom plants, pasture-raised meats, handmade soaps, locally roasted coffee and in-season vegetables. The market accepts SNAP/EBT benefits.

name: The Market At Alhambra

description: Founded in 2010, the Market at Alhambra (McAllen Farmers Market) is held at the McAllen Public Library to broaden access to fresh local food and promote healthy eating and living.

description: Established in 2008, The Woodlands Farmers Market at Grogan's Mill hosts over 60 vendors each Saturday offering local produce, meat, eggs, honey, coffee, bread, and prepared meals.

name: Tyler Farmers' Market

description: A Saturday farmers market at the Onion Creek Cafe parking lot on White Oak Drive in Houston's Heights area.

city: 2 W Saint George Blvd St. George

name: Garden City Farmers' and Artisans' Market
Holladay Farmer's Market
city: 4800 S. Highland Drive Salt Lake City

name: Alexandria Farmer's Market
description: The Old Town Farmers' Market in Alexandria is the oldest farmers' market in the country held continuously at the same site, dating to when George Washington sent produce from Mount Vernon. During peak season more than 70 vendors offer local produce, prepared foods, meats, dairy, baked goods, flowers, and art.

name: Bedford Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, May through September, 8:00 AM - 12:00 noon
description: The Bedford Farmers Market is held under the Centertown Pavilion on West Washington Street in Bedford, Virginia, offering fresh fruits and vegetables, jams, jellies, baked goods, meats, and eggs. Local artisans also bring jewelry, leather and sewn goods, and woodwork.

name: Birdhouse Farmers Market
season: Tuesdays, May through November, 3:00 PM - 6:30 PM (in-person and online); December through April, online ordering only
description: The Birdhouse Farmers Market in Richmond grows community connections and fights food insecurity through education, neighborhood outreach, and a commitment to local agriculture. It operates as an in-person Tuesday market in season and an online ordering market over the winter.

name: Buckland Farm Market
season: Sunday through Saturday, 9:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m., year-round.
description: Buckland Farm Market is a family-owned farm market in Warrenton, Virginia offering locally grown produce, meats, eggs, and baked goods. The property also features an onsite bakery, ice cream, and a brewery.

name: Cascades Farmers Market
description: Cascades Farmers Market is one of Loudoun's oldest farmers markets, held in the Senior Center parking lot in Potomac Falls, Sterling, VA, and run by the Loudoun Valley HomeGrown Markets Cooperative. It is a producer-only market where vendors must grow or produce all they sell within a 125-mile radius. Over 25 vendors offer fresh produce, baked goods, prepared foods, plants and handcrafted items.

name: Catawba Valley Farmers Market
season: Thursdays 3:00 p.m.–6:30 p.m., first Thursday in May through mid-October, rain or shine
description: Held each Thursday at the Catawba Community Center, this seasonal market requires all foods and handicrafts to be grown or produced within 100 miles of the market, with most coming from just a few miles away.

name: Cedar Lane Farm Market
season: Open year-round; Wednesday through Saturday 10 a.m.–5 p.m. and Sunday 12 p.m.–5 p.m.
description: Cedar Lane Farm Market in Stephens City operates year-round inside Stonewall Antiques and Collectibles, offering fresh, locally sourced produce and artisanal goods from local vendors alongside antiques and collectibles.

name: Chesapeake Farmers' Market - Chesapeake City Park
Chesapeake Farmers Marketplace at City Hall
description: Located at City Hall in the heart of Chesapeake's Great Bridge area, this Saturday marketplace offers seasonal produce, flowers, fresh seafood, meats, breads and baked goods, handmade soaps and candles, honey, gourmet dog treats, and one-of-a-kind works of art.

name: Chesapeake Health Department Wednesday Farmer's Market
Chilhowie Farmers' Market
description: The Chilhowie Farmers' Market is operated by the Town of Chilhowie, Virginia, offering locally grown and produced goods. The market provides rules, guidelines, and vendor applications for those wishing to sell. It is held in the town of Chilhowie.

name: Chincoteague Island Farmers and Artisans Market
City Center Farmers Market Oyster Point
Coleman Nursery and Farmer's Market
description: Coleman Nursery is a local garden center serving the Williamsburg area with a wide range of annuals, perennials, trees, and shrubs, and operates a barn section featuring a farmers market, local honey, and chicken feed.

name: Colonial Heights Farmers Market
description: A year-round open-air market at 2600 Boulevard in Colonial Heights featuring locally grown produce, meats, eggs, plants, baked goods, and honey.

name: Columbia Pike Farmers Market
description: The Columbia Pike Farmers Market is located at 2820 Columbia Pike in Arlington, in front of Walgreens. It features around 13 vendors selling produce, baked goods, cheese, honey, salsas, and prepared foods. The market is organized by Columbia Pike (columbiapike.org).

name: Cullipher Farm Market
season: Open Tuesday through Sunday, 9am-6pm, from mid-to-late April (when strawberries are in) through late October.
description: Cullipher Farm Market is a family-run Virginia Beach destination on Princess Anne Road offering pick-your-own strawberries, blueberries, peaches, apples, pumpkins and sunflowers across the season, alongside a popular house-made ice cream window with floats and fruit slushies.

name: Damascus Farmers' Market
season: Thursday afternoons 4–7 p.m., May through October
description: The Damascus Farmers Market offers local produce, meats, eggs, baked goods, and craft items on Thursday evenings, with live music each week.

name: Daughter of the Stars Farmers Market
Deltaville Farmers' Market
description: The Holly Point Market (Deltaville Farmers' Market) is held at the Deltaville Maritime Museum, offering fresh produce, meats, honey, handcrafted goods, and homemade items. Visitors enjoy complimentary museum admission, discovery cruises, live music, and model train displays. It is organized by the Deltaville Maritime Museum.

name: Downtown South Boston Farmers Market
description: The Downtown South Boston Farmers Market is home to many local farmers, bakers, and artisans. Shoppers can find homegrown vegetables, baked goods, plants and flowers, handmade gifts, and home decor. The market operates seasonally in downtown South Boston, Virginia.

name: Earlysville Farmers Market
description: The Earlysville Farmers Market is a community market held in the Buck Mountain Episcopal Church Parish Hall parking lot near Earlysville Road and Reas Ford Road. Local vendors sell fresh produce, baked goods, grass-fed beef and chicken, relishes, pickles, salsa, and jams.

name: Fairfax County Farmers Markets
season: Seasonal, generally April through November; most markets run 8am-noon on weekdays or Saturdays, with some afternoon markets (2pm-6pm or 3pm-7pm). Days and dates vary by location.
description: The Fairfax County Park Authority operates 10 producer-only farmers markets across the county, where vendors sell only items they grow or make from scratch sourced within 125 miles, providing residents access to locally grown products and creating market opportunities for area farmers and food producers.

name: Fairfax Sunday Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays 9:00 a.m.–1:00 p.m., May through October
description: This established market near the Fairfax Courthouse features roughly 45 vendors selling fresh produce, baked goods, and other items in a family- and pet-friendly setting with plenty of parking and picnic tables.

name: Fairlington Farmers Market
description: Nestled in the historic Fairlington neighborhood of Arlington, the Fairlington Farmers Market has operated since 2014 with vendors who harvest, bake, and create their food products within 125 miles of Arlington.

name: Farmers' Market at Citizen's Square
description: The Farmers' Market at Citizen Square is operated by the Town of Rocky Mount, Virginia, serving as a gathering place where local growers sell produce, meats, eggs, baked goods, preserves, crafts, and seasonal items. It hosts annual events including a Strawberry Festival, Warren Street Festival, and Rocky Mount Fest.

name: Farmers Market at St Stephens
season: Year-round, Saturdays; October–April 9 a.m.–noon, May–September 8 a.m.–noon (closed the Saturday after Christmas)
description: St. Stephen's hosts a year-round farmers market in Richmond featuring seasonal produce, eggs, poultry, meats, and flowers from local farms along with local artisan goods. Established in 2009 as a self-supporting ministry, the market accepts SNAP benefits with Virginia Fresh Match funding.

name: Farmville Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, 9am-1pm, from the first Saturday in April through the Saturday before Thanksgiving, plus winter markets 10am-2pm on select Saturdays in December through March.
description: The Farmville Community Marketplace farmers market at 213 North Street offers locally grown fruits and vegetables, meats, home-baked goods, handcrafted goods and more, operating rain or shine.

name: Floyd Farmers Market
description: The Floyd Farmers Market is a community market under the downtown community pavilion at 203 South Locust Street in Floyd, VA, featuring local farmers and artisans focused on sustainable agriculture and seasonal food. Vendors offer fresh produce, goat's milk products, baked goods, handmade soap and other artisan goods.

name: Fluvanna Farmers Market
season: Sundays 11 a.m.–2 p.m., March through October

name: Four Mile Run Farmers and Artisans Market
season: Sundays, 9am-1pm; soft open mid-March, full market mid-April through early December.
description: The Four Mile Run Farmers & Artisans Market serves the Arlandria neighborhood as a community gathering place for live music, fellowship, and quality food and crafts, offering fresh produce and artisan goods.

name: Fredericksburg Farmers Market - Mary Washington Healthcare
Fredericksburg, VA Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 7 a.m.–1 p.m., April through early December, plus Wednesdays 3–7 p.m. in summer
description: An open-air market at Hurkamp Park where over 30 vendors sell homegrown, homemade, and hand-baked goods including vegetables, fruits, meats, eggs, breads, and plants, all within walking distance of downtown Fredericksburg restaurants and shopping.

name: FRESHFARM Rosslyn Farmers Market
season: Wednesdays 2 p.m.–6 p.m., May through October
description: Located at Central Place Plaza in Rosslyn, this FRESHFARM market draws neighborhood residents and commuters with conventional and certified organic produce, pasture-raised meat, baked goods and breads, popsicles, fresh flowers, ferments, and pickles.

name: Galax Farmers Market
description: The Galax Farmers Market is the heart of Galax's local agriculture and craftsmanship, located at 206 N Main Street in downtown Galax, VA. It offers locally grown produce, home-baked goods, and handmade crafts, with weekly food trucks, music, and kids' activities.

name: Game's Farmers Market
season: Open daily 7 am – 9 pm year-round (closed Christmas Day; early close Thanksgiving and Christmas Eve)
description: Game's Farmers Market is a local family-owned grocery store in Newport News that has been in operation since 1972. It is known for friendly service, quality meat products, locally-grown produce, and an Amish bulk section.

name: Greene Farmers Market
description: The Greene Farmers Market is held year-round at the Greene Commons Pavilion, 40 Celt Road in Stanardsville, VA, at the corner of Main Street and Celt Road. It offers fresh fruits and vegetables, locally-raised meats, prepared foods, coffee and artisan goods, and doubles SNAP benefits on produce purchases.

name: Harrisonburg Farmers Market
description: The Harrisonburg Farmers Market is a producer-only market at 228 South Liberty Street in Harrisonburg, VA, offering fresh local produce and artisan goods year-round. Under its Producer-Only Principle, all products are homegrown, homemade, or handmade.

name: Heart of Virginia Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 8 a.m.–12 p.m. (winter hours 10 a.m.–12 p.m.), first Saturday in April through the Saturday before Thanksgiving
description: Held rain or shine at the STEPS parking lot in Farmville, this market carries local in-season produce such as berries, apples, peaches, greens, and tomatoes alongside local meats including pasture-raised poultry, beef, lamb, and pork.

name: Isle of Wight & Smithfield Farmers' Markets
Lakeside Farmers Market
description: Lakeside Farmers' Market is a year-round market at 6110 Lakeside Avenue in Richmond (Henrico), VA, with vendors selling fresh produce, flowers, and local goods.

name: LF Market
description: Founded on preserving the City of Suffolk's agricultural heritage through wholesome family activities, the Suffolk Farmers' Market operates at the Suffolk Visitor Center Pavilion offering fresh local produce, beef, poultry, eggs, honey, baked goods, jams, plants and flowers, handmade soaps and other artisan crafts.

name: Long Farmers Market
season: Wednesday evenings 3–6 p.m., mid-May through August
description: A veteran-owned-and-operated market located behind the University of Mary Washington Stafford Campus on Route 17, offering fresh produce, meats, honey, baked goods, granola, ice cream, pickles, pizza, popcorn, salsa, coffee, tea, and soaps.

name: Louisa Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, 8am-noon, from Memorial Day through the last Saturday in September.
description: The Louisa County Farmers Market is held on the hill next to the Essex Bank at the intersection of East Main Street and the 208 Connector, with special Saturdays featuring extended hours, entertainment and displays.

name: Manassas Farmers Market (Thursday)
season: Thursdays, April through October, 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM
description: The Thursday Manassas Farmers Market, operated by the nonprofit Historic Manassas, Inc., is held at the Harris Pavilion in Downtown Manassas and offers fresh produce, baked goods, and artisan products from local vendors. The market also serves as a small business incubator for area producers.

name: Marion Regional Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 8:00 a.m.–12:00 p.m., first Saturday in May through last Saturday in October; winter market second Saturday of each month January through April, 10:00 a.m.–12:00 p.m.
description: Located on South Chestnut Street in Marion, the market offers a variety of seasonal produce, locally raised meats, farm products, and hand-crafted items from across the region.

name: Marker Miller Orchards Farm Market and Bakery
season: Monday-Friday 9:00 AM - 6:00 PM, Saturday 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM (summer hours)
description: Marker-Miller Orchards is a family farm market and bakery on a 325-acre orchard in the Shenandoah Valley near Winchester, offering fresh local produce, baked goods, and seasonal family activities. The market features homegrown fruits, a bakery, and pick-your-own offerings.

name: Narrows Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 9 am–1 pm (excluding Locals Night dates)
description: The Narrows Farmers Market is located at 135 Monroe Street in downtown Narrows and features vendors selling locally-grown produce, homemade treats, and artisan goods like lotions and soaps. The market provides onsite tables, a sheltered pavilion for rain coverage, and clean restroom facilities.

name: Nokesville Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, May through October, 8:00 AM - 12:00 noon
description: The Nokesville Farmers Market is a Saturday market in Nokesville, Virginia, offering fresh local produce, meats, cheeses, baked goods, honey and preserves. Vendors also bring fiber, art, home crafts, and livestock to the market.

name: North Stafford Farmers' Market
season: Sundays, April through November, 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM
description: The North Stafford Farmers Market is a veteran owned and operated market held behind Stafford Hospital in Stafford, Virginia. It offers farm-fresh produce, meats, eggs, honey, and baked goods alongside a variety of other locally produced goods.

name: Norton Friends and Farmer's Market
season: Tuesdays, June through September, 4:00 PM - 6:00 PM
description: The Norton Friends and Farmers Market is held at the Norton Expo Center in Downtown Norton, Virginia, offering fresh produce, treats, and crafts from local farmers and friends.

name: Oak Marr Farmers Market
season: Wednesdays, May through October, 8:00 AM - 12:00 noon
description: The Oak Marr Farmers Market is one of the producer-only farmers markets operated by the Fairfax County Park Authority, located at the Oak Marr RECenter in Oakton. Vendors sell locally grown and produced goods sourced from within a set radius of the county.

name: Oakton Farmers Market
products: Fresh fruits; Fresh vegetables; Juices/non-alcoholic ciders; Prepared foods; Red/other non-poultry meat and products
description: FRESHFARM is a nonprofit based in Washington, DC, that works to create a more resilient, equitable, and sustainable food future. For 25 years, we have operated producer-only farmers markets in DC, Maryland, and Virginia. Our farmers market network is the third-largest in the country and the largest in the Mid-Atlantic region, providing economic opportunity to a network of 250 Mid-Atlantic farmers and producers and a community space to restore the vital connections in our local food systems.

name: OnTheSquareVA Farmers Market
season: Thursdays 11:00 a.m.–1:00 p.m., mid-May through late September
description: A weekly market on the plaza at 109 Governor Street adjacent to Capitol Square in Richmond, offering local seasonal produce, fruits and berries, cheeses, baked goods, prepared foods, honey, plants, and a few artisan crafts.

name: Pearisburg Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays 9:00 AM - 2:00 PM and the third Thursday of the month 4:00 PM - 8:00 PM, May through October

name: Portsmouth Farmer's Market - PortsEvents
season: Saturdays year-round (closed Saturday before Christmas through first Saturday in February); 9:00 AM - 2:00 PM April-October, 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM November-April
description: The Portsmouth Olde Towne Farmers Market is a volunteer-run 501(c)(3) nonprofit market focused on fresh local food, farm-related products, and artisan goods. It serves as a community gathering place with live music, seasonal yoga, and ready-to-eat options.

name: Powhatan Farmers' Market
description: The Powhatan Village Farmers Market is a boutique market at Fighting Creek Park, 2200 Mann Road, Powhatan, VA, offering farm-fresh seasonal produce, grass-fed meats, fruits and berries, honey, baked goods and select artisan crafts. Weekly food trucks and live performances are also featured.

name: Prince George Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, May through October, 9:00 AM - 1:00 PM
description: The Prince George County Farmers' Market is a producer-grower market held at Scott Park in Prince George, Virginia, dedicated to sustainable agriculture and supporting the regional farm economy. It connects local food choices with environmental quality and the surrounding community.

name: Pulaski Farmers' Market - The Marketplace
description: The Marketplace is a vendor market organized by Pulaski on Main at the Historic Train Station in downtown Pulaski, where all goods sold must be grown, produced, or handcrafted by the vendor and sourced within a 75-mile radius. It features seasonal produce, baked goods, crafts, and food vendors.

name: Radford Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 9 a.m.–1 p.m., April through October, rain or shine
description: A downtown Radford staple held every Saturday in season, the market offers a rotating selection of handmade goods, crafts, plants, meats, and produce.

name: Reston Farmers Market
description: The Reston Farmers Market is operated by the Fairfax County Park Authority at Lake Anne, featuring produce vendors, pop-up vendors, musicians, and community agencies selling fresh local vegetables and other items. Pets except service animals are not permitted.

name: River Street Market
description: A blend of arts, produce, and culture in Petersburg, the River Street Market offers fresh produce picked that morning plus handmade goods including jewelry, honey, jams, meats, seafood, soaps, and baked goods from local producers.

name: Rural Retreat Farmers Market at the Depot
Saturday Community Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, May through October, 9:00 AM - 1:00 PM
description: The Fairfax Saturday Market is a large, long-running market with roughly 45 vendors offering fresh produce, bakery items, and local goods in a family- and pet-friendly setting near historic Old Town Fairfax. It has operated for over 25 years.

name: South of the James Farmers Market
South of the James Winter Market
description: The South of the James Market is an open-air, producer-only market in Forest Hill Park where farmers and artisans sell locally-grown and hand-made items. Operated by GrowRVA, it runs year-round on Sundays, including a winter season, connecting local farms, food, and arts with Richmond communities.

name: Southwest Virginia Farmers' Market
season: Year-round, seven days a week (closed Thanksgiving and Christmas Day and during inclement weather)
description: The Southwest Virginia Farmers' Market in Hillsville operates both retail and wholesale sides at 497 Farmers Market Dr, just off I-77 Exit 14. The retail market offers farm fresh produce, produce-related items, in-season plants, and area arts and crafts.

name: Spotsylvania Farmers Market - VDOT Commuter Lot
season: April-December (Saturdays 8am-1pm); winter market second Saturday of Jan/Feb/Mar
description: The Spotsylvania Farmers Market is one of the largest farmers markets in the region, with more than 40 vendors supporting local farms, nurseries, bakeries, and food producers. The Saturday market runs at the Route 3/Gordon Road commuter lot, with additional Wednesday and Sunday locations.

name: Spotsylvania Farmers Market at Spotsylvania Regional Medical
season: Saturdays 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM (April-December); Wednesdays 2:00 PM - 6:00 PM (April-September)
description: The Spotsylvania Farmers Market is one of the region's largest, featuring more than 40 vendors supporting local farms, bakeries, and specialty food producers across multiple locations in Spotsylvania County. It offers seasonal produce and locally made goods.

name: Staunton-Augusta Farmers' Market
street: Byers St. and W. Johnson St.
season: Saturdays April-September 7am-12pm, October-November 8am-12pm; Winter Holiday Market in December
description: The Staunton-Augusta Farmers' Market is a producer market in Staunton, VA featuring local growers and vendors selling farm products, with vendor spotlights such as Barren Ridge Vineyard.

name: Tappahannock Farmers' Market
description: The Tappahannock Farmers' Market is a family-friendly market in Historic Downtown Tappahannock featuring local farmers, food producers, artisans, and musicians. It operates rain or shine.

name: The Bedford Farmer Managed Market, LLC
The Carytown Farmers Market
description: The Carytown Farmers Market is Richmond's premier Sunday market, created to give local farmers, makers, and consumers the opportunity to deal directly with each other. Held at City Stadium, it has been supporting local since 2012.

name: The Community Farmers' Market
description: The Old Town Fairfax Community Farmers Market (the Saturday Market) is a large market with approximately 45 vendors, established more than 25 years ago by the Downtown Fairfax Coalition. It operates seasonally on Saturdays at 10500 Page Avenue in Fairfax.

name: The Markets Fare at East Beach
season: Saturdays, May through November, 9:00 AM - 12:00 noon
description: The Markets Fare at East Beach is a seasonal Saturday farmers market in the East Beach community of Norfolk, Virginia, featuring fresh produce, meat, seafood, eggs, artisanal breads, and baked goods. Vendors also include preserves, bath and body products, and food trucks.

name: The Onancock Market
season: Summer market May-October, Saturdays 9am-12pm; Tuesday markets June-September (1st & 3rd Tuesdays) 3-6pm; Winter market November-April, Saturdays 9am-12pm
description: The Onancock Market is a community farmers market on Virginia's Eastern Shore featuring local seasonal vegetables, prepared foods, artisan hand crafts, and live music. The summer market is held at the corner of Market & Ames Streets in Onancock on Saturdays, with additional Tuesday afternoon markets at the Riverside Shore Memorial Hospital parking lot and a winter market at 20 Market Street.

name: Town of Gate City Farmer's Market
Town of Mount Jackson Farmers' Market
Tricycle Gardens' Four Season Farmers' Market
Vienna Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, May through October, 8:00 AM - 12:00 noon
description: The Vienna Farmers Market, located at the Vienna Community Center and run by the Optimist Club of Greater Vienna, is a producer market featuring locally sourced fruits, vegetables, and homemade foods. Local musicians perform live each week.

name: Vienna Saturday Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, May through October, 8:00 AM - 12:00 noon
description: The Vienna Saturday Farmers' Market, run by the Optimist Club of Greater Vienna at the Vienna Community Center, offers locally sourced fruits, vegetables, and homemade foods. Live music by local performers is a weekly feature of the market.

name: Vinton Farmers Market
season: May-October, Tuesday-Friday 9am-5pm and Saturday 9am-1pm (2026 season opens May 2)
description: The Vinton Farmers Market, operating since 1988, is a gathering place in downtown Vinton for residents and visitors to connect with local farmers, makers, and small businesses. Located at 204 W Lee Avenue, it supports small enterprises, promotes wellness, and strengthens community bonds.

name: Virginia Beach Farmers Market
description: The Virginia Beach Farmers Market at 3640 Dam Neck Road is a year-round open-air market featuring fresh local and regional produce, a butcher shop, a dairy store, organic grocery, seafood, florist, candy and bakery, specialty garden shops, and a restaurant. It hosts over 15 year-round merchants plus daily agriculture and crafter vendors, and is operated by the City of Virginia Beach Department of Agriculture.

name: Virginia Grown Farmers Market at the Olde Dominion Ag Ctr
Wakefield Farmers Market
season: Wednesdays, May through October, 2:00 PM - 6:00 PM
description: The Wakefield Farmers Market is a producer-only market operated by the Fairfax County Park Authority at the Audrey Moore RECenter in Annandale. Farmers and producers may only sell what they raise on their farms or make from scratch.

name: Wellness Connection Farmer's Market
description: Wellness Connection sponsors a local farmers' market built on the belief in the connection between mood and food, bringing fresh, nutritious options to the community. The market runs Saturdays from April through November, 9am to noon.

name: West End Farmers Market
description: Every Sunday, a stunning array of farmers, bakers, cooks, crafters, and artisans set up tents and tables at the West End Farmers Market in Ben Brenman Park to sell delicious food, superb crafts, and beautiful art. The market is now fully open all year round.

name: West Point Farmers Market
season: May 16-September 5, 2026, Saturdays 8am-noon (closed July 4); plus seasonal special markets (Spring, Autumn, Holiday)
description: The West Point Farmers Market is held Saturday mornings at The Pavilion at Riverwalk Park in West Point, offering fresh produce, baked goods, flowers, plants, jams and jellies, honey, eggs, gourmet dog treats, and handcrafted items in a riverside setting. It is run by the Town of West Point.

name: Westlake Farmers Market
street: Intersection of Booker T. Washington Hwy

name: Westover Farmers Market
description: The Westover Farmers Market is a producer-only market featuring 36 local farm and food vendors, where everyone who sells grows, raises, bakes, cooks, or prepares the food themselves, all from within 125 miles of Arlington. Vendors offer fresh produce, herbs, cut flowers, grass-fed meats, dairy, baked goods, and prepared foods.

name: Wise Farmers' Market
description: The Wise Farmers Market offers locally grown produce, herbs, and flowers as well as homemade, handcrafted products, with fresh fruits and vegetables, crafts, and honey. It is held at the Big Glades Community Square at 309 East Main Street in downtown Wise.

name: Woodbine Farm Market
description: Woodbine Farm Market in Strasburg, Virginia is a family farm market offering hanging baskets, herbs, perennials, vegetable plants, fresh produce, and bakery items. It also operates Spring Valley Cottages and a Berry Club membership program.

name: Woodstock Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, April through October, 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM
description: The Woodstock Farmers Market is held under cover at Fort Valley Nursery on South Hisey Avenue in Woodstock, Virginia, operating rain or shine. It offers seasonal vegetables, eggs, local meats, baked goods, and crafts.

name: YNot Wednesday Farmers Market at Town Center
Zion Crossroads Farmers and Flea Market
Caledonia Farmers' Market Association
products: Fresh fruits; Fresh vegetables; Baked goods; Canned or preserved fruits/vegetables; Coffee/tea; Crafts/woodworking items; Cut flowers; Dairy products.; Dry beans; Eggs; Fish/seafood; Fresh/dried herbs; Honey; Maple products; Poultry/fowl meat and products; Prepared foods; Red/other non-poultry meat and products; Soap/body care products; Wine, spirits, beer, hard cider

name: Craftsbury Common Market
season: Saturdays 10:00am-1:00pm, late May through late September
description: Nestled in the historic town of Craftsbury at the Craftsbury Common, this Saturday market features local produce, handcrafted goods, prepared foods, and live music.

name: Five Corners Farmers' Market
description: The Five Corners Farmers' Market in Essex Junction features local fruits and vegetables, perennials, herbs, fresh baked goods, breads, sauces, wines, maple products, eggs, grass-fed beef, and crafts, plus prepared foods. Live entertainment, children's activities, and cooking demonstrations are part of the festivities throughout the season.

name: Jay Village Farmers' Market
description: Located in the field next to the Jay Country Store, this market offers local produce, maple products, baked goods, and crafts.

name: Lakes Region Farmers' Market
season: Thursdays 9:00am-2:00pm, late June through early October

name: Manchester Farmers' Market
description: The Manchester Farmers' Market is a producer-only market held in Adams Park, off Route 7A in the heart of Manchester Center, Vermont. Organized by the Vermont Farmers Market Association, it features local farmers and artisans offering produce, crafts, prepared foods, and other goods. It began in 2000 and relocated to Adams Park in 2012.

name: Walloomsac Farmers' Market
description: The Walloomsac Farmers' Market is a producer-only market in Bennington, Vermont, held at Bennington Station along the Walloomsac River riverwalk park on Depot Street. It showcases locally grown produce, fruits, flowers, baked goods, dairy, meats, prepared foods, and select local crafts. The market accepts EBT, debit cards, and Farm to Family coupons.

name: Farmboat - Floating Markets & Maritime Heritage Events
Farmhouse Market
city: Se Redmond Fall City Rd Fall City

name: Friday Night Market at Juanita Beach
Pike Place Market Express @ Seattle City Hall
city: 600 4Th Ave B/W James St. & Cherry St. Seattle

name: Pike Place Market Express @South Lake Union
Saturday Market at The Port Of...
Thursday Winter Market in The South Perry District
Appleton Indoor Farmers' Market
description: The Downtown Appleton Indoor Farm Market is the winter edition of the Downtown Appleton Farm Market, organized by Appleton Downtown Inc. During November and December the market moves indoors to City Center Plaza in downtown Appleton, Wisconsin, offering local produce, foods, and crafts.

name: Ashland Area Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, mid-May through October, 8:30 a.m.-12:30 p.m.
description: The Ashland Area Farmer's Market is a Saturday-morning open-air market on the 600 block of Main Street West in downtown Ashland, offering fresh local produce, meats, honey, and maple syrup.

name: Balsam Lake Farmers' Market
season: Late May through early October, Fridays 3pm-6pm
description: The Balsam Lake Farmers Market is a seasonal open-air market in Balsam Lake, Wisconsin, held in the parking lot near Main Street across from the library. It runs on Friday evenings during the summer and early fall and is associated with the Balsam Lake Chamber.

name: Beloit Farmers' Market
street: State St. and E. Grand Ave.

name: Black River Falls Downtown Farmers' Market
description: The Black River Falls Downtown Farmers Market, located at 1 Main St. in Black River Falls, offers a selection of fresh, locally sourced produce, artisanal goods, and unique items from local vendors.

name: Bristol Farmers Market
description: The Bristol Farmers Market is held Wednesday afternoons at Veterans Park near the Bristol Municipal Building, offering fruits and vegetables, perennials, honey, eggs, fresh chicken, candles, and craft items.

name: Brown Deer Farmers' Market
season: Wednesdays, late June through mid-October, 10:00 a.m.-3:00 p.m. (closing at 2:00 p.m. after Labor Day)
description: The Brown Deer Farmers Market is one of the larger North Shore markets, held Wednesdays at Village Park and Pond near Village Hall with over 30 vendors offering a wide variety of produce and goods.

name: Burlington Farmers' Market
street: N. Pine St. and E. Washington St.
description: The Burlington Farmers Market is a weekly community market in Burlington, Wisconsin, operating since 2008. Held at Riverside Park (517 Congress St), it brings together local farmers, food producers, artisans, and musicians, offering seasonal produce, meats, cheeses, baked goods, honey, prepared foods, wine, craft beer, and local crafts.

name: Burnett County Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, late May through mid-October, 10:00 a.m.-1:00 p.m.
description: The Burnett County Farmers' Market in Siren is held Saturday mornings in the Adventure Church parking lot on State Road 35, offering farm-fresh fruits and vegetables, baked goods, meats, and crafts.

name: Delafield Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, May through late October, 8:00 a.m.-1:00 p.m.
description: The Delafield Farmers Market is a community market in downtown Delafield featuring growers, musicians, crafters, food artisans, and local businesses offering locally sourced goods.

name: Downtown Fond du Lac Farmers Market
description: The Downtown Fond du Lac Farmers Market is a seasonal Saturday market operated by the Downtown Fond du Lac Partnership in downtown Fond du Lac, Wisconsin. It features local vendors and includes a Market Bucks program.

name: Downtown Racine Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, May through October, 9:00 a.m.-1:00 p.m.
description: The Racine Farmers Market at Festival Park features more than 60 regional vendors offering produce, eggs, cheeses, baked goods, flowers, and artisan food products; admission is free and open to the public.

name: East Side Green Market
street: E. North Ave. and N. Cramer St.

name: Eau Claire Downtown Farmers' Market
season: May: Saturdays 7:30am-1:00pm; June-October: Wednesdays & Saturdays 7:30am-1:00pm, Thursdays 12:00pm-5:00pm; plus monthly Winter Market
description: The Eau Claire Downtown Farmers Market is a producers-only market located in Phoenix Park in downtown Eau Claire, Wisconsin, fostering connections between family farms and the community. It offers a SNAP token match program and live local music, set in an open-air pavilion along the Chippewa River.

name: Elkhart Lake Farmers' and Artisans' Market
description: The Elkhart Lake Farmers and Artisans Market is a seasonal Saturday market organized by the Elkhart Lake Chamber of Commerce in the heart of the Village of Elkhart Lake, Wisconsin. It features farmers, artisans, and food vendors selling fresh produce, crafts, and prepared foods.

name: Enderis Park Farmers Market
description: Since 2016 the Enderis Park Neighborhood Association has hosted a Sunday morning farmers market in Enderis Park at 72nd and Locust Streets in Milwaukee, featuring fresh produce, prepared foods, handmade crafts, and entertainment.

name: Farmers' Market on Broadway
description: The Farmers' Market on Broadway is one of Wisconsin's largest farmers' markets with 100+ vendors, located in the Broadway District of Green Bay and organized by On Broadway, Inc. Vendors sell produce, prepared and packaged foods, baked goods, meats, cheeses, flowers, plants, and handcrafted goods, alongside a beer garden and live music.

name: Festival Foods Darboy Farmers' Market
description: The Festival Foods Darboy Farmers' Market is hosted in the parking lot of the Appleton-Darboy Festival Foods, offering a way to support local farmers and businesses while shopping for fresh produce.

name: Festival Foods Eau Claire Farmers' Market
description: The Festival Foods Eau Claire Farmers' Markets are hosted in the parking lots of Festival Foods locations in Eau Claire, offering a way to support local farmers and businesses while shopping for fresh produce.

name: Festival Foods Oshkosh Farmers' Market
description: The Festival Foods Oshkosh Farmers' Market is hosted in the parking lot of the Oshkosh Festival Foods, offering a way to support local farmers and businesses while shopping for fresh produce.

name: Festival Foods Sheboygan Farmers' Market
description: The Festival Foods Sheboygan Farmers' Market is hosted in the parking lot of the Sheboygan Festival Foods, offering a way to support local farmers and businesses while shopping for fresh produce.

name: Festival Foods Suamico Farmers' Market
description: The Festival Foods Suamico Farmers' Market is hosted in the parking lot of the Suamico Festival Foods near Green Bay, offering a way to support local farmers and businesses while shopping for fresh produce.

name: Fort Atkinson Farmers' Market
description: The Fort Farmers Market is a community marketplace in Fort Atkinson, Wisconsin featuring local vendors offering produce, arts, crafts, and other goods. It hosts Art weekends for local artists plus special events including a Writers Festival and live music.

name: Fox Point Farmers' Market
description: The Fox Point Farmers' Market is held Saturday mornings in the parking lot of the North Shore Congregational Church on Santa Monica Blvd., offering local produce, honey, baked goods, prepared foods, and live music.

name: Germantown Farmers' Market
description: The Germantown Farmers' Market is a seasonal Saturday market organized by the Village of Germantown, Wisconsin, held at the Germantown Village Hall. Vendors offer locally grown vegetables, fruits, eggs, honey, maple syrup, cut flowers, potted plants, candles, jewelry, and crafts.

name: Green Bay Farmers' Market
street: S. Washington St. and Stuart St.
description: The Saturday Farmers Market is the largest farmers market in Green Bay with 100+ vendors, organized by Downtown Green Bay, Inc. in the downtown CityDeck area. Vendors offer fresh produce, meats, cheeses, flowers, and more.

name: Greenfield Farmers Market
season: May - October (closed Memorial Day weekend), Sundays 10:00am-2:00pm
description: The Greenfield Farmers Market is a seasonal Sunday market organized by Greenfield Parks & Recreation at Konkel Park in Greenfield, Wisconsin. It features local vendors, musicians, and a monthly makers market on the first Sunday of each month.

name: Hayward Farmers' Market
season: Mondays, June through September, 9:00 a.m.-1:00 p.m.

name: Hmong Mutual Assistance Association Farmers' Market
Jackson Park Farmers' Market
description: The Jackson Park Farmers Market is a neighborhood market in Milwaukee located on the Kinnickinnic River Parkway next to the pond and boathouse, near 3500 W. Forest Home Ave. It runs on Thursday afternoons with a convenient after-work time slot. Vendors offer fresh produce, honey, coffee, tea, flowers, popcorn, bakery, and prepared food including food trucks, with kids activities and live music.

name: Kaukauna Farmers' Market
season: June 3 - September 30, Wednesdays (4:30-8:00pm through Aug 26, 4:30-7:00pm Sep 2-30)
description: The Kaukauna Farmers' Market is held on Wednesday evenings in the public parking lot behind the Second Street business district at 101 Crooks Ave, the same night as the Live! from Hydro Park concert series. It is managed by the City of Kaukauna Community Enrichment Coordinator. Vendors sell produce, prepared foods, baked goods, coffee, beverages, handmade crafts, candles, and artisan items.

name: Lodi Valley Farmers' Market
season: Friday evenings, May through September, 3:00-6:00 p.m.
description: The Lodi Valley Farmers' Market is held Friday evenings in Lodi, offering produce, plants, meat, cheese, eggs, and crafts from local vendors.

name: Main Street Marshfield Downtown Farmers Market
season: Tuesdays, June through late October, 8:00 a.m.-1:00 p.m.
description: The Main Street Marshfield Downtown Farmers' Market is held Tuesdays in downtown Marshfield, featuring Wisconsin-made and grown plants, flowers, produce, baked goods, foods, and crafts including natural and certified organic products.

name: Manitowoc Farmers' Market
season: Saturdays, May through October, 8:00 a.m.-1:00 p.m.
description: The Manitowoc Farmers Market is held Saturdays at the Quay Street parking lot, welcoming over 60 vendors featuring produce, processed and prepared foods, artisan crafts, and handmade goods.

name: Merrill Area Farmers' Market
season: Wednesdays and Saturdays, June through October, 7:30 a.m.-noon

name: Milwaukee Public Market
season: Open daily; Monday-Saturday 10:00 a.m.-8:00 p.m., Sunday 10:00 a.m.-6:00 p.m.
description: The Milwaukee Public Market is a downtown food destination in the Historic Third Ward featuring 19 independently owned merchants offering artisan and ethnic products, prepared foods, and bars.

name: Mineral Point Farmers' Market
description: The Mineral Point Market is a Saturday morning tradition at Water Tower Park in Mineral Point, WI, offering just-picked organic produce, berries, fruits, locally grown eggs, meats and cheese, plus baked goods, honey, flowers, herbs, plants, soaps, candles and handcrafted items. Vendor lineup is subject to change week to week.

name: Monroe Farmers' Market on the Square
season: Saturdays 8:00 a.m.-noon and Wednesdays 12:00-3:00 p.m., May through October, on the Square in downtown Monroe
description: The Main Street Monroe Farmers Market is held on the Square in downtown Monroe, where over 20 vendors sell fresh Wisconsin-grown produce, plants, flowers, baked goods, meats, cheese, and handmade crafts and pottery.

name: New Berlin Farmers' Market
season: May through last weekend in October, Saturdays 8am-noon
description: The New Berlin Farmers' Market, organized by the New Berlin Chamber of Commerce, features fresh, locally grown and produced goods from 15+ regular vendors plus drop-ins, including produce, fruits, vegetables, herbs, baked goods, honey, maple syrup, mushrooms, salsas, soaps, meat, flowers, plants and crafts. It is within walking distance of local shops and restaurants.

name: Northwind Perennial Farm Market
season: Sunday-Wednesday 10:00 a.m.-3:00 p.m.; Thursday-Saturday 10:00 a.m.-4:00 p.m. (seasonal)
description: Northwind Perennial Farm in Burlington is a 10-acre perennial farm, retail nursery, and event venue offering garden design and farm market goods, operating since 1991.

name: Oshkosh Farmers' Market in the Park
Phillips Farmers' Market
street: Maple St. and S. Avon Ave.

name: Princeton Farm to Fork Farmers' Market
Racine County Indoor Outdoor Farmers Market, LLC
season: Year-round: Tuesday 1:00-6:00 p.m., Friday 8:00 a.m.-1:00 p.m., Saturday 7:00 a.m.-2:00 p.m., Sunday 10:00 a.m.-4:00 p.m.
description: The Racine County Indoor/Outdoor Farmers' Market is a Wisconsin producers-only market on the north side of Racine with over 40 vendors, offering year-round access to fresh local produce across all four seasons.

name: Racine Farmers' Market
description: A lakefront community market at Festival Park in downtown Racine featuring over 60 regional farmers, food producers, and artisans selling fresh produce and local artisan crafts on the shores of Lake Michigan.

name: Reedsburg Area Medical Center Farmers' Market
season: May through October, Fridays 10 a.m. - 3:00 p.m.
description: The RAMC Farmers Market welcomes visitors and vendors to the front lawn of Reedsburg Area Medical Center. The market offers locally grown produce and goods on Fridays during the warm-weather season. It is sponsored by Reedsburg Area Medical Center as a community health initiative.

name: Richland Area Farmers' Market
description: The Richland Area Farmers' Market is an all-food and fresh-flower market in Richland Center, WI, held at the corner of Orange and Court Streets and managed through Richland Center Tourism/Visitor's Center. Vendors sell fresh flowers, breads and baked goods, produce (apples, greens, beans, herbs, onions, garlic, potatoes, tomatoes, radishes, squash, pumpkins), maple syrup, mushrooms, honey, canned goods, eggs and meats.

name: Sheboygan Farmers' Market

Enjoy a wide variety of Vegetables & Fruit, Herbs, Cage-Free Eggs, Fresh Bread, Jam, Coffee, Seafood, Mexican Food, Food Artisans, Sweets, Art, Products, Clothing, Body Products, Jewelry, Nonprofit information, and Live Music. Updates are posted on Facebook & Twitter @RSfarmersmarket or Instagram @RS_farmersmarket. The market is nestled between a very nice playground, picnic tables, and the Running Springs Branch Library at 2645 Whispering Pines Drive. At 6,000 feet elevation, the field has nice views of nearby pine trees, blue skies (mostly) and cooler mountain temperatures. The area is considered a Gateway to the San Bernardino Mountains. Many visitors come year-round to enjoy the San Bernardino National Forest.
