name: Westport Farmers Market
description: Support for local farmers and producers is the Westport Farmers Market's number one priority. Its mission is to increase awareness, knowledge and support for local farmers and producers focused on sustainable and healthy growing practices. The market is held Thursdays at 50 Imperial Avenue in Westport, Connecticut.

name: Acorn Farmers Market and Cafe
season: Sundays 10:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m. (Wurster Park) and Thursdays 3:00 p.m. to 6:00 p.m.; cafe and market open daily 10:00 a.m. to 6:00 p.m.
description: Acorn Farmers' Market and Cafe in Manchester, Michigan sells locally-sourced meat, dairy and produce, baked goods, cut flowers, honey, maple syrup and coffee, and runs a sit-down cafe serving sandwiches, soups and meals prepared on-site.

name: Ada Farmers Market
description: The Ada Village Farmers Market features over 35 vendors offering fresh Michigan-grown fruits and vegetables from West Michigan growers, held in The Community Church parking lot in Ada.

name: Allen Farmers Market
description: The Allen Farmers Market is a year-round market run by the Allen Neighborhood Center in Lansing, Michigan, featuring over 15 vendors each week with seasonal produce, artisan and gluten-free baked goods, pasture-raised eggs, fresh cheeses, local meat and fish and ready-to-eat foods.

name: Alpena Farmers Market
season: Year-round; Saturdays 8:00 AM-1:00 PM outdoors at Mich-e-ke-wis Park June through October and 9:00 AM-1:00 PM indoors October through May, plus Wednesdays 8:00 AM-1:00 PM mid-July through September.
description: Founded in 1978, the Alpena Farmers Market gathers growers from five local counties selling locally grown produce, baked goods, grass-fed beef, eggs, poultry, herbs, mushrooms, plants, maple syrup, honey, and arts and crafts.

name: Ann Arbor Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 8:00 AM-3:00 PM January through April and 7:00 AM-3:00 PM May through December; Wednesdays 7:00 AM-3:00 PM May through December.
description: The Ann Arbor Farmers Market is a producer-only market featuring roughly 130 Michigan vendors offering seasonal fruit and vegetables, mushrooms, herbs, fresh eggs, pastured meat and poultry, lake fish, flowers, plants, honey, maple syrup, cheese, bread, prepared food, and handmade goods.

name: Benton Harbor Farmers Market
season: Wednesdays 9:00 AM-3:00 PM, June through October.
description: The Benton Harbor Farmers Market offers a variety of locally grown produce at affordable prices in downtown Benton Harbor's Dwight P. Mitchell City Center Park, supporting local farmers and the community economy.

name: Birmingham Farmers Market
season: Sundays 9:00 AM-2:00 PM, May through October.
description: The Birmingham Farmers Market is a weekly outdoor market where area residents shop for farm-fresh agricultural products, Michigan-grown produce, meat, dairy, and floral arrangements while gathering for entertainment and family-friendly activities.

name: Boyne City Farmers Market
season: Summer: Wednesdays and Saturdays 8:00 AM-12:00 PM, mid-May to mid-October. Winter: Saturdays 9:00 AM-12:00 PM, mid-October to mid-May.
description: The Boyne City Farmers Market is a year-round market and community gathering space in downtown Boyne City, featuring local small-scale farmers, artisans, and entrepreneurs selling fresh fruits and vegetables, herbs, baked goods, flowers, pasture-raised meat, maple syrup, honey, and handmade artwork.

name: Buchanan Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 8:00 AM-1:00 PM, May through October.
description: The Buchanan Farmers' Market is a seasonal market located in downtown Buchanan where local vendors offer fresh produce and locally made goods to the community.

name: Canton Farmers Market
season: Sundays 9:00 AM-1:00 PM, rain or shine, May through October.
description: The Canton Farmers Market at Preservation Park features Michigan farmers, producers, and artisans selling fresh produce, prepared foods, and handcrafted items, with live music and cooking demonstrations for guests.

name: Charlevoix Farmers Market
season: Thursdays 8:00 AM-1:00 PM, May through October.
description: Now in its third decade, the Charlevoix Farmers Market is a community gathering in downtown Charlevoix offering local produce, meats, and artisan baked goods while supporting regional growers and small businesses.

name: Chelsea Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 8:00 AM-1:00 PM at Palmer Commons and Wednesdays 10:00 AM-3:00 PM at Chelsea State Bank, May through October.
description: Operating for over 25 years, the Chelsea Farmers Market features old and new vendors offering seasonal produce, meats, baked goods, and artisan crafts alongside musical entertainment and special events.

name: City of Au Gres Downtown Farmers Market
City of Gladstone Farmers Market
season: Mondays 3:00 PM-6:00 PM, June through September.
description: The City of Gladstone Farmers Market is held at Market Square in downtown Gladstone, offering locally grown produce, baked goods, and one-of-a-kind artisan creations alongside live music from local bands.

name: Cobblestone Farmers Market
season: Tuesdays 4:00 PM-7:00 PM, June through October.
description: The Cobblestone Farm Market is held at historic Cobblestone Farm on Packard Road in Ann Arbor, featuring fresh local produce, free kids' activities, live music, and live farm animals.

name: Corner Farmers Market
description: The Corner Farmers Market in Coldwater supports high-quality locally grown produce and sustainable practices, working to increase access to healthy, wholesome food and promote community interaction.

name: Davison Farmers Market
season: Year-round; Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Saturdays 9:00 AM-5:00 PM.
description: The Davison Farmers Market is a year-round market at the corner of Irish Road and Court Street featuring over 70 vendors from across Michigan offering local produce, baked goods, and specialty items.

name: Dixboro Farmers Market
season: Fridays 3:00 PM-6:00 PM, May through October.
description: The Dixboro Farmers Market is held on the Dixboro Village Green next to the historic schoolhouse, supporting local growers and producers with fresh, locally grown produce and handmade goods.

name: Dowagiac Farm and Artisan Market
season: Wednesdays and Saturdays 9:00 AM-2:00 PM, through mid-to-late October.
description: The Dowagiac Farm and Artisan Market operates seasonally on the Museum lawn at Division and Railroad, offering produce, flowers, artisan works, and other goods from local vendors.

name: Downtown DeWitt Farmers Market
season: Tuesdays 4:00 PM-7:00 PM, June through October.
description: The Downtown DeWitt Farmers Market is held at 414 E. Main Street in DeWitt, offering the freshest produce, locally farmed meats, fresh eggs, baked goods, and locally produced foods.

name: Downtown Marquette Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 9:00 AM-1:00 PM May through November; Wednesdays 5:00 PM-8:00 PM June through August. Held at the Marquette Commons.
description: The Downtown Marquette Farmers Market supports regional producers and artisans, offering wholesome, quality food and goods at the Marquette Commons with both weekend morning and summer evening sessions.

name: Downtown Petoskey Farmers Market
season: Fridays 8:30 AM-1:00 PM, late May through early October.
description: The Downtown Petoskey Farmers Market promotes economic development of downtown Petoskey and the local agricultural community, connecting local producers with customers seeking fresh produce, meats, and flowers.

name: Downtown Rochester Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 8:00 AM-1:00 PM, May through October (closed July 4th).
description: The Downtown Rochester Farmers Market, located at the corner of E. Third and Water Streets, gives families access to the freshest locally grown Michigan-made products including produce, herbs, flowers, and specialty foods.

name: Farwell s Arts & Crafts Farm Market
season: Saturdays 9:00 AM-2:00 PM, rain or shine, May through October.
description: The Farwell Farmers' Market is a Saturday seasonal market where local vendors sell their goods, with free vendor spaces offered to nonprofit organizations.

name: Fenton Farmers Market
season: Thursdays 5:00 PM-8:00 PM, mid-May through October.
description: The Fenton Farmers Market, operated by Southern Lakes Parks & Recreation at 150 S. Leroy Street, offers fresh seasonal produce from local farmers along with baked goods and handmade gifts from local artisans and crafters.

name: Flint Farmers Market
season: Year-round; Tuesdays, Thursdays, and Saturdays 9:00 AM-5:00 PM.
description: The Flint Farmers' Market is a year-round public market featuring more than 50 vendors offering farm-fresh produce, artisanal goods, prepared foods, and unique gifts in a community gathering space.

name: Fremont Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 8:00 AM-1:00 PM, early July through early October, and Tuesdays 3:30 PM-6:30 PM.
description: The Fremont Farmers' Market is held at the Market Place Pavilion in downtown Fremont behind The Artsplace along Dayton Street, offering a covered, accessible space with close parking for shopping local goods.

name: Fulton Street Farmers Market
season: Wednesdays, Fridays, and Saturdays 8:00 AM-2:00 PM, May through October; Saturdays 10:00 AM-1:00 PM, November through April.
description: Established in 1922, the Fulton Street Farmers Market in Grand Rapids has connected farmers, food creators, and artists with the community for over 100 years, offering fruits, vegetables, dairy, meat, prepared foods, and handmade crafts.

name: Georgetown Township Farmers Market
season: Mondays 4:00 PM-8:00 PM, June through August.
description: The Georgetown Township Farmers Market is held outside the Georgetown Township Public Library at 1525 Baldwin Street in Jenison, featuring fresh produce, baked goods, kettle corn, and artisan products with food trucks each week.

name: Gladwin Farmers Market
season: Saturdays from May 9th through October 10th, 9 am to 1 pm

name: Grand Haven Farmers Market
season: Saturdays in May, then Wednesdays and Saturdays June through October, 8am-1pm
description: The Grand Haven Farmers Market is held on the river side under the canopy at Chinook Pier, offering garden-fresh seasonal produce, flowers, plants, baked goods and more.

name: Grayling Back To Our Roots Farmers Market
Holland Farmers Market
description: Located in beautiful and historic downtown Holland, this market features only Michigan-grown products including produce, flowers, meat, eggs, baked goods and specialty foods from over 75 local vendors.

name: Huss Project Farmers Market
description: A community-centered market at the Huss Project in Three Rivers featuring local produce, maple syrup, baked goods and bike tune-ups in a historic setting.

name: Kalamazoo Farmers Market
season: Saturdays 7 a.m. to 1 p.m. through November; Tuesdays and Thursdays 8 a.m. to 1 p.m. June through October
description: The Kalamazoo Farmers Market connects the community with more than 100 vendors including local growers, producers, artisans and retailers every week. The market operates seasonally in Kalamazoo, Michigan, with multiple market days through the warm months. It is one of the region's longstanding public markets.

name: Kalamazoo Indoor Flea and Farmers Market
Market on the Grand
description: Held at Jaycee Park in Grand Ledge, Market on the Grand is a place for farmers and artisans to sell fresh produce, baked goods, honey, maple syrup, smoked fish, flowers and handcrafted items directly to the community, alongside food trucks and entertainment.

name: Marshall Area Farmers Market
season: Saturdays year-round; Summer market 8am-12 noon May through October, Winter market 9am-12 noon November through April
description: The Marshall Area Farmers Market runs all year in downtown historic Marshall, offering fresh fruits and vegetables, meat, poultry, eggs, cheeses, baked goods, jams and more.

name: Mount Clemens Downtown Farmers Market
description: The Mount Clemens Downtown Farmers Market is an open air experience where shoppers can purchase the freshest, locally grown produce directly from area farmers. Growing for the community since 1979, the market is located near the Anton Art Center in downtown Mount Clemens, Michigan.

name: Muskegon Farmers Market
season: Summer market Tuesdays, Thursdays and Saturdays 8:00am-2:00pm May through November; Winter market Saturdays 9:00am-2:00pm December through April
description: One of the largest farmers markets in Michigan, the Muskegon Farmers Market in downtown Muskegon offers a wide selection of fresh produce, baked goods, flowers and handmade items from local farmers and artisans.

name: Native Farmers Market
description: The Native Farmers Market provides healthy, home-grown and unique natural produce along with Native American art from Saginaw Chippewa Tribal members, community and employees to the general public.

name: New Buffalo Farmers Market
season: Thursday evenings 4pm-8pm, Memorial Day through Labor Day
description: The New Buffalo Farmers Market features over 40 vendors offering local produce, honey, jams and food trucks in downtown New Buffalo with sunset views and live music.

name: Niles Artisan Alley Market
description: The Niles market offers a lively event space on Main Street full of specialty goods including fresh baked breads, fresh produce, pre-loved books, handcrafted clothing and fresh flowers.

name: Oakland Avenue Farmers Market
description: Run by the Oakland Avenue Urban Farm in Detroit's historic North End, this seasonal Saturday market sells the farm's produce along with value-added products like herbal teas, eggs, spices and jams.

name: Old Town Emmet Farm Market
description: A community farmers market in Petoskey featuring homemade, handmade and homegrown goods with live music and vendors supported under Michigan's Cottage Food Law.

name: Paw Paw Farmers Market
description: Located at the corner of Gremps St. and E. Michigan Ave. in Paw Paw, the market showcases homemade baked goods, crafts and fresh produce from local producers in Michigan and neighboring Indiana and Ohio counties.

name: Pentwater Farmers Market
season: Mondays and Thursdays, 10am-1pm, first Monday in June through Labor Day plus the first four Thursdays in September
description: The Pentwater Farmers Market is held on the Village Green in downtown Pentwater, bringing local vendors to the heart of the village twice weekly through the summer.

name: Plymouth Farmers Market
description: Held under the covered parking at The Gathering next to Kellogg Park in downtown Plymouth, the market features fresh produce, flowers, herbs, baked goods, fish, meats, cheese, coffee and a variety of crafts.

name: Port Austin Farmers Market
description: The Port Austin Farmers Market features over 100 vendors offering fresh produce, flowers, baked goods, nursery items, fresh meats, fish, dairy products and specialty foods in downtown Port Austin.

name: Port Sanilac Farmers Market
description: A local-producers market on the grounds of the Sanilac County Historic Village & Museum, featuring items grown or made exclusively in Sanilac, Huron and Tuscola counties including produce, eggs, honey, maple syrup, baked goods, soaps, plants and crafts.

name: Portage Farmers Market
description: The Portage Zhang Farmers Market offers locally grown produce, artisan baked goods, gourmet cheeses and mushrooms, locally roasted coffee, pasture-raised meats, flower bouquets, handmade soaps and food trucks from more than 35 vendors at 125 East Centre Avenue.

name: Richland Farmers Market
description: The Richland Farmers Market offers delicious and fresh local produce, food, artisans, and artists, with food trucks at every event. It serves as a community gathering place while supporting local farmers, bakers, and crafters.

name: Rockford Farmer s Market
description: The Rockford Farmers Market is held in the city parking lot behind the Post Office, with the entrance off Main Street across from Rocky's.

name: Rose City Farmers Market
season: Every Saturday, 8am-noon, April through November, with a 9am-1pm winter market on select dates December through March.
description: The Rose City Farmers Market is a community market in Rose City offering fresh local products from area vendors during a Saturday season plus extended winter market dates.

name: Royal Oak Farmers Market
description: The Royal Oak Farmers Market is a community market open Saturday mornings featuring farm-fresh produce and local vendors.

name: South Haven Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 8am-2pm, mid-May through mid-October; Wednesdays, 8am-2pm, June through August.
description: The South Haven Farm Market provides a direct link between the agricultural community and people who value fresh, local produce and farm products.

name: South Lansing Farmers Market
season: Thursdays, 3-7pm, May through September; 3-6:30pm in October.
description: The South Lansing Farmers Market operates weekly at 5016 S. MLK Jr Blvd in Lansing throughout the spring, summer, and early fall.

name: Southeast Area Farmers Market
description: The Southeast Area Farmers' Market at Joe Taylor Park offers local produce, cottage kitchen foods, personal care items, crafts and ready-to-eat foods from vendors who are primarily women of color, home growers, and neighborhood residents.

name: Spring Lake Farm & Garden Market
description: The Spring Lake Farm & Garden Market is an open-air market in Tanglefoot Park featuring seasonal produce, fresh-cut flowers, plants, breads, jams, jellies, kettle corn and garden items.

name: Summer Thyme Farmers Market and Flea Market
Tecumseh Farmers Market at The Market on Evans
description: Tecumseh's Farmers Market at The Market on Evans is held at 213 North Evans Street in Downtown Tecumseh, offering local produce, farm-fresh eggs, herbs, flowers, handmade craft items, and baked goods.

name: Texas Township Farmers Market
season: Saturdays, 8am-noon, May through October; Tuesdays, 4-7pm, June through August.
description: The Texas Township 'Fresh on Q!' Farmers Market is held at the Joyce I. Neubauer Farmers' Market Pavilion in Kalamazoo, offering locally grown produce and meats, Great Lakes fish, local honey, bakery items and cheeses.

name: The Village at Grand Traverse Commons Farmers Market
season: Mondays outdoors, 1-5pm, June through October; Saturdays indoors, 10am-2pm, November through April.
description: The Village at Grand Traverse Commons hosts a year-round farmers market with over 30 vendors offering farm-fresh eggs, meats, cheeses, fruits, vegetables, homemade breads and more.

name: Three Oaks Farmers Market
description: The Three Oaks Farmers Market gathers fresh produce, food products, and unique artisan creations from across the region at Carver Memorial Park in Three Oaks.

name: Troy Farmers Market
description: The Troy Farmers Market is held at Jeanne M. Stine Community Park and offers local Michigan-grown fruits, vegetables, flowers, meats, honey, baked goods, and ready-to-eat foods, plus weekly musical entertainment and a food truck.

name: Vassar Farmers Market
description: The Vassar Farmers Market is held in the Townsend North Pavilion behind Betty Lou's Restaurant in Vassar, offering fruits, vegetables, eggs, crafts, and baked goods.

name: Vicksburg Farmers Market
season: Summer season late May through early October, Fridays 2:00 PM - 6:00 PM
description: The Vicksburg Farmers Market is a community gathering featuring fresh, locally-grown food alongside live music and handmade goods. Its mission focuses on supporting local farmers, providing access to fresh produce, and uplifting area small businesses.

name: West Branch Farmers Market
description: The West Branch Farmers' Market offers a variety of locally grown produce, and from time to time baked goods, jams, honey, and craft items made by the farmers' families.

name: West Willow Farmers Market
description: West Willow Farmers Market is a non-profit rooted in the West Willow community of Ypsilanti, Michigan, operated by former US Marines. The market offers a mobile EBT reader and accepts SNAP Bridge Cards and Senior Project FRESH, making fresh food more accessible to older adults in the neighborhood.

name: Yankee Farmers Market
season: Monday through Thursday 10 AM to 5 PM, Saturday 9 AM to 4 PM, closed Friday and Sunday
description: Yankee Farmer's Market in Warner, NH is a family-run farm specializing in pasture-raised buffalo (bison) and grass-fed beef, also partnering with local farms to offer New England-raised, all-natural, hormone-free and antibiotic-free meats.

description: Bring A Leash, Dogs Are Welcome! Live music, fresh produce, prepared food, a beer garden featuring local breweries, and plenty of space for your favorite four-legged friend to run! Visitors will be able to buy locally-grown produce and other homemade foods and arts and crafts on a weekly basis all while enjoying a family-friendly, summertime experience. Join us Tuesdays from 4:00 pm - 7:00 pm from June through October!

name: Pullman Farmers Market
description: The Pullman Farmers Market is proud to offer our community farm-fresh local produce, specialty culinary herbs, eggs, meat, cut flowers, and beautiful plant varietals.   Join us Wednesdays from 3:30 pm - 6:00 pm from May through October!".

name: Grangeville Farmers Market
description: Local Produce, Crafts, Food, Art, and Music Goods such as produce, dairy, meat, honey, and jams are sold; and local handmade crafts and baked goods are available. Community support and collaboration have made fertile soil in which the Grangeville Farmers' Market has matured and continued to thrive. Join us Saturdays from 9:00 am - 1:00 pm from the end of June through the end of September!

name: Orofino Farmers Market
description: Local Produce, Crafts, Food, Art and Music in City Park!  Orofino Farmers Market was founded with the intention of providing an outlet for local food growers, producers, and craftspeople. Orofino Farmers Market provides consumers with direct-from-farm fresh produce and products in a family-oriented, enjoyable community event.  New vendors of home-grown and produced goods are always welcome; we hope you join us. We welcome all people who grow or make their own products within 75 air miles of Orofino. Vendor spaces are only $5 per week. Join us Tuesdays from 11:00 am - 3:00 pm fro

name: Winter Pullman Farmers Market
description: Local farmers and artisans within 60 miles of Pullman. Produce, meat, poultry, jams, jellies, soap, honey, cider and more! Shop local this season at the Pullman Winter Market presented by the Pullman Farmers Market. Find our vendors every third Saturday, November thru April at our new host location: the Brelsford WSU Visitor Center. Farmers, crafters, and artisans will showcase their seasonal produce and goods from 10:00 am to 2:00 pm for your shopping needs.

name: Weippe Farmers Market
description: This is the 4th year in a row we have had the Market! We are open every Friday from 3-5 p.m. starting July 19th till September 20th. We normally have 10 to 12 vendors. We have vendors who sell fresh and locally-grown vegetables and a few vendors who make bread and jellies and various other handmade items!  It is a great time for everyone! The community seems to love coming out to visit with each other after the long winter. There is definitely a strong feeling of community at our Market.

name: Kootenai County Wednesday Farmers Market - Coeur D'Alene
description: From May through October, Kootenai County Farmers' Market has a mid-week location in downtown Coeur d'Alene.  The market specialize in vegetable, flower, and native plants and an ever-changing array of local food products--meats, cheeses, bread, honey, and jams--all season long. So grab a cup of coffee, listen to the live music and peruse the producers and artisans who make up our two markets--from cheese to chairs, breads to birdhouses--you'll find it at the market! Join us Saturdays from 4:00 pm - 7:00 pm from mid-May through the end of September!

name: Silver Valley Community Market
description: The Silver Valley Market (SV Market) is an artisan market that runs through the summer months on Thursday evenings from 5-8:00 PM. There is live music, 20+ vendors, hundreds of market-goers, and various other fun events like our Annual Cornhole Tournament. Come out and enjoy our beautiful summer evenings with us! Some products you'll see offered include produce, local food, baked items, sweets, local artisan products, beer, kombucha.

name: Main Street Market in Rathdrum
description: Main Street Market is a seasonal, monthly market, operating in the months of July, August, & September. The market will run on the 4th Thursday of each month. The intent of this market is to offer commerce from a range of categories, including Artisans & Crafters, Agricultural Producers & Growers, Product Vendors, Alcohol Vendors, Food Court Vendors, Flea Marketers. name: Millwood Farmers' Market
description: The Millwood Farmers Market meets Wednesdays 3:00-7:00 p.m., from May 29, 2019 to October 9, 2019, in front of the Millwood Presbytery.  The first Millwood Farmer's Market opened in May of 2006. Since then it has undergone many changes. It originated in the Millwood Presbyterian Church parking lot, moved to the street in front of the church, and finally around the corner to the beautiful tree-lined street of Dalton Ave. The market provides the Millwood community with a source of fresh local farm products and handcrafted artisan goods. It also provides the Millwood neighborhood educational

name: Perry Street Thursday Market
description: Thursday Market people unabashedly promote the cultural riches of local food, support farmers and food artisans, and help build thriving communities.  The Thursday Market seeks to make available fresh, healthy, locally grown food and locally crafted products to the South Perry neighborhood and the broader Spokane community. The market intends to serve as a resource to the community offering affordable and high-quality products while giving small farmers and local artisans a venue to sustain their enterprise and support their quality of life. name: Liberty Lake Farmers' Market
description: The Liberty Lake Farmers' Market was founded in 2002 by brother and sister Jim Frank and Susan Parker. With the help of a handful of volunteers and some dedicated farmers, eight vendors began selling their goods in the parking lot of the liberty square building. Today, the Liberty Lake Farmers' Market averages 40 to 50 vendors each Saturday. It is full of wonderful produce, baked goods, eggs, meats, fish and specialty items from all around the northwest. The vision for Liberty Lake Farmers' Market is to connect the area community with local farmers and each other. Join us Saturdays from 9:00 a

name: Emerson-Garfield Farmers’ Market
description: Located in the parking lot of the IEL Adult Education Center (2310 North Monroe), the Emerson-Garfield Farmers’ Market is a seasonal weekly marketplace featuring around two dozen regional and neighborhood vendors. The aim of the market is to bring fresh, healthy, affordable goods that are locally grown or produced to the residents of Emerson-Garfield as well as all those who pass through our up-and-coming neighborhood. An intentional side benefit is that it also serves as a community hub, bringing both people and organizations together and cultivating meaningful relationships between them. Joi

name: The Fairwood Farmers’ Market
description: The Fairwood Farmers’ Market plan is progressing forward with more events after having such a successful first three years. We are located in the old Fairwood Business District parking lot in front of Thomas Hammer Coffee Co.  The Fairwood Famers Market is being hosted by Allen Family Properties, owners of the 319 W. Hastings building. Bathrooms in our building will be available for public use during these events. Join us Tuesdays from 3:00 pm - 7:00 pm from May through mid-October!""&nbsp

name: Spokane Farmers' Market
description: The Spokane Farmers' Market Association is a group of farmers from the Spokane area who joined together in 1998 to operate the Spokane Farmers' Market as an independent and self-managed farmers' market in downtown Spokane. Our members bring fresh produce, baked goods, meat, eggs, and other products (including organic vegetables) to the market for sale. Join us Saturdays and Wednesdays from 8:00 am - 1:00 pm from May through October!

name: 1912 Center's Winter Market
description: The Winter Market at the 1912 Center features farmer's selling fruit, eggs, squash, honey, jams, lettuces, cheese, breads, and more!  The Winter Market takes place the first two Saturdays in December, the first two Saturdays in Decembers, and the first Saturdays in February and March (6 total dates).  The market runs from 9am-1pm on those Saturdays and Backyard harvest offers EBT card use at each market date.
